claim: The kernel coverage gradient, now anchored at BOTH ends by MEASURED points (self-authored internal define-op-census 0.7710 at the self-authored end -> external define-lane kappa_B^engine 0.0000 at the external-benchmark end, with friendly-NL m0b 0.3542 between), remains a CROSS-INSTRUMENT QUALITATIVE framing device: its points measure NON-COMMENSURABLE quantities (internal define-op resolvability of self-authored records; content-word vocabulary membership; external benchmark define-checkability under a closed mapper grammar) on different corpora with different denominators. It licenses ONLY the placement of the 0.0000 at the external-benchmark end and the monotone-decreasing/negative inference; it NEVER licenses interpolation between points or a single decay-law claim.
rationale: The gradient is an interpretive placement aid, not a measured single curve; adding a directly-MEASURED external endpoint (0.0000) does not upgrade it to a commensurable curve. Used only to place 0.0000 at the external-benchmark end and to license the negative inference (which here is directly confirmed: the external point is measured at ~0, not merely bounded). No conclusion rests on the gradient being a real curve; the scope bounds rest on the independently-MEASURED three-legs discipline and the positive-control validity. Revisit only if a single commensurable instrument ever measures multiple gradient points.

claim: Expanding the mapper's closed define-templates so they fit benchmark MCQ/cloze stems (the template-shape gap, which dominated the 0.0000 at 1,497/1,550 no-template) AND authoring definitional endorsements for the minted-but-un-endorsed CL/UBERON/PO/PATO/CHEBI shards (the endorsement-coverage gap) would raise the external define-lane kappa_B^engine above 0.0000 on the biomedical Tier-A slice. The direction is named (two distinct gaps, two named routes); the magnitude of any lift is UNMEASURED and may remain small or ~0 (def-MMLU largely non-biomedical; WiC general-vocabulary, per design memo section 4).
backing_ref: poc/b-cov-define-lane/census-summary.json sha 03f09f42; docs/design-kot-query-define-op.md section 4 (yield projection, ASM-0019) / section 5 (mapper templates)
resolution_path: Re-run poc/b-cov-define-lane/census.py after (a) the mapper template expansion (defineTemplates.ts / parseDefineQuestion) and (b) authoring definitional endorsements for CL/UBERON/PO/PATO/CHEBI; count gold-parse and mapper-parse separately, and MEASURE the delta from 0.0000. Never a premise for any downstream claim; does not gate the feasibility verdict.

claim: The b-cov define-lane census is an EXPLORATORY, biomedical-skewed slice (OpenBookQA-test + 6 def-MMLU biomedical subjects, 1,550 items; WiC unmeasured, HTTP-401 data gap). Its MEASURED kappa_B^engine = 0.0000 characterises the external-benchmark end of the coverage gradient and is a MEASURED bound on efficiency-thesis BREADTH for the define lane on this slice, but it INFORMS without GATING the feasibility verdict: feasibility-synthesis.md section 4 rests the efficiency bottom line on de-confounding f2b (human f2b-transfer / K-NULL) and the correctness bottom line on the NL boundary (l3a-parse/a5-nl) + the g2/g3/g8/g9 semantics core, none of which this census touches.
rationale: A single exploratory, biomedical-skewed slice cannot move a cross-experiment feasibility verdict whose bottom lines rest on independent, untouched measurements; the honesty discipline is to record the 0.0000 as a breadth-substrate bound, not a verdict input. Revisit if a general/lexical (non-biomedical) external define census (e.g. WiC after the data unblock) reads out.

claim: B-double-prime (B2P) gate read-out: source-keyed counterfactual-pair discrimination with a paired teacher-forced margin. For each swept item i with candidate pair {top_i, bridge_i}, two donor carriers byte-identical except for the answer-slot name yield Delta_v = v_top - v_bridge at the harvest layer; the injection for key=top is the +alpha-scaled unit Delta_v, for key=bridge the sign-flipped vector; the read-out is m(v) = logp(' '+top_i | prompt, inject v) - logp(' '+bridge_i | prompt, inject v), teacher-forced over each candidate's full token sequence, and the per-(item, cell) success bit is m(+) > m(-) with ties and non-finite margins counted as failure. The shuffled-source control uses the unit Delta_v of the deranged item sigma2(i) with a seed-pinned fair coin deciding the key-to-sign assignment, so E[control success] = 0.5 * P(no tie) <= 0.5 exactly: a content-free perturbation of ANY magnitude scores chance in expectation, closing the B-prime confound (where output-change frequency alone scored 0.525 on an any-cell exact-gold read-out). A second, reported-only role-consistent control (same deranged vector, fixed key=top -> + assignment, derived from the same forwards) separates item-keyed content from generic role-direction transfer.
backing_ref: ASM-0111 (successor-probe constraint set, clauses a+b); registry/assessments/nsk1-bprime.json fork_recommendation; poc/nsk1/out/bprime/bprime_summary.json sha256 539040e93c78ec244ad20f77ee32ab7cf19358add5033c525851cea799f1ef7c
rationale: ASM-0111(a)+(b) require a read-out a content-free perturbation cannot pass in expectation; the paired +/- margin design cancels the item's baseline preference between the two candidates, the coin-XOR construction makes the control's null exactly 0.5 by arithmetic rather than by estimate, and ties-count-as-failure biases every arm conservatively. Revisit condition: superseded for any form whose effective answer space is demonstrably larger than two candidates (a fresh per-cell null analysis is then required in that spec).

claim: B2P delivery operator: norm-matched additive contrastive injection at the final prompt-token position, single position, forward/prefill only - h_p <- h_p + s * alpha * ||h_p|| * (Delta_v / ||Delta_v||), s in {+1,-1} the key sign, alpha selected once by a pre-declared mechanical calibration (the largest alpha in the ladder {0.125, 0.25, 0.5, 1.0} whose name-emission-collapse rate on the 32-item calibration slice at the pivot cell is <= 0.10; if none qualifies, alpha = 0.125 with all collapse rates reported). NOT wholesale replacement: B-prime measured final-position replacement transplant destructive (98.5 percent mean correct-item cell breakage) and content-indifferent. The final position is retained because the additive, norm-bounded contrastive DIFFERENCE removes the foreign-context common component that drove the measured destruction, the gate read-out needs no generation coherence, and ASM-0112's resolution path runs through exactly this operator contrast.
backing_ref: ASM-0111 clause (d); heatmap_breakage_c100 + rescue_p2/rescue_p2_ctrl in poc/nsk1/out/bprime/bprime_summary.json sha256 539040e93c78ec244ad20f77ee32ab7cf19358add5033c525851cea799f1ef7c; contrastive activation addition arXiv:2312.06681 (2023)
rationale: Additive low-alpha satisfies ASM-0111(d) directly; the contrastive difference of two byte-identical-but-for-the-key donors cancels shared content (relation surface, base name, syntax, position), which is simultaneously the cleanest key encoding and the mildest perturbation; norm-matching makes alpha a resident-state-relative strength so one ladder covers all cells and items; the calibration rule is mechanical so the runner decides nothing. Revisit condition: if B2P returns INSTRUMENT-INVALID or KILL, non-final-position and multi-position additive operators are the registered next operator family, not a re-run of this one.

claim: B2P grid, floor, and multiplicity: a bounded 9-cell grid (harvest layer lh in {lay(1/2), lay(2/3), lay(5/6)}, injection layer lt in {lay(1/3), lay(1/2), lay(2/3)}; at L=24: lh in {12,16,20} x lt in {8,12,16}; pivot cell (16,12) for calibration and generation secondaries), a single floor 0.70 on keyed accuracy (chance exactly 0.5), and Bonferroni-corrected any-cell gating: PASS-KEYED iff at some completed cell the one-sided Wilson LB at confidence 1-0.05/9 (z=2.5427) is >= 0.70 AND the real arm paired-beats BOTH controls at that cell (exact one-sided sign tests on discordant items, p<0.05); KILL-NO-USABLE-CHANNEL iff at EVERY cell (all 9 completed) the one-sided Wilson UB95 (z=1.6449) is < 0.70. This complies with ASM-0111(c): family-wise PASS error is bounded by 9 x 0.05/9 = 0.05 by arithmetic because the per-cell control success probability is <= 0.5 by construction - unlike B-prime's uncorrected any-of-35 compounding of a ~0.5-probability discrete event.
backing_ref: ASM-0111 clause (c); ASM-0041 (predecessor floor-plus-control-conjunct structure); registry/assessments/nsk1-bprime.json
rationale: 0.70 = chance + 0.20 is stipulated as the minimum keyed accuracy at which residual-stream delivery could plausibly carry engine grounding usefully after Stage-1 calibration; at n=200 the rule is decidable both ways (observed keyacc >= ~0.785 clears the corrected PASS floor; a dead channel at true 0.5 clears the every-cell KILL bar with per-cell miss probability ~3e-5) with an honest INCONCLUSIVE band between. The KILL side uses uncorrected per-cell UB and an all-cells conjunction, conservative against false kills. Revisit condition: the floor value is re-derivable only in a successor spec, never post hoc on B2P data.

claim: B2P contact discipline: B2P re-touches all 958 covered CLUTRR items (text-only pass, donor-carrier forwards, keyed injections) as the second patched-probe contact after B-prime. ASM-0043's clauses carry over verbatim: (i) every B2P row is phase:exploratory - quarantined, uncitable, flips no verdict; (ii) B2P derives no campaign selection parameter (the alpha calibration and pivot-cell secondaries decide only the registered maintainer fork; campaign-grade L*/alpha remain custom-calibration-only under their own record); (iii) covered-set membership stays fixed by the committed S4 predicate, so per-item outcome knowledge cannot re-select items without a visible predicate change; (iv) any later frozen record on this corpus MUST disclose the B-prime AND B2P contacts verbatim and its skeptic memo must attack them explicitly.
backing_ref: ASM-0043 (the B-prime contact discipline this extends); data/nsk1-clutrr/manifest.json; docs/next/nsk1-bprime2-spec.md
rationale: The built corpus has zero unused remainder, so any R3 probe on this task re-touches the same covered set; extending the disclosed quarantine discipline is cheaper and more honest than building a fresh corpus for a pre-freeze diagnostic, and the revisit clause prices the exploitation risk. Revisit condition: identical to ASM-0043 - if a post-B2P skeptic memo identifies a concrete exploitation channel from this contact, the campaign rebuilds fresh items instead of reusing the touched set.

claim: Sizing prior only: a working residual-stream content channel at mid-stack should key at roughly >= 0.8 on a two-candidate discrimination (basis: token-identity read-outs from transplanted hidden states decode at high accuracy at mid layers on larger hosts in the patchscopes family; no measurement exists at 1.7B, on this form, or under additive contrastive injection). Used ONLY to justify the 0.70 floor's headroom and the n=200 power framing; never a premise for any gate outcome or spend beyond B2P's caps.
backing_ref: arXiv:2401.06102 (2024) patchscopes; arXiv:2406.12775 (2024) back-patching (instrument-class precedent, via reports/lit-structured-parsing-and-inner-symbolic.md section 2.2)
resolution_path: B2P measures keyed accuracy directly at every grid cell (poc/nsk1/out/bprime2/); whatever it returns supersedes this band, which is sizing-only and load-bearing for nothing.

claim: G3 materials concept selection: the 20 concepts are drawn from kernel-v0 EXCLUDING the 22 manifest-flagged knownWeak records, with frame-stratified quotas InstanceSchema 6 / WhenTrue 6 / RelationalSchema 8 (proportional to the corpus's 17/16/21), selected deterministically by ascending sha256('kot-g3-mat/1|seed=0|'+slug) with 'promise' (the HS2 litmus) force-included in InstanceSchema if unranked (not triggered; it ranks 4th of 9). Scope consequence: the G3 verdict licenses claims about the non-flagged kernel-v0 explications only; knownWeak records, whose per-record notes already concede inadequacy, are out of the tested population, so the verdict cannot be read as covering them in either direction.
backing_ref: data/instance-descriptions/manifest.json concept_selection; data/kernel-v0/manifest.json knownWeak; registry/experiments/g3.json design.seeds=[0] + n_planned.concepts=20; docs/research-plan/01-hypotheses-experiments.md HS3
rationale: The frozen g3 record fixes ~20 concepts x ~10 instances but not which concepts; a selection rule had to be pinned. Excluding knownWeak separates the semantics-pin question (does the necessary-conditions reading survive?) from the authoring-quality question (are these particular explications any good?) - including records whose own notes flag inadequacy would push violations that measure authoring defects into a verdict about the reading. The sha256 ranking under the frozen seed removes designer cherry-picking within the eligible set; frame stratification keeps all three explication frames in the tested population.
resolution_path: If G3 PASSes and a later bulk-authoring wave wants the verdict extended to flagged-quality records, a successor experiment over a knownWeak-included sample re-tests; the scope limit dissolves only by that measurement.

claim: G3 materials composition: each concept's 10 instances are authored 4 control / 3 necessity-stress / 3 sufficiency-stress (totals 80/60/60 of 200), where stress candidates are deliberately constructed counterexample CANDIDATES (necessity: arguably-genuine instances built so one specific explication condition plausibly fails; sufficiency: near-miss non-instances built to satisfy as many conditions as possible) and controls are typical expected-satisfiers. Interpretation consequence, binding on the readout: the measured violation rates are rates over this deliberately stress-stratified candidate set - a hard test of the universally-quantified pin C [= Pi(C) - NOT an estimate of violation frequency in natural text, and no readout may quote them as such. Decidability consequence: under the pinned analysis (analysis/g3.py, z=1.645 Wilson, n=200) PASS needs <=13 and FAIL needs >=27 confirmed violations, so each 60-instance stress lane makes both branches of the frozen two-decision rule reachable; a materially smaller lane (e.g. 20) could not trip FAIL on its own and would bias the instrument toward PASS.
backing_ref: data/instance-descriptions/manifest.json composition_rationale + counts; data/instance-descriptions/design-intent.jsonl; analysis/g3.py wilson_bounds (13/200 -> ub 0.0999, 14/200 -> ub 0.1058; 27/200 -> lb 0.1001, 26/200 -> lb 0.0958, direct computation from the pinned formula); registry/experiments/g3.json endpoints + kill_criterion_verbatim; docs/research-plan/08-stats-and-extrapolation.md 1.6
rationale: The frozen record fixes n=200 and the Wilson gates but not the material mix; the mix determines what the rate means and whether both verdict branches are reachable, so it had to be pinned and registered rather than left implicit. Authoring stress candidates does not preordain the outcome: a candidate only becomes a violation if blinded annotators independently judge concept-applies-and-conditions-fail (or the converse) - if the explications are sound, stress candidates get rejected on one side and score no violation. Controls anchor the judgment scale, provide the negative-signal mass that keeps annotators from inferring an all-violations set, and supply agreement data across easy and hard items.
resolution_path: The rate-interpretation clause is discharged by the g3 readout quoting it; if a natural-frequency estimate is ever wanted, that is a different experiment over a naturally-sampled instance corpus (D-IR-N-style mining), pre-registered separately.

claim: G3 annotator-facing operationalization: (a) Pi(C) is presented to annotators as the kernel-v0 record's gloss field rendered as an enumerated clause-wise condition list (conditions.jsonl, opaque ids K-01..K-20); the pinned semantic object remains the kot-ast/1 explication, of which the gloss is the corpus's documented human-readable mirror. (b) Judgments are collected in two passes per annotator: pass A collects the ordinary-usage concept judgment Q1 (target claim + text only, no conditions shown) and is completed in full before pass B collects the condition-satisfaction judgment Q2 (condition set + bindings + text, concept word withheld) - so Q1 cannot be colonized by the explication and Q2 is a literal condition check rather than a gestalt re-judgment. (c) Designer intent (design-intent.jsonl) is never annotator-visible, and instance texts are scrubbed of the concept word and close derivatives while target/bindings carry it by design. Violation derivation is NOT stipulated here - necessity := Q1-yes & Q2-no, sufficiency := Q1-no & Q2-yes is the frozen design's own definition (P1 HS3 / F7 verbatim).
backing_ref: data/instance-descriptions/annotation-protocol.md; data/instance-descriptions/conditions.jsonl _meta; data/kernel-v0/README.md (gloss as the explication's human mirror); registry/experiments/g3.json arms_mandatory_baselines ('blind to hypotheses'); docs/research-plan/07-redteam.md DAG-audit finding (ii)
rationale: Human annotators cannot read kot-ast/1, so SOME rendering of Pi(C) had to be fixed; the gloss is the only rendering the corpus itself certifies as the record's mirror, and enumerating it clause-wise is presentation, not translation. The two-pass order and concept-word withholding are the cheapest instrument design that keeps Q1 an ordinary-usage judgment and Q2 a checklist judgment - collapsing them into one pass would let the explication define the concept for the annotator, which is exactly the circularity HS3 exists to test for. Registered because gloss-fidelity is inherited, not re-verified here: if a gloss misrenders its AST, G3 measures the gloss, and the ASM makes that exposure explicit.
resolution_path: G4's decode-legibility probe and any gloss-vs-AST fidelity audit convert the inherited gloss-fidelity exposure into a measurement; if a gloss defect is found in a G3 concept, the affected concept's judgments are quarantined by a pre-readout amendment.

claim: Agent-authored NL phrasings produced under the section-5 blind protocol of docs/design-nl-boundary-l3a-parse-a5-nl.md (fresh authoring identities with no repo/lexicon/front-end/answer access, pinned prompts, committed transcripts, mechanical lints, recoverability audit) are an adequate held-out proxy for natural-language phrasings of the frozen l3a/a5 eval queries for the purpose of the NL-boundary records l3a-parse and a5-nl. Natural-user-distribution robustness claims are excluded from both extrapolation envelopes regardless of outcome.
rationale: No human phrasing panel is available at Tier 0; the blind protocol removes the tuning channels that could inflate or deflate the primary (authors never see what maps), and the residual shared-training-distribution risk is named in the design doc and carved out of the envelope rather than argued away.
resolution_path: A future human-authored phrasing set (or any externally sourced phrasing corpus for these queries) re-tests the same pinned pipeline; agreement retires this stipulation, disagreement bounds it.

claim: The pre-declared fraction in HL3a clause 2 (and its HA5 analog) is instantiated at 10% absolute maximum NL-boundary loss: primary floor 0.90 = measured gold ceiling 1.0 minus 0.10, for both l3a-parse and a5-nl. This is the programme's reachability POLICY level, not a measured quantity; an INCONCLUSIVE landing re-opens fork FK-NLB-2 with data.
rationale: Genuinely uncertain where reachable flips; 0.90 keeps the exactness premium the engine leg banked (>=9/10 covered NL queries answered exactly, every miss separately gated to be a refusal) while remaining decidable at both frozen n (P8 section-1.6 lint, design doc section 7).
resolution_path: The two records' verdicts measure against this floor; FK-NLB-2 re-opens it only via a new record id if the landing is INCONCLUSIVE.

claim: Giving authoring identities the entity labels (URN slugs rendered as names) is not lexicon leakage: the mapper lexicon contains concept labels only (minted sourceIds from kernel-v0/molecules-v0/code-v0), no entity surface forms, so entity names cannot tune a phrasing toward or away from mappable concept surface forms; entity labels are also the entities' ONLY names, so no faithful question can be asked without them.
backing_ref: mapper/src/lexicon.ts (lexicon built from concept labels); tools/experiments/nlb/nlb_map.mjs mintedConcepts (labels = sourceIds of the three minted corpora); data/*/minted-urns.jsonl
rationale: The CHOICE to hand authors the labels is stipulated (backed by the inspectable lexicon content, which contains no entity names); without entity names the phrasing task is impossible, and the gazetteer side is exercised precisely by how authors render those names in free text.
resolution_path: Standing; lapses only if the mapper lexicon ever ingests entity surface forms, which would mint new record ids.

claim: For the S1 control-refusal endpoint of l3a-parse/a5-nl, a front-end ERR_PARSE refusal is an ACCEPTABLE control outcome alongside the pre-authored expected engine code (FK-NLB-6): under an NL front-end a control may legitimately fail earlier in the pipeline (unknown entity -> gazetteer miss) than the engine stage the structured query would have reached. Conservative-direction note: this eases S1, but the dangerous direction (control ANSWERED, mis-parse answering wrongly) is separately gated by S2 and the control-answered count, and strict engine-code match is reported descriptively.
backing_ref: docs/design-nl-boundary-l3a-parse-a5-nl.md section 2 (FK-NLB-6), section 6.2; registry/experiments/l3a-parse.json + a5-nl.json dependent_vars n_control_refused_acceptable
rationale: Fail-closed is the property under test; WHERE the pipeline refuses is instrument detail, WHETHER it answers a control is the safety property. Scoring strict engine-code equality would indict the pipeline for refusing too early, which is not a safety failure.
resolution_path: Never re-opened for this record pair; a successor caring about refusal-stage fidelity gates on the descriptively-reported strict rate.

claim: FK-NLB-8 (freeze-candidate fork, replaces the draft section-5 paraphrase quota): the draft's 'at most 50% of covered phrasings may contain the relation label verbatim' quota is REPLACED by (a) mechanically-linted syntactic-diversity quotas (>= min(6, n) distinct masked templates per covered family; no template > 50% of a family; canonical 'wh the <label> of <entity>' scaffold <= 50% per family), with free natural use of the relation's name, plus (b) a 60-item/vertical no-label synonym PROBE that is descriptive ONLY, never gated, and carved out of the envelope. Reason: the mapper lexicon carries exactly one label per concept and a rule lemmatizer with no synonym table, so no-label phrasings are unmappable BY CONSTRUCTION (measured probes: 'who gave birth to X' maps birth+give not mother; 'makers of'/'parts of' map nothing); a forced 50% no-label stratum would predetermine a FAIL arithmetically (0.5x1.0+0.5x~0 < 0.90) and make the primary undecidable at its planning value, violating the P8 section-1.6 lint the design binds itself to. The label-verbatim vs paraphrase stratification remains a reported endpoint, so residual label-use inflation is visible, not deniable.
backing_ref: docs/design-nl-boundary-l3a-parse-a5-nl.md sections 2 (FK-NLB-8), 5, 10.1; mapper/src/lexicon.ts + mapper/src/lemmatize.ts (single-label lexicon, rule lemmatizer); tools/experiments/nlb/nlb_lint.py EVAL-DIVERSITY/EVAL-SCAFFOLD/PROBE-NO-LABEL
rationale: A gate that only a known-impossible synonym capability could pass measures nothing about the deterministic boundary under test; moving the synonym question to a never-gated probe measures the same direction honestly without manufacturing the verdict either way.
resolution_path: The probe's measured rates bound the synonym penalty; a parser-investment successor (FK-NLB-3 routing) with a synonym/alias layer would re-instate a gated paraphrase leg under a new record id.

claim: FK-NLB-9 (freeze-candidate fork, DEV-set re-basing): the DEV set used to finalise the frame layer is 60 designer-authored phrasings per vertical over FRESH minted identities (data/nlb-phrasings-*/dev-entities.jsonl) that are disjoint from every scored eval item and from both world stores (mechanically linted, DEV-FRESH), and DEV items are never scored in any arm; the DEV gazetteer augmentation (world entities + dev entities) exists only in the dev pass, never in a scored arm. Under this disjointness, designer authorship of DEV is admissible despite the designer having read the lexicon: the front-end is pinned BEFORE any EVAL phrasing exists, EVAL phrasings are authored blind by fresh identities, and a designer-DEV that fits the frame to unrepresentative phrasings can only LOWER the primary (the conservative direction) - there is no channel from DEV authorship to the blind EVAL distribution.
backing_ref: docs/design-nl-boundary-l3a-parse-a5-nl.md sections 2 (FK-NLB-9), 5.3, 10.8; data/nlb-phrasings-l3a/manifest.json + data/nlb-phrasings-a5/manifest.json (authorship disclosure + ordering attestation); tools/experiments/nlb/nlb_lint.py DEV-FRESH
rationale: The draft protocol required disjoint AGENT identities for DEV; the coordinator-directed build strengthens the disjointness to the ITEM level (fresh entities, never scored) which is what actually blocks leakage into the primary, and it makes the DEV set buildable this pass without spawning authoring agents. G4 stays meaningful: it bounds front-end brokenness on phrasings the frame was ALLOWED to fit.
resolution_path: Standing for this record pair; the independent skeptic re-attack (build item 6) attacks it against the BUILT corpus before freeze.

claim: The frame layer's closed knowledge is part of the deterministic front-end under test and is legitimate NON-answer knowledge: the per-relation surface-orientation table (ROLE_DIR: which engine direction realises 'the R of E' per relation with world edges), the label-variant matcher over the mapped concept's OWN label (inflections + the two-irregular table made->make, children->child), the closed a5 op-keyword cascade, and the fail-closed op default (inverse-possessive shapes parse as lookup unless an exactly-one marker is present, because unique would GUESS cardinality and could fabricate a scalar answer). The frame layer performs NO concept aliasing: the mapper remains the sole concept-binding component, so a lexicon miss stays a mapper-abstain (clean stage indictment) and the G5 derangement of mapper bindings remains the scramble control for the whole semantic path.
backing_ref: tools/experiments/nlb/nlb_frontend.py (ROLE_DIR, _IRREG, _l3a_shape, _a5_op; sha 590377760ed5067688ddc0dd859c1a1cfa955e640b3a07c0f75e91d3bc908ea0 pinned in both records); docs/design-nl-boundary-l3a-parse-a5-nl.md section 3
rationale: Some closed grammar-like knowledge is what a deterministic front-end IS; the registered line is that none of it may bind text to concepts (the mapper's job, scrambled by G5) or encode any eval answer. Orientation entries exist only for the five relations with world edges; every other relation defaults to a direction the engine refuses fail-closed.
resolution_path: Pinned; any extension (e.g. a synonym/alias layer after a FAIL) is the pre-declared parser-investment successor under a new record id.

claim: Engine continuity for l3a-parse/a5-nl: tools/axiom/kot_axiom.py at the freeze-candidate pin (sha256 d26408815238cd9f73b50bb2c4b1f659c8a783ff6a5f16e27357eb2071bb3d08) is NOT byte-identical to the pin the frozen parents carry (d2064989c208c37df5a3d15a4fae6118c82516055fd6552f45a2a92fe2b7a659) because the define-op line extended the engine after the parents froze (additive define-op surface). The NL-boundary records rely on instrument gate G2 - the gold-replication arm must reproduce the parent-perfect outcome (600/600+300/300; 855/855+122/122) IN-RUN on the byte-identical parent evals/stores - as the behavioural-equivalence guard on the surface this experiment uses, instead of asserting byte-identity.
backing_ref: git commits fa210a6 + 9910052 (define-op engine extension); registry/experiments/l3a.json + a5.json harness pins (parent sha); poc/nlb-mock/l3a/mock-receipt.json + poc/nlb-mock/a5/mock-receipt.json gold_replication_parent_perfect=true (2026-07-10); registry/experiments/l3a-parse.json + a5-nl.json harness_manifest + verdict_rules (gold_replication_identical is a PASS conjunct)
rationale: Restoring the old engine bytes would fork the shared module against the define-op line; the honest alternative is to pin what actually runs and make ceiling-reproduction an executable in-run gate (G2 is already a PASS conjunct and its failure is INSTRUMENT-INVALID), which is strictly stronger than an unchecked byte-identity claim about behaviour.
resolution_path: G2 in the final run re-verifies per run; if the engine is edited again before freeze, the records' pins must be re-stamped and the mock re-greened, else freeze is blocked.

backing_ref: poc/truthstyle-2x2/judges-invocation.md sections 4.3, 7, 9, 11 (staging capability checks MEASURED 2026-07-10 on claude-code 2.1.201); registry/experiments/truthstyle-2x2.json design.n_planned.adjudication_protocol + pins.model_revisions.judge-p3-haiku45; registry/amendments/truthstyle-2x2/3-pin-model-revisions-judge-p3-haiku45.json
claim: truthstyle-2x2 judge-p3 operationalization: the frozen judge-p3-haiku45 slot (Claude Haiku 4.5, temperature 0, single-word yes/no/cannot-say, no tools, blind stateless per-item invocations) is LAWFULLY filled by a headless one-item-per-process Claude Code sub-process — claude -p with the exact dated model id claude-haiku-4-5-20251001, --tools "" (init-event tools:[] — tool use structurally impossible), --setting-sources "", --no-session-persistence, MAX_THINKING_TOKENS=0, the pinned format-only system prompt, an empty out-of-repo working directory, and subscription auth (apiKeySource none; no Anthropic API key), per the maintainer ruling of 2026-07-10. The ALTERNATIVE in-session Agent-tool sub-agent form is adjudicated NON-conforming for this record and MUST NOT be used: it injects the project CLAUDE.md and auto-memory context (which name the project and NSM) into the judge's context — violating the frozen 'judges see only the question bytes (never ... kernel)' clause — and can pin the model only by alias.
rationale: The headless form preserves every frozen judge property to the maximum the interface exposes and is byte-auditable per item (captured events.jsonl with mechanical zero-tool, identity, and blinding tripwires), while the in-session form structurally leaks project-identifying context and cannot be driven mechanically by the runner. Interface facts are MEASURED and recorded in the spec; this entry stipulates the CHOICE. Revisit condition: if a future claude-code version changes any verified flag semantics, the preflight fails closed and the operationalization must be re-verified or the record re-frozen; if the maintainer rules the Agent-tool form was intended exclusively, this is a maintainer decision point, not a silent substitution.

backing_ref: poc/truthstyle-2x2/judges-invocation.md section 9; data/d-adj-t-llmproxy/judge-1p-invocation.md section 4 (the discharge pattern the frozen record already cites for judge-p1); registry/experiments/truthstyle-2x2.json pins.model_revisions
claim: truthstyle-2x2 pool-wide 'temperature 0' discharge: neither codex-cli 0.144.1 nor claude-code 2.1.201 exposes a temperature/sampling parameter (MEASURED at staging), so the frozen commitment is discharged as maximum-determinism-the-interface-exposes, identically for all three judges: pinned model ids + pinned tool versions and flags + pinned prompt bytes; stateless single-shot per-item calls; minimal reasoning (codex model_reasoning_effort=low for judge-p1/p2; MAX_THINKING_TOKENS=0 for judge-p3, thinking-block-free MEASURED); output constrained to the exact token set (server-side enum for codex; pinned format-only system prompt + pinned normalization for judge-p3); FIRST VALID ANSWER IS FINAL — never re-sampled, re-rolled, or checked again, so residual server-side nondeterminism can never become a selection effect and is bounded operationally by the frozen retest gate (agreement >= 0.85 over >= 48 duplicate judgments, disclosed as a floor).
rationale: The frozen record already incorporates this exact discharge for judge-p1 by pinning its invocation form to the judge-1p spec, whose section 4 operationalizes 'temperature 0' as interface-maximal determinism; applying the same reading uniformly is the frozen design's own precedent, not a new laxity. The literal alternative (a raw Messages-API call with temperature=0) is unavailable by the maintainer ruling that no Anthropic API key is used on this box. Revisit condition: if any interface later exposes a true temperature parameter, successors pin it to 0 explicitly; this record's readout is interpreted with the retest-gate stability floor as the disclosed bound.

backing_ref:
poc/truthstyle-2x2/judges-invocation.md sections 3 and 6; registry/experiments/truthstyle-2x2.json design.n_planned.adjudication_protocol ('per-judge pinned item order dts/1|judge-

|20260710') and design.dependent_vars accept definition ('labels normalised by the runner to {yes,no,cannot-say}, anything else = unlabelled'); analysis/truthstyle_2x2.py load_labels
claim:
truthstyle-2x2 mechanical staging choices, pinned before any adjudication call: (a) the frozen item-order seed template 'dts/1|judge-

|20260710' is read with

in {p1,p2,p3}, giving the literal seeds dts/1|judge-p1|20260710, dts/1|judge-p2|20260710, dts/1|judge-p3|20260710; order = the 808 item ids ascending by sha256_hex(utf8(seed + '|' + item_id)); each judge pass runs items strictly sequentially, and the three passes may run concurrently with each other (<= the frozen concurrency cap); (b) one uniform answer normalization applied to every judge's raw answer, in order: strip leading/trailing ASCII whitespace; strip at most one trailing period; ASCII-lowercase; map the exact string 'cannot say' to 'cannot-say'; the result is VALID iff byte-exactly in {yes,no,cannot-say}, anything else is an invalid attempt (retried up to 3 times, then the (item,judge) pair is unlabelled — never repaired, never mapped to the escape token).
rationale:
Both choices complete frozen clauses that license them without fixing bytes: the seed template names

and the judges are keyed p1/p2/p3 in every frozen judge id; stateless per-item calls make order science-neutral, so any fixed reading is lawful and this one is recorded. The normalization is conservative and mechanical: it can only map trivial formatting variants onto the three declared tokens and routes everything else to the unlabelled instrument-accounting path consumed by the frozen label-coverage and pool gates — it cannot manufacture an acceptance. Load-bearing because label validity feeds the frozen gates. Revisit condition: none within this record (one-shot pin discipline); a different reading requires a pre-run correction record.

claim: Interpretive scope rule for the B2P PASS-KEYED outcome (successor to ASM-0110 per its revisit condition): the label licenses exactly this - content-specific, item-keyed internal-write delivery is MEASURED-exploratory at R3 on the CLUTRR entity form at 2 of 9 grid cells ((lh,lt) in {(12,16),(16,16)}), under a teacher-forced paired logprob-margin read-out at alpha=1.0, n=200 swept text-only failures, beating both the arithmetic-chance coin control and the role-consistent deranged control with FWER <= 0.05. Every B2P row is phase:exploratory - quarantined, uncitable in a verdict, flips nothing.
rationale: Fixes now what the strongest-looking exploratory positive in the line may be cited FOR before it ages into an overclaim: a 2-cell, single-host, single-seed, margin-read-out Stage-0 diagnostic. Symmetric to ASM-0110's function for the B-prime negative. Revisit condition: superseded for successor results by the Stage-1 free-generation probe's own scope rule when that probe reads out.

claim: Ratified runner operational instantiation (B2P disc-c100 deranged arm): the spec pins the sigma-2 Sattolo derangement (seed 20260712) over Swept order only and leaves the reported-only C100 deranged arm's donor map unpinned; the runner applied the SAME pinned Sattolo operator (same seed) over C100 ascending order, fixed-point-free asserted. CONFIRMED benign and faithful: (a) every gate-bearing section 5 quantity is computed over Swept only and the verdict object never reads the disc-c100 store; (b) C100 and Swept are disjoint by construction (asserted), so the two derangements share no items and no leakage path exists; (c) the operator is deterministic, seed-pinned, content-independent, and auditable - it cannot manufacture keying, only permute which wrong item's vector serves as each reported-only control.
rationale: The runner disclosed the choice for designer confirmation per the run-ne-interpret split; ratifying it as a registered stipulation prevents a later reader from treating the disc-c100 recount (keying replication on correct items) as resting on an unpinned choice. Revisit condition: none within B2P (one-shot instantiation); successor specs should pin their reported-arm derangements explicitly to retire this class of disclosure.

backing_ref: registry/assessments/nsk1-bprime2.json fork_recommendation + analyst_recount (pivot_secondaries_uninformative, breakage_sign_asymmetry); docs/next/nsk1-bprime2-spec.md section 8 PASS path; poc/nsk1/out/bprime2/bprime2_summary.json sha256 0ee800f7...; run committed at b404754
claim: Post-PASS-KEYED sequencing constraint (binding on successor specs in the nsk1 line): no claim upgrade, no nsk1 freeze, and no scale-successor spend is licensed until a Stage-1 FREE-GENERATION keyed probe at the two measured keying cells ((12,16) and (16,16) at R3) reads out - keyed emission + rescue-of-text-only-failures under real vs deranged-coin arms, an alpha ladder below 1.0 (the keying-vs-damage frontier), and >= 2 additional derangement seeds. Rationale for the gate: the B2P generation secondaries were spec-fixed at the pivot (16,12), which the margin sweep measured DEAD, so the margin->generation inference is the single unmeasured link between PASS-KEYED and anything the flagship can use; power/rung logic for the redesigned nsk1 DRAFT record must be derived from measured Stage-1 rescue rates, not extrapolated from teacher-forced margins.
rationale: Prevents the two known failure modes of a strong-looking exploratory positive: freezing a campaign on margin extrapolation (the extrapolation-as-premise error) and spending scale money on an echo-grade channel before integration is measured. The maintainer may override at the fork; this constraint binds designer specs, not the maintainer. Revisit condition: discharged when the Stage-1 probe reads out (either sign), or explicitly re-registered if the maintainer redirects the line.

claim: The g9.author mechanical result (50/50 explications pass the encoder profile-1 gates: validateExplication + encodeExplication unit-norm/NaN sanity, encoder 40e8c8ba) is the LEGALITY LEG ONLY of the g9 composite validator bar and is an UPPER BOUND on the composite pass count - it is NEVER an authoring-capability claim. Substitutability + cross-translatability are scored at g9.review (GATE-H, blinded human review, Baartmans et al. arXiv:2505.11764 definitions reused as-is); blinded_review_done=0 at this node, so /gates/instrument_valid is FALSE by construction and no PASS/FAIL/INCONCLUSIVE verdict exists for HS-A yet. Provenance: the batch specs were authored in an interrupted same-day session and pass the mechanical gates on the first recorded iteration of this resume session; any in-loop repair history from the interrupted session predates validation/loop-log.jsonl and is not captured (the pinned artifact is the final authored state).
resolution_path: g9.review (blinded human review, ~4h, annotator sourcing O-3) produces composite pass counts; g9.log appends them; the pinned analysis/g9.py computes the verdict-bearing Wilson bound. This entry closes when the g9 verdict object exists; until then no document may cite g9 as evidence of authoring capability.
rationale: The composite endpoint is defined over three legs and only the legality leg is machine-checkable in-repo; quoting the mechanical 50/50 as capability evidence would be exactly the overclaim the HS-A kill criterion guards against, so the bound-vs-claim distinction is stipulated up front, before any review data exists.

claim: The review surface for each authored item is its NL gloss (concepts/<slug>.json gloss = review/sheets.json candidate_explication), stipulated as the faithful Minimal-English rendering of the kot-ast/1 explication AST. Gloss<->AST fidelity is authoring-asserted, NOT mechanically verified (no NL-realisation instrument exists in-repo). Conservative scoring direction stipulated: if the blinded review finds the gloss defective, the item scores as a composite FAIL for the authored arm - a gloss/AST divergence is an authoring defect of the arm under test, never grounds to re-score against the AST.
resolution_path: Scored implicitly by g9.review (reviewers see only the gloss). A future NL-boundary instrument (l3a-parse/a5-nl lineage) could mechanise the fidelity check; until then this stipulation stands and is cited wherever the mechanical 50/50 is quoted.
rationale: Reviewers judge NL text while the encoder judges ASTs; the arm must be scored on what reviewers actually see, and the conservative direction prevents the authored arm from ever benefiting from its own rendering defects.

claim: g9.review blinding is PROCEDURAL, not cryptographic: review/sheets.json withholds the dataset reference explications (verified: zero reference-explication text in the sheets file; the one substring hit is dataset id 49748 whose reference is the empty string) and withholds all 17 KNOWN-WEAK authoring notes; sheet order is a seed-0 mulberry32 Fisher-Yates shuffle; the unblinding key (review/sheet-key.json) and the public DeepNSM dataset both remain reachable by a determined reviewer. The review-stage owner must administer sheets without repo/dataset access at scoring time. Choice of shuffle PRNG and the key-adjacent storage are design conveniences, not measured properties.
resolution_path: The g9.review administration record states how sheet isolation was enforced (print/separate-context). If it cannot, the review is scored but flagged blinding-degraded in the g9 verdict interpretation.
rationale: Procedural blinding is the strongest available without a separate annotation platform; declaring the residual unblinding surface before the review starts keeps the GATE-H stage honest and auditable.

claim: 17/50 authored concepts (brother, child, day, depression, elbow, fire, freedom, ground, island, law, leg, male, salty, sell, sharp, size, thirst) carry KNOWN-WEAK notes: profile-1's 65-prime lexicon (no sun/light/solid/body-part/kinship/etc. primes or molecules) forced reduced renderings. This is stipulated as PART OF THE ARM UNDER TEST - g9's independent variable is 'fable-class authoring' inside THIS validator/profile, so lexicon-gap concessions count against the arm's composite score, not as instrument exclusions. Direction named: the gaps are expected to depress substitutability on the affected items; magnitude is measured only at g9.review. No item was dropped or swapped for being hard (the frozen seed-0 selection is intact).
resolution_path: g9.review measures the actual substitutability hit; if the composite verdict is FAIL/INCONCLUSIVE, the per-item KNOWN-WEAK split in the readout distinguishes 'authoring weakness' from 'profile-1 lexicon ceiling' for the why-now rewrite (interpretation split only - the verdict itself is never adjusted).
rationale: Dropping or swapping lexicon-hard items would silently shift the scored population away from the published DeepNSM test-split population the comparison point was computed on; keeping the frozen selection intact preserves comparability and pushes profile-1 lexicon costs into the measured result where they belong.

claim: truthstyle-2x2 section-7.3 blinding-audit matching rule, clarified pre-data after the ERR_DTS_BLINDING boundary stop: a case-insensitive token match in user-prompt.txt / events.jsonl / stderr.log counts as a HIT unless (i) the token consists entirely of hexadecimal characters [0-9a-f] AND (ii) the match lies strictly inside a longer maximal [0-9a-f] run (the byte immediately before or after the match is itself hex). Within the pinned token set {kernel, nsm, truthstyle, f2b} this affects ONLY f2b (the sole all-hex token); matching for the other three tokens is byte-identical to the original literal scan. Abort-on-hit semantics, the token list, the scanned surfaces, and all other tripwires are unchanged. This is a false-positive CLARIFICATION of the audit's matching predicate, not a guard weakening: every real leak spelling (f2b, f2b-transfer, f2b-replicate, f2b-errors, repo path fragments carrying kernel/truthstyle) keeps non-hex delimiters and still fires; the excluded shape is arithmetic coincidence inside machine-generated hex identifiers (session/thread/request UUIDs).
backing_ref: poc/truthstyle-2x2/blinding-audit-clarification.md sections 1-4 and 6 (measured abort facts, rate analysis, repo census, normative code); registry/corrections/truthstyle-2x2/1-blinding-audit-clarification.json; poc/truthstyle-2x2/opus-runs/20260710T115128Z (retained void-run artifacts: judge-p3 pos-016 session-UUID hit, byte-clean user-prompt.txt everywhere)
rationale: The strict literal scan has a structural false-positive channel: f2b is valid hex, vendor harnesses emit UUIDs on every call, and P(f2b inside one UUID) is about 0.5 percent, so under a PERFECTLY blind harness the audit aborts every 808-item pass with probability near 1 (observed: judge-p3 aborted at item 16 on its session UUID) - a control that fires with certainty under the null detects nothing, and clarifying it RESTORES discriminative power. The hex-run-boundary rule is the narrowest edit that closes the channel: it modifies matching only where the channel exists (all-hex token embedded in a hex run) and leaves the three project-name-bearing tokens byte-identical. Rejected alternatives: identifier-field-value exclusion (needs vendor field-name lists that drift, cannot handle non-JSON stderr, and blinds the audit to leaks inside excluded values) and naive word-boundary matching (weakens the non-hex tokens where no false-positive channel exists). Residual risk disclosed: f2b immediately adjacent to a hex letter; repo census shows every such committed adjacency lies inside a 40/64-char digest except one f2b-errors mock scratch filename with no path into any judge surface.
resolution_path: Mechanically re-derivable: the post-pass sweep (poc/truthstyle-2x2/blinding-sweep.py, normative code in the clarification doc section 5) must be GREEN over the completed run and lists every hex-embedded exclusion with its surrounding hex run; all attempt bytes are retained, so the GATE-A auditor can re-run both the strict and clarified scans independently. Revisit condition: any clarified-scan hit, or any exclusion whose hex run is not a machine identifier, is a boundary stop back to Fable.

claim: truthstyle-2x2 void-and-restart discipline for the section-7.3 false-positive abort: the aborted run poc/truthstyle-2x2/opus-runs/20260710T115128Z is VOID for labels and retained as provenance; the adjudication campaign restarts from item 1 for all three judges (fresh section-8 preflight + section-3-ordered main pass, new run dir, clarified runner committed before launch with sha256(run-dts-judges.py) recorded in the new run dir). No resume mechanism is used: the pinned spec defines none, and an aborted pass writes no responses by construction. No selection effect is possible: data/d-ts-labels was never created, no label from the void run was assembled or read by any deciding agent, and the abort surfaced only a token name and file basename; unblinding state is pre. The roughly 69/55/16 items already judged once are re-judged with the first samples discarded UNREAD - a disclosed re-sampling whose magnitude is bounded by the ASM-0241 interface-maximal-determinism discharge and the frozen retest gate (agreement >= 0.85 over >= 48 duplicate judgments).
rationale: Restart is the only option that stays inside pinned mechanics: resuming would require trusting and re-ingesting partial on-disk state through a mechanism the frozen spec never defined (runner improvisation, forbidden by the stage discipline), while restart re-runs the exact pinned protocol end to end. The re-sampling objection has no force because first-valid-answer-is-final protects against outcome-conditioned re-rolls, and no outcome was ever visible to any agent; the discarded calls are inside the frozen budget caps (usd_cap 40, wall clock 48h).
resolution_path: One-shot: applies only to the 20260710T115128Z void run. Any future mid-pass abort is its own boundary stop; this entry is not a standing resume-or-restart policy.

claim: truthstyle-2x2 mechanical-verdict path: the verdict is computed by the scoped corpus-direct driver tools/registry/verdict-gen-dts.py (committed by the coordinator to the contract in registry/corrections/truthstyle-2x2/2-verdict-path-reconciliation.json, sha256-pinned inside the verdict object it emits), NOT by invoking tools/registry/verdict-gen.py directly. The driver imports the existing spine (verdict-gen internals + kot_common) for frozen-record integrity, amendment overlay, log chain + eligibility, completeness gate, unblind-line discipline, verdict_rules evaluation via kot_common.eval_expr, PASS audit gating, and RT-14 linting, and differs in exactly one step: the sha-verified pinned analysis analysis/truthstyle_2x2.py is invoked per its OWN frozen interface (--items data/d-ts/items.jsonl --labels data/d-ts-labels/labels.jsonl, empty stdin, cwd repo root) after re-verifying BOTH corpus digests against the (amended) effective record at readout time - strictly stricter than the generic spine, which never re-verifies corpora at readout. At least one eligible phase:final run row is still required before any readout; the shared verdict-gen.py is not modified.
backing_ref: registry/corrections/truthstyle-2x2/2-verdict-path-reconciliation.json (full contract, rejected alternatives, post-run order of operations); analysis/truthstyle_2x2.py docstring + main() (frozen corpus-direct interface, sha bf171ed951c2100e0a768c9ecea571da604e912a08164e2dd563e103f1c071c8); registry/experiments/truthstyle-2x2.json pins.corpus_hashes.d-ts-labels placeholder
rationale: Every lawful alternative fails closed: the analysis pin is design scope (no amendment may touch it), the kot-reg/1 pins schema is additionalProperties:false (no new pin key survives the post-overlay validation), the staged analysis bytes are pinned in the already-filled harness manifest (editing them would permanently brick the record), and per-record special-casing of the shared verdict spine would enlarge the re-audit surface of every already-audited record. A scoped driver that reuses the spine by import adds no second implementation of any guardrail, is self-declaring in the verdict object (inputs.analysis_invocation), and must pass a mock-green gate on a scratch repo copy before the real readout. The frozen design itself declares the analysis corpus-direct: the labels are a pinned INPUT corpus (d-ts-labels placeholder filled by ops amendment after adjudication, before the final-phase record).
resolution_path: The GATE-A auditor recomputes the verdict from committed bytes and may re-run the driver read-only; inputs.analysis_invocation makes the divergence from the generic spine explicit. Revisit condition: if the shared spine later gains a generic corpus-direct interface, successors use it and this driver is retired; this entry never licenses a second record to bypass verdict-gen.

claim: nsk1 Stage-1 design frame: a free-generation keyed-rescue probe at the TWO measured B2P keying cells only ((lh,lt) in {(12,16),(16,16)} at R3, ASSERT L==24, no new grid search), alpha ladder {0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 1.0} run in full at both cells for margins, rescue generations, and breakage (1.0 = the only measured keying strength, kept as anchor and internal replication; 0.125 dropped); THREE Sattolo derangements over Swept ascending order (seeds 20260712 = the B2P sigma-2 seed for continuity, 20260713, 20260714; each ASSERT fixed-point-free) with per-(seed, item, cell) coin bits by the B2P sha256 recipe with the derangement seed in the seed slot, alpha-independent; arms per item x cell x alpha = real +/- (2 gens) and deranged +/- per seed (6 gens), with the coin control series (content-free null: the coin bit picks which deranged generation counts) and the role-consistent control series (fixed sign mapping matching the endpoint under test) both DERIVED from the same six deranged generations; host, corpus, prompts, donors, scorer, injection operator, hook mechanics, Swept/C100 construction (seed 20260712, cap 300, C100 = 100 correct, disjoint) all carried over from B2P byte-verbatim via _build_specs_bprime2 and the ASM-0200/0201 instrument family.
backing_ref: ASM-0342 (the Stage-1 sequencing gate this spec discharges); registry/assessments/nsk1-bprime2.json fork_recommendation rank 1; poc/nsk1/out/bprime2/bprime2_summary.json sha256 0ee800f7f7d1aec0aa245986aa9a27a15017ebc33d5780a9cb4104da366f76f1; docs/next/nsk1-stage1-spec.md
rationale: Two cells keep the multiplicity minimal where B2P measured keying twice over (Swept + disc-c100) and seven cells cleanly dead; the ladder answers the keying-vs-damage frontier named by the B2P breakage secondary (13-16 percent plus-sign damage at alpha=1.0) while keeping the measured anchor; three derangement seeds retire the single-seed attack on the B2P control at near-zero cost because the coin and role series share the deranged generations; carrying the B2P instrument byte-verbatim makes Stage-1 an extension of a measured-valid instrument rather than a new one. Revisit condition: superseded for any successor that changes host, form, operator, position, or read-out family - such a probe needs its own frame ASM.

claim: nsk1 Stage-1 decision rule: per endpoint e in {minus, plus} and unit (cell, alpha), FIRE iff (i) paired exact one-sided sign test real_e > coin_s at p < 0.05/8 for ALL THREE seeds (Bonferroni over the endpoint's 8-unit family; FWER <= 0.05 by the corrected conjunct alone), (ii) real_e > role_e_s at p < 0.05 for all seeds (mechanism separator, raw as in B2P), (iii) effect floors mean_s Delta_s >= 0.10 AND min_s Delta_s >= 0.05 with Delta_s = (b_s - c_s)/n; ordered labels INSTRUMENT-INVALID -> KEYING-NOT-REPLICATED (alpha=1.0 margin keyacc Wilson-UB95 z=1.6449 < 0.70 at BOTH cells, B2P floor continuity) -> PASS-INTEGRATION-RESCUE (minus endpoint fires) -> PASS-EMISSION-RESCUE-ONLY (plus only) -> ROLE-RESCUE-ONLY ((i)+(iii) without (ii)) -> KILL-NO-KEYED-RESCUE (all 8 units complete AND both endpoints' pooled UB95 = Delta-bar + 1.6449 * max_s SE_s < 0.10 everywhere, SE_s = sqrt(b_s + c_s - (b_s-c_s)^2/n)/n) -> INCONCLUSIVE; validity tripwires on the two arithmetic-nulled coin series (margin coin and strict-both emission coin), Wilson-LB at z=3.2790 (two-sided 0.05 over 48 coin series) > 0.5 at any completed unit/seed => INSTRUMENT-INVALID; ties, non-finite values, and empty generations count as failure in every arm.
backing_ref: ASM-0200 (paired-margin + coin-XOR family); ASM-0202 (predecessor floor/correction structure); docs/next/nsk1-stage1-spec.md section 5; poc/nsk1/out/bprime2/bprime2_summary.json sha256 0ee800f7f7d1aec0aa245986aa9a27a15017ebc33d5780a9cb4104da366f76f1
rationale: The rescue null is empirical (content-free rescue measured 0.11-0.165 at the dead pivot), not arithmetic, so FWER control moves into the paired coin conjunct (0.05/8, all seeds) instead of a chance-anchored Wilson floor; the 0.10/0.05 floor pair is the smallest content-specific rescue increment that can power a campaign rung at feasible n per ASM-0342's requirement that DRAFT-record power derive from measured rescue rates; the KILL margin equals the mean floor and uses the max single-seed SE (conservative: variance of a mean of correlated estimators is bounded by the max variance), accepting that a true-nothing world may land INCONCLUSIVE with tight reported bounds - a conservative kill on an exploratory probe is the honest direction. Revisit condition: floors and margins are re-derivable only in a successor spec, never post hoc on Stage-1 data.

claim: nsk1 Stage-1 contact discipline and custody: Stage-1 re-touches all 958 covered CLUTRR items (text-only pass, donor forwards, keyed injections and generations) as the THIRD disclosed patched-probe contact after B-prime and B2P; ASM-0203's clauses carry over verbatim, with clause (iv) now requiring any later frozen record on this corpus to disclose ALL THREE contacts and its skeptic memo to attack them explicitly; every Stage-1 row is phase:exploratory (quarantined, uncitable, flips no verdict); Stage-1 derives no campaign selection parameter; outputs live under poc/nsk1/out/stage1/ with gate label NSK1-S1, margin/baseline rows storing both raw candidate logprobs so the entire gate recomputes offline; the entrypoint is appended to poc/modal/modal_nsk1_g2.py with existing content byte-untouched (or a sibling module reusing its helpers), a runner operational choice disclosed in the summary; the runner reports mechanical facts and artifact sha256s only, interpretation being designer work.
backing_ref: ASM-0203 (the B2P contact discipline this extends); ASM-0043; docs/next/nsk1-stage1-spec.md sections 6-7; data/nsk1-clutrr/manifest.json
rationale: The corpus has zero unused remainder, so the integration probe necessarily re-touches the same covered set; extending the disclosed quarantine discipline for a third time is cheaper and more honest than building a fresh corpus for a pre-freeze diagnostic, and the growing disclosure duty prices the exploitation risk exactly as ASM-0043/0203 did. Revisit condition: identical to ASM-0203 - a skeptic memo identifying a concrete exploitation channel from the three contacts forces fresh items for the campaign.

claim: Sizing prior only, two legs: (a) the coin-arm content-free rescue rate at the Stage-1 keying cells is planned at roughly 0.10-0.17 (basis: rescue_real_plus 22/200 and rescue_drg_plus 33/200 measured at the DEAD pivot cell at alpha=1.0 - a different cell, extrapolated to the keying cells where no rescue measurement exists); (b) free-generation keyed steering at the keying cells is strong enough for rescue rates in the tens of percent to be reachable under the plus sign (basis: the C100 breakage sign-asymmetry, 26-28 of 100 minus-sign generations emitting the bridge name at alpha=1.0, extrapolated from correct items to failures and from the minus to the plus direction). Used ONLY for the section 5 decidability arithmetic (per-seed sign-test power approx 0.98/0.84/0.45-0.57 at true Delta 0.20/0.15/0.10, n=200; KILL per-unit probability approx 0.88-0.98 in a true-zero world) and the floor headroom; never a premise for any gate outcome, claim, or spend beyond the Stage-1 caps.
resolution_path: Stage-1 measures rescue and control rates directly at both cells and all four alphas (poc/nsk1/out/stage1/); whatever it returns supersedes this band, which is sizing-only and load-bearing for nothing.

claim: nsk1 Stage-1 sign-semantics and claim boundary, fixed before the run: the donor pair encodes the hop-1 fact frame (D_bridge = the TRUE hop-1 fact, byte-equal to the g2/g2d feedback sentence; D_top = the same frame with the 2-hop gold surface in the answer slot), so injection sign -1 delivers the true-fact identity (echoing it emits BRIDGE, which scores wrong - only using the fact to compose the 2-hop relation yields gold) and sign +1 delivers the gold-name-in-frame counterfactual (echo-confounded: the pushed surface IS the gold answer). Therefore: R-minus is the INTEGRATION endpoint and is conservative (the name-push works against gold, so a minus-endpoint pass cannot be produced by echo and a minus-endpoint miss does not prove integration absent at other operators); R-plus is the DELIVERED-CONCLUSION endpoint and its pass licenses ONLY content-specific emission of an internally delivered conclusion (an engine-verified-answer use-mode candidate), NEVER an integration, grounding-composition, or reasoning claim; no Stage-1 label resolves ASM-0042 beyond its own scope; no Stage-1 outcome is a verdict or feeds a freeze without the redesigned nsk1 DRAFT record.
backing_ref: poc/modal/modal_nsk1_g2.py sha256 b524ff574e82877ae11022c9cb7024d80530fcc0796cacf1e0bb035d4f21abbc (_build_specs_bprime2 donor asserts); poc/nsk1/out/bprime2/bprime2_rows.jsonl sha256 dadabaaea914485e387b9c1f558f5f938a7b9905159b35000957aea2a340eeba (breakage sign-asymmetry); docs/next/nsk1-stage1-spec.md sections 1-2, 5
rationale: The B2P interpretation names Stage-1's question as rescue-by-use rather than rescue-by-echo; the donor construction happens to make the two readings separable by sign, and fixing that separation plus each endpoint's claim ceiling BEFORE the run is what prevents a strong plus-endpoint result from being re-narrated as integration (the exact overclaim failure mode ASM-0340 exists to block for B2P itself). Revisit condition: superseded by the Stage-1 interpretive assessment's own scope rule when the probe reads out.

claim: FK-NLB-10 (l3a-parse shape-recoverable scope cut): the primary and S2 gated denominators of l3a-parse are the 527 covered items of the 7 shape-recoverable families (children-lookup 100, count-maker 43, instance-false-disjoint 40, instance-true 50, part-lookup 50, unique-father 122, unique-mother 122); unique-maker (43) and made-lookup (30) are excluded from every gate and reported as a descriptive quarantined shape-ambiguous stratum, carved out of the envelope. The exclusion is applied in the pinned analysis script only: corpus, packets, phrasings, front-end, instrument and the G2 gold-replication arm stay byte-identical, and controls stay at 270 with S1 unchanged. Precedent FK-NLB-5; here the absence of a shape-faithful NL rendering is MEASURED (blind round-trip shape recovery failed in exactly and only these covered families) rather than a priori.
rationale: English unmarked wh-questions are number-neutral and orientation-flexible for agentive noun relations, so the grammar's unique-vs-lookup and role-vs-flip distinctions on non-functional relations are not injectively recoverable from faithful natural phrasings; scoring them against exact gold shape lets phrasing underdetermination (not parser inadequacy) move the primary and can mechanically fire the S2 kill on semantically correct singleton answers (43 unique-maker items vs the 18/600 trigger). Dropping the strata records the measured partial-negative instead of letting it masquerade as a parser verdict. Revisit condition: a successor with marked-English phrasings or answer-shape-equivalence scoring (new record id) re-measures the dropped shapes.

claim: NLB recoverability-audit gate split: the freeze-blocking gate is the SHAPE+ENTITY recovery rate over a 60-item covered-only in-scope sample, >= 0.95 (>= 57/60); relation/concept-label recovery and control-item recovery are reported descriptively and gate nothing. Applies to the l3a r1 audit and to the not-yet-run a5 audit (pre-data on that vertical).
rationale: The gate's registered purpose is answerability (a FAIL must not be manufacturable by unanswerable phrasings) and shape+entity carries exactly that; gating on label recovery under a stem-overlap rule forces label-verbatim authoring through the back door (re-imposing what FK-NLB-8 registered-removed) and double-counts the synonym penalty the experiment itself measures via the label-verbatim stratification and the never-gated probe; controls are insensitive to shape recovery in the dangerous direction because any parse of an out-of-scope or unknown item still refuses. Registered POST-DATA on the l3a vertical - disclosed as such; the independent skeptic re-attack must attack it as a forking-paths move before freeze.

claim: NLB l3a audit r1 protocol (no judge re-roll): audit-recoverability-r1.json is produced by (i) mechanically re-scoring the COMMITTED judge transcript (sha256 7f3f2291a7e46fb554e7477fc92ff3611d4a0131e117aa5c027960563f36fb1a) restricted to the in-scope covered sampled items, and (ii) topping the covered-only sample to 60 via a FRESH judge identity given ONLY new in-scope items (same pinned prompt-audit.md, same deterministic sample rule over the 7 in-scope families, already-judged qids skipped). No item is ever judged twice; the original audit artifact stays committed as the driving evidence and is never overwritten.
rationale: The spec's no-re-roll rule exists to stop judge-shopping; re-scoring the same committed transcript under the registered split gate and extending the sample with new items to a fresh judge preserves that guarantee while making the gate decidable on the re-scoped population (27 in-scope covered items are already judged, 33 are owed). Revisit condition: none for this pair; a successor audit protocol supersedes it with its own ASM.

claim: FK-NLB-11 (a5 forced-substring lint exemption): EVAL-NO-ANSWER masks all occurrences of the item's queried-entity surfaces (longest-first) out of the phrasing and flags only answer surfaces that survive masking; every exempted qid is disclosed in the receipt field waived_forced_substring. The 28 structurally self-naming a5 items (nested-function name prefixes: where-defined 9, contained-in 9; self-recursive call edges: callers-of 5, callees-of 5; verified 28/28 forced) STAY in the a5-nl scored set. MANDATORY REUSE CAVEAT: any successor with a model-based arm (a5-llm re-freeze) MUST exclude or re-phrase every waived qid, because a model can read the answer off the question surface; the caveat is written into the a5-nl record's successors field. l3a must re-lint green under the new rule with an EMPTY waived list.
backing_ref: data/nlb-phrasings-a5/lint-receipt.json sha256 da8c693da230f8dd6fdc75917ccbd40a476fcb28bddc5076ef477e64fda9acc8; data/nlb-phrasings-a5/eval.jsonl sha256 074b686cb5b0d8227c21f966f7c935e18fab002eb5d245033558e2321a5f1ba8; docs/design-nl-boundary-l3a-parse-a5-nl.md section 14.4; ASM-0142
rationale: The leak is structural, not an authoring defect: the identifier is the entity's only name (ASM-0142) and the answer is a substring of it by the nested-naming/self-edge construction, so no faithful phrasing can avoid it. No arm in this record reads answers off surfaces (deterministic pipeline; surface-blind answer-all policy), the 28 items are the only coverage of nested-definition and self-recursion semantics, and the masked rule is exactly-scoped: any answer occurrence OUTSIDE the mandatory entity mention still flags. Dropping the items would delete a real semantic slice to satisfy a guard aimed at arms this record does not have; a permanently RED receipt would kill gate G3. Revisit condition: the reuse caveat re-opens as a blocking design question for any successor that adds a model arm.

claim: NLB post-data edit boundary (pre-freeze design iteration lawfulness): the blindness invariant of the l3a-parse/a5-nl pair is carried by the FRONT-END artifacts and the committed phrasing bytes - nlb_frontend.py, nlb_map.mjs, the mapper a1-hybrid policy pin, and every phrasing in data/nlb-phrasings-* stay byte-untouched from the pre-EVAL pin (2026-07-10) through freeze. Scorer-side artifacts (nlb_lint.py, analysis/l3a_parse.py, analysis/a5_nl.py, the two DRAFT record JSONs) may change in this registered pre-freeze iteration in response to instrument-side findings, with new shas re-pinned in both records, because no scored outcome exists and the front-end cannot have adapted to the phrasings. The independent non-designer skeptic re-attack must explicitly attack ASM-0420..0425 as potential forking-paths moves, and the maintainer holds a veto at prereg-freeze.
backing_ref: docs/design-nl-boundary-l3a-parse-a5-nl.md sections 5.3, 10.2, 14.5; registry/experiments/l3a-parse.json + a5-nl.json harness_manifest (front-end pins 590377760ed5067688ddc0dd859c1a1cfa955e640b3a07c0f75e91d3bc908ea0 unchanged)
rationale: The design's ordering protocol exists to prevent the front-end fitting the held-out phrasings; lint and analysis scripts neither parse nor answer, so amending them before any arm runs cannot leak eval content into the system under test, while refusing all post-data edits would leave a mechanically broken gate (RED G3 on structural non-defects) and a gate the re-scoped population cannot satisfy. Fail-closed boundary: any edit to a front-end artifact or phrasing byte after the pre-EVAL pin still mints a new record id, no exception registered.

claim: Sizing/expectation note only: the l3a-parse primary planning value 0.95 is implausible - the audit sample measured natural-synonym phrasing on 9/27 = 0.333 of in-scope covered sampled items (count-maker 4/4, part-lookup 4/4, instance-true 1/4), and against the single-label mapper lexicon (no synonym table) those items are expected to abstain, putting the covered-exact rate plausibly in or below the 463-485/527 INCONCLUSIVE band (extrapolated from a 27-item audit sample to the 527-item scored set and from judge label-use to mapper parse behaviour - neither measured end-to-end). Used ONLY to set maintainer expectations that an INCONCLUSIVE/FAIL landing is live; never a premise for any gate, re-authoring, or re-design; the re-scope keeps every synonym-phrased item scored.
resolution_path: The l3a-parse run measures the covered-exact rate and the label-verbatim vs paraphrase stratification directly; whatever it returns supersedes this band. An INCONCLUSIVE landing re-opens FK-NLB-2 with data per ASM-0141; a FAIL routes to parser investment per the frozen kill text.

claim: Ops-amendment pin-value law + model-revisions manifest convention (truthstyle-2x2 correction seq 3; reserved-block 0440-0459 use: truthstyle-2x2 verdict-path governance rulings): a post-freeze ops-amendment pin value is a COMMITMENT, not content - the shared verdict-spine guard (tools/registry/verdict-gen.py PIN_VALUE_RE, bare 40/64-hex only) is correct law and stays unmodified; API-served judge models, which expose no upstream revision sha, are pinned post-freeze as the sha256 of a committed canonical-JSON manifest whose values carry the exact model-id + operationalization prose (poc/truthstyle-2x2/model-revisions-manifest.json), extending the f2 amendment-6 / truthstyle amendment-1 staged-manifest convention to /pins/model_revisions/*; truthstyle-2x2 amendments seq 2 and seq 3 carry the IDENTICAL manifest digest by construction (both commit to the same manifest; precedent f2 amendment seq 1).
rationale: Prose inside the FROZEN record is reviewed under frozen_sha256 + the GNG-0 signoff, but a post-freeze fill bypasses freeze review, so it must commit to committed bytes: free text in an ops pin is a channel the design-scope guard (DESIGN_SCOPE_PREFIXES) cannot inspect, while a digest of a committed file keeps the prose byte-auditable AND the mechanical guard uniform for every record. Widening or path-scoping PIN_VALUE_RE in the shared spine was rejected on correction seq 2's own ratified grounds: every already-CONFIRMED audit certifies verdicts computed under the current spine semantics, and mutating the shared spine mid-programme enlarges the re-audit surface of ALL records to serve one. Revisit condition: a future registry schema revision may type /pins/model_revisions natively, superseding this convention for NEW records only.

claim: Amendment re-issue lawfulness scope (truthstyle-2x2 correction seq 3; reserved-block 0440-0459 use: truthstyle-2x2 verdict-path governance rulings): an amendment file may be re-issued in place under a correction record ONLY while UNCONSUMED - no successful overlay witnessed by any verdict object, unblind log line, or recorded amended_record_sha256, and unblinding state pre. truthstyle-2x2 seq 2/3 met this at re-issue time: results-log/truthstyle-2x2.jsonl did not exist, the PIN_VALUE_RE guard had aborted every overlay attempt before an effective record was derived, and data/d-ts-labels was never created. Once an amendment has entered ANY successful overlay it may never be re-issued; the only lawful path is a new experiment id with supersedes. Provenance is mandatory: superseded file sha256s recorded in the correction record, original bytes preserved in git history at the pre-reissue commit, and the pin CONTENT relocated byte-verbatim into the committed manifest.
backing_ref: registry/corrections/truthstyle-2x2/3-amendment-hexpin-reissue.json (reissued_files sha256s ae3f58f2... -> 6e9a4fea..., 7c39c893... -> 64a6f9e0...); registry/amendments/truthstyle-2x2/1-pin-harness-manifest.json (one-shot pin discipline naming the pre-run correction mechanism)
rationale: The amendment layer has no supersede mechanism - verdict-gen.load_amendments applies every file in seq order and fails closed on the first invalid one - so an unconsumed-but-unlawful amendment can only be corrected in place; bounding that power to the pre-consumption window with mandatory sha256 provenance keeps it from ever rewriting anything a verdict, audit, or unblinding has relied on.

claim: N-IOC sign-off ruling (bead kernel-of-truth-5iu; reserved-block 0460-0479 use: io-compression idea sign-off): (1) idea-compositional-rollup-invented-concepts = DEFER — the interpretive PARK of registry/assessments/compression-census.json is RATIFIED as the design-side sign-off; no B-E1 consumption experiment is designed or run, no OR grammar extension, no B-F1 composite-bridge training, while parked; the re-entry gate is a B-E0 re-run (same instrument; host-BPE denominated once the R1 tokenizer is pinned) after a coverage-growth wave that materially changes the mapper lexicon, un-parking iff achievable corpus savings clears the freeze-time K-B0 floor (2% sanity default until reset at a freeze), and an un-park re-opens B-E1 DESIGN only, never execution directly. (2) idea-crosslingual-phrase-coverage-io-compression = PURSUE at Tier-0 only, census-first: A-E2 before A-F0 before any Tier-1 spend, with idea A ruled DIFFERENTIATED from the parked idea B (different lever: mints NEW vocabulary coverage — exactly the census's measured binding constraint and the park's named re-entry lever; different engagement structure: per-span/disjunctive vs B's conjunctive-fragment engagement; different value axis: cross-lingual BPE fertility, unmeasured by B-E0; the census carries over exactly one caution — mappability m-hat is the binding term on covered English, so K-A4 is live and m-hat-weighted cells are mandatory in A-E2) and NOT dominated by existing evidence (B-E0 measured roll-up of EXISTING coverage on English word mass; A's estimand — savings from NEW coverage, fertility-weighted, cross-lingual — has no measurement anywhere in the registry).
rationale: DEFER (not reject) for idea B: measured savings 0.399-0.444% of word mass is ~4.5-5x below the 2% K-B0 sanity floor on the deliberately favourable m0b corpus, and every lever INSIDE the idea's own design is measured small or off-target (parse permissiveness +~10% relative and lossy; OR-forfeit <0.005%; clause-AND ~0), while the machinery itself is exact (B-RT 1786/1786) and the constraint (vocabulary coverage) is exogenous and growable — a reject would overclaim beyond the single-corpus single-instance envelope, a pursue would spend on a measured coverage ceiling. PURSUE-Tier-0 (not full pursue) for idea A: its live-or-die number (the A-E2 value curve vs the K-A2 floor) is unmeasured and costs $0 once inputs are pinned, so cheapest-decisive-first requires buying that number before any of the maintainer-cleared $5-40 spend; strong nulls (tokenizer-vocabulary extension K-A3, trained compressor K-A4/HE3) are consumption-stage kills that cannot be settled by census and are not grounds to skip it. Revisit condition: superseded by the freeze-time margins of any A/B candidate prereg, or by a fired re-entry census for idea B.

claim: A-E2 input pins (discharges the a-e2-frequency-corpus-signoff boundary-stop of registry/assessments/compression-census.json): frequency source = the wordfreq-lineage aggregated per-language frequency lists (published data files, consumed directly; no counting pipeline), sha256-pinned at fetch time with source URL + version recorded in a committed manifest, fail-closed on provenance; census languages = en (anchor + only m-hat-sampled cell), es (high usage share, moderate BPE fertility), fi (extreme morphological fertility, Latin script), ja (non-Latin script fertility); language weights w_l = uniform primary + a usage-share sensitivity run reported alongside; tokenizers = SmolLM2-135M tokenizer.json (the mandatory R1 host tokenizer) + one R4-family tokenizer.json for the extension predicate; m-hat handling = sampled with the English-only a1-hybrid mapper on English cells, membership-only (m-hat=1 upper bound, disclosed per cell) on non-English cells with K-A4 marked unpriced there. The input fetch (tokenizer.json files + frequency lists) is ruled IN-SCOPE of the bead's existing maintainer Tier-0 clearance once pinned per this ASM; execution and the actual download are the coordinator's, not the design record's.
rationale: wordfreq-lineage lists are multi-source-harmonised per language, small, permissively licensed, and deterministic to consume, whereas Leipzig/OSCAR/CC-100 slices require building and pinning a counting pipeline that adds cost and no decision value at census stage (they remain available for a later robustness pass); the four languages span the fertility axis the value objective weights (already-1-token English contributes ~0 savings by construction, so an English-only census would be biased toward K-A2 firing) while staying within what published per-language lists cover; uniform w_l avoids smuggling an unmeasured workload-mix assumption and the sensitivity run prices the choice per the workload-mix discipline; membership-only non-English cells are the honest $0 scope given the mapper is English-only — they upper-bound value and are disclosed as such. Revisit condition: all pins re-affirmed or reset at any A-candidate freeze; maintainer veto standing.

claim: A-F1 (cross-lingual concept alignment) is DEFERRED out of A-E2: the census runs WITHOUT cross-lingual concept identity, computing per-language value curves independently, and reads them as exact brackets of the aligned quantity — at a fixed concept budget k per language, the per-language curve is a LOWER bracket of aligned value (alignment only merges surface rows into fewer concepts, raising surfaces-per-concept and hence value-per-concept, never lowering it under the additive value objective), and the cross-language sum at total budget = sum of the k_l is an UPPER bracket (perfect alignment cannot exceed pooling every language's savings).
