Decision asymmetry, fixed now: K-A2 may be declared to fire only off the UPPER bracket; a go/proceed reading may rest only on the LOWER bracket; a between-brackets outcome requires designing A-F1 before any mint decision.
rationale: Alignment data (translation/sense links) is exactly the input class that is absent locally and expensive to pin, and the bracketing property is a construction-level consequence of the additive form of value(c) — so deferring A-F1 keeps A-E2 at $0 without weakening either decision direction: a kill off the upper bracket is conservative-valid (aligned value can only be lower than pooled), a go off the lower bracket is conservative-valid (aligned value can only be higher than unaligned per-language), and only the ambiguous middle pays for alignment work. Revisit condition: A-E2 lands between brackets, or any mint decision is reached.

claim: Compression-track ordering and spend gates (tightens the design's parallel Tier-0 ordering): A-E2 runs FIRST (Tier 0, $0, CPU-only, after the ASM-0461 input fetch); A-F0 (the maintainer-flagged symbolic-output fork sub-test, ~$5-10, already maintainer-cleared) runs only AFTER the A-E2 readout but SURVIVES a K-A2 fire, because cached-prefix mint economics serve the coverage-growth track generally (idea B's named re-entry lever and the linter's coverage staircase), not idea A alone; A-E1 (500-concept mint pilot, ~$20-40) additionally requires ALL of {A-F0 verdict, a live selection target whose A-E2 curve did not fire K-A2, maintainer spend-ack}; A-E3 and B-E1 remain behind F3's own gates (and B-E1 also behind the ASM-0460 un-park). No compression-track item may displace the feasibility-synthesis critical path (docs/next/feasibility-synthesis.md section 5): every item authorized here is Tier-0 CPU or already-cleared sub-$10 spend.
backing_ref: docs/next/io-compression-ideas.md (evaluation plans + recommended order); docs/next/feasibility-synthesis.md section 5; registry/assessments/compression-census.json idea_a_a_e2_read
rationale: Strict cheapest-decisive-first: A-E2 is free and can kill A-E1's premise (a dead value curve makes volume-mint economics moot) but cannot kill A-F0's (mint economics retain programme value for coverage growth regardless of idea A's selection), so the design's original parallel ordering is tightened to sequential at zero cost — A-E2 has no dependency on A-F0, the re-order wastes nothing, and it prevents spending the $5-10 an afternoon early on a fork whose primary consumer may be dead. The critical-path clause exists because the feasibility synthesis ranks attribution/circularity work above all compression work and the sign-off must not create scheduling pressure against it. Revisit condition: reset at any A-candidate freeze.

claim: Sizing expectations for A-E2, load-bearing for nothing: (a) the value curve is expected to concentrate in the non-English cells, because high-frequency English words are frequently already single BPE tokens (contributing ~0 by construction of value(c)) and English-centric BPE fertility is reported to run severalfold higher on non-English surfaces (direction from the design doc's explicitly-unverified memory-tagged literature note); (b) m-hat-weighting is expected to discount the high-frequency (most polysemous) strata materially, direction extrapolated from B-E0's finding that unmapped-word/mappability dominates abstention on covered English — a different estimand, English-only, magnitude unknown for A's selection strata. Used only to set expectations and to justify making m-hat-weighted reporting mandatory in A-E2; never a premise for any gate, verdict, or spend.
backing_ref: registry/assessments/compression-census.json (direction b: unmapped-word 2,068,450 dominant abstain cause); docs/next/io-compression-ideas.md (direction a: the memory-tagged fertility-asymmetry note, must be verified at source before any prereg leans on it)
resolution_path: A-E2 measures both terms directly on the ASM-0461-pinned tokenizers and frequency lists (per-language fertility on OUR pinned tokenizers replaces the literature claim; m-hat sampling on English cells replaces the B-E0 cross-caution); whatever it returns supersedes this band.

claim: NLB scorer-enrichment lawfulness (extends the ASM-0424 lawful scorer-side edit set by exactly ONE named, diff-scoped edit): score_nl in tools/experiments/nlb/nlb_instrument.py gains four ADDITIVE per-family covered-outcome buckets in by_family - {exact, wrong, refused_parse, refused_engine} - each incremented on exactly the same branch as its existing run-level counterpart (n_covered_exact / n_covered_answered_wrong / n_covered_refused_parse / n_covered_refused_engine), zeros on control families, n and ok semantics unchanged: a pure re-bucketing of outcomes already classified AFTER the front-end has produced them. The blindness invariant is untouched: nlb_frontend.py, nlb_map.mjs, the mapper a1-hybrid policy pin and every phrasing byte stay byte-frozen; run_nl_arm, fabricate, oracle construction and the parse invocation are byte-unchanged; the doubled-pass determinism gate G6 compares outcomes, not metrics. The new sha 3d92e1ab7ef71ae577f63f8955f4381bc90a7c257e44102089220b96e25853d2 (supersedes pre-EVAL pin 426722aa813a7843190849348a6b309f3e898d19a0b979f354d5207dc85c6073) is re-pinned in BOTH DRAFT records' harness_manifest (l3a-parse + a5-nl; shared harness FK-NLB-1); analysis/a5_nl.py ignores the additive keys and stays unchanged. The edit is OUTCOME-INVARIANT: its schema is forced by the pre-diagnostic section-14.6 step-2 requirement (per-family wrong/refused for the FK-NLB-10 re-scope), not by the runner's disclosed post-audit diagnostic peek at mapper-parse outcomes (commit 0847ce079189b4d1244911fe026b0799e1db4da7, no committed artifact) - the same edit would be ratified whatever that diagnostic had shown. Any OTHER edit to nlb_instrument.py after the pre-EVAL pin still mints a new record id; the independent skeptic re-attack scope extends to this ASM and to the diagnostic disclosure.
backing_ref: docs/design-nl-boundary-l3a-parse-a5-nl.md section 14.7; tools/experiments/nlb/nlb_instrument.py sha256 3d92e1ab7ef71ae577f63f8955f4381bc90a7c257e44102089220b96e25853d2; prior pin 426722aa813a7843190849348a6b309f3e898d19a0b979f354d5207dc85c6073; runner blocker + diagnostic disclosure commit 0847ce079189b4d1244911fe026b0799e1db4da7; verification: both green-mocks re-run under the enriched instrument with receipts byte-identical except wall-clock frontend_total_ns (poc/nlb-mock/l3a + poc/nlb-mock/a5, checks all green)
rationale: Section 14.6 step 2 needs the S2 numerator restricted to the 7 in-scope families and a descriptive per-family shape-ambiguous stratum; by_family {n, ok} carries exactness only, so neither quantity is derivable from the pinned emission plus run totals, and substituting the run-level total_wrong would silently rest on the contingent observation that the dropped families all abstained - exactly the manufactured-kill pathway section 14.1 preempts if that observation ever fails to hold on a re-run. Enriching the aggregation function is the minimal correct-by-construction alternative: score_nl neither parses nor answers, so amending it before any scored arm runs cannot leak eval content into the system under test (the ASM-0424 lawfulness basis, which enumerated the lint/analysis scripts but not the instrument aggregation function; this ASM closes that enumeration gap for this one edit and no other). Emitting raw per-qid outcomes instead was rejected as a larger surface change to the pinned kot-log/1 body; re-scoring inside the analysis script was rejected because the pinned analysis consumes record bodies only and must not re-run the pipeline. Fail-closed boundary: the permission is single-edit and diff-scoped, verified by the pinned sha pair; it does not open nlb_instrument.py to further amendment.

claim: Interpretive scope rule for the nsk1 Stage-1 INCONCLUSIVE outcome (successor to ASM-0340 for Stage-1 results, per ASM-0340's revisit condition): the label licenses exactly this - (a) internal-write DELIVERY at echo grade is REPLICATED at R3: keyed margin accuracy 0.810/0.850 at (lh,lt)=(12,16)/(16,16) at alpha=1.0 (same point values as B2P), Wilson UB95 0.8514/0.8868 >= the 0.70 replication floor, against THREE independent Sattolo derangement seeds with all coin validity tripwires clean; (b) keying decays into the control band below alpha=1.0 (0.685/0.625 at 0.75; 0.54-0.63 at 0.5; 0.54-0.56 at 0.25) while C100 breakage co-attenuates (32-34 to 4-5 per 100) - no measured strength window separates keying from damage on this operator; (c) the echo-proof integration endpoint R- fired at ZERO of 8 units and is at-or-below the content-free coin null everywhere (mean delta vs coin -0.050 to -0.010; pooled UB95 <= 0.0384 vs the 0.10 kill bound; never beats the role control), a NET bound under ASM-0404's conservative anti-gold sign semantics; (d) the echo-confounded R+ endpoint carries a sub-floor positive residual at alpha=1.0 only (0.245/0.225 vs coin 0.125-0.240; mean delta +0.075/+0.078; not all-seeds significant at 0.00625; delta floors unmet). It does NOT license: any integration claim ('internally delivered grounding helps generation' remains UNRESOLVED - not upgraded); any integration-kill claim (not downgraded - see ASM-0501); any emission-rescue claim (R+ never fired); safety claims; or any widening across hosts, forms, tasks, operators, positions, cells, alphas, or n - each widening re-classifies the citation as EXTRAPOLATION. Every Stage-1 row is phase:exploratory - quarantined, uncitable in a verdict, flips nothing.
backing_ref: poc/nsk1/out/stage1/stage1_summary.json sha256 63525fcd5f67407752abeeedb0f02e31892d78ca74249f044bbb2094a5832626; poc/nsk1/out/stage1/stage1_rows.jsonl sha256 fd9f4e02baf2b1c4b9a9def9cae59a1b4cabf62c78116a4b2b32efabe3047bd7 (offline gate recompute with exact agreement in registry/assessments/nsk1-stage1.json); docs/next/nsk1-stage1-spec.md section 5 ordered rule + ASM-0401 floors; harness poc/modal/modal_nsk1_g2.py sha256 97f9758c2fff39e07f97ddfe74cf93cc7561807ea73b45ebd6e79b87a2833ec4
rationale: Fixes now what an INCONCLUSIVE that closes the flagship internal-steering arc may be cited FOR, before it ages into either an overclaim (echo-grade delivery quietly becoming 'steering works') or an over-negation (a blocked both-endpoint KILL quietly becoming 'integration is dead'). Symmetric to ASM-0340's function for the B2P positive and ASM-0110's for the B-prime negative. Revisit condition: superseded for successor results by any future internal-channel probe's own scope rule when that probe reads out.

claim: Stage-1 kill-attribution rule (binding on all citation of the Stage-1 bounds): the pre-declared KILL-NO-KEYED-RESCUE label is a BOTH-endpoints x ALL-8-units conjunction (spec section 5 rule 6) and failed at exactly 2 of 16 endpoint-units - both on the echo-confounded PLUS endpoint at alpha=1.0 (pooled UB95 0.1423 at (12,16) and 0.1390 at (16,16) vs the 0.10 bound); the MINUS (integration) endpoint is below the kill bound at every unit (max pooled UB95 0.0384). Because NO per-endpoint kill label was pre-declared, no 'integration is killed/refuted' statement may be minted post hoc from the R- bounds; the R- numbers are citable only as MEASURED-exploratory NET-rescue bounds under ASM-0404's conservative sign semantics (the minus-sign write pushes the bridge surface AGAINST gold - measured on this run's C100 frontier: 32/34 per 100 minus-sign breaks at alpha=1.0 with 27/26 emitting the bridge name), which do not separate 'no integration' from 'integration cancelled by the operator's own anti-gold surface push' from 'integration reachable only under a different operator/read-out family', at any sample size on this design.
backing_ref: poc/nsk1/out/stage1/stage1_summary.json sha256 63525fcd5f67407752abeeedb0f02e31892d78ca74249f044bbb2094a5832626; poc/nsk1/out/stage1/stage1_rows.jsonl sha256 fd9f4e02baf2b1c4b9a9def9cae59a1b4cabf62c78116a4b2b32efabe3047bd7 (offline gate recompute with exact agreement in registry/assessments/nsk1-stage1.json); docs/next/nsk1-stage1-spec.md section 5 ordered rule + ASM-0401 floors; harness poc/modal/modal_nsk1_g2.py sha256 97f9758c2fff39e07f97ddfe74cf93cc7561807ea73b45ebd6e79b87a2833ec4
rationale: The single most tempting misreading of this run is 'the integration endpoint was tightly bounded below the kill threshold everywhere, therefore integration is dead'. That reading fails twice: it mints a decision label the spec never pre-declared (the anti-re-narration rule cuts against over-negation exactly as it cuts against overclaiming), and it ignores the registered construct conservatism that makes the R- bound a net bound. Fixing the attribution mechanically (which endpoint-units blocked the kill, with numbers) prevents both the overclaim and the over-negation. Revisit condition: mooted if a successor spec with pre-declared per-endpoint kill labels reads out on a de-confounded operator.

claim: Stage-1 instrument lesson, adopted as a binding successor-design constraint for this probe family (two-candidate keyed steering/delivery/rescue probes with derangement controls): (1) content-free rescue is SEED-HETEROGENEOUS - the coin null at the same (cell, alpha) unit spanned 0.125-0.240 across the three derangement seeds at alpha=1.0 - and a single-seed design would have fired a false-or-unreplicable emission-rescue positive at 2 of 3 seed choices at (12,16)/alpha=1.0 (all three FIRE conjuncts satisfied under seed 20260713 alone: coin p 0.0023, role p 0.0427, delta 0.100; and under 20260714 alone: coin p 0.00036, role p 0.0063, delta 0.120; blocked only by seed 20260712, coin null 0.240); therefore successor probes in this family MUST carry >= 3 independent derangement seeds with an all-seeds conjunct. (2) Probes with multiple endpoints of different claim grades MUST pre-declare PER-ENDPOINT kill labels if a decisive per-endpoint negative is wanted - Stage-1's both-endpoint KILL left a tightly-bounded endpoint formally unresolved because a different endpoint blocked the label.
backing_ref: poc/nsk1/out/stage1/stage1_summary.json sha256 63525fcd5f67407752abeeedb0f02e31892d78ca74249f044bbb2094a5832626; poc/nsk1/out/stage1/stage1_rows.jsonl sha256 fd9f4e02baf2b1c4b9a9def9cae59a1b4cabf62c78116a4b2b32efabe3047bd7 (offline gate recompute with exact agreement in registry/assessments/nsk1-stage1.json); docs/next/nsk1-stage1-spec.md section 5 ordered rule + ASM-0401 floors; harness poc/modal/modal_nsk1_g2.py sha256 97f9758c2fff39e07f97ddfe74cf93cc7561807ea73b45ebd6e79b87a2833ec4; single_seed_counterfactual + kill_blocker_attribution recounts in registry/assessments/nsk1-stage1.json
rationale: Both clauses are choices ratified from measured facts, so the constraint is STIPULATED with measured backing: the seed-heterogeneity counterfactual is the programme's first direct measurement of how a single-derangement design of this family would have minted a false positive, and the kill-conjunction shape cost this run a decisive per-endpoint reading it had effectively earned. Exported alongside the ASM-0041/ASM-0111 lesson chain (controls must be chance by arithmetic; now also: control NULLS vary by seed, and kill labels must match the endpoint structure of the claims). Revisit condition: none - supersede by a stronger successor convention if one is registered.

claim: Post-Stage-1 sequencing constraint (successor to the discharged ASM-0342, binding on successor specs in the nsk1 line): the internal-steering measurement arc (B-prime -> B2P -> Stage-1) is CLOSED as a measurement sequence; NO further spend on the Stage-1 operator/read-out construct is licensed for the INTEGRATION question, because (a) the echo-proof endpoint is already tightly bounded NET (pooled UB95 <= 0.0384 everywhere measured) so added power sharpens a bound, not an answer; (b) the only positive residual (R+ at alpha=1.0) is echo-confounded by construction and cannot license integration at any n (ASM-0404 boundary), while design-time arithmetic - not evidence - puts even its per-seed 0.00625 conjunct near n >= 540 at the observed ~0.25 discordance; and (c) the corpus failure population is EXHAUSTED (Swept = full F = 200; n_failures 200, swept_is_full_F true), so added power requires new items and a FOURTH disclosed corpus contact. Any revival of the internal-integration question requires a NEW operator/read-out family under a fresh spec with its own gates: one that de-confounds fact-content delivery from answer-surface push (e.g. fact-frame injection without a name-slot push, multi-position or earlier-position delivery, or read-out-placement designs), with per-endpoint kill labels and >= 3 derangement seeds per ASM-0502. Until the maintainer commissions such a spec, the flagship's delivery-topology weight defaults back to engine-external designs per the feasibility synthesis. The maintainer may override at the fork; this constraint binds designer specs, not the maintainer.
backing_ref: poc/nsk1/out/stage1/stage1_summary.json sha256 63525fcd5f67407752abeeedb0f02e31892d78ca74249f044bbb2094a5832626; poc/nsk1/out/stage1/stage1_rows.jsonl sha256 fd9f4e02baf2b1c4b9a9def9cae59a1b4cabf62c78116a4b2b32efabe3047bd7 (offline gate recompute with exact agreement in registry/assessments/nsk1-stage1.json); docs/next/nsk1-stage1-spec.md section 5 ordered rule + ASM-0401 floors; harness poc/modal/modal_nsk1_g2.py sha256 97f9758c2fff39e07f97ddfe74cf93cc7561807ea73b45ebd6e79b87a2833ec4; registry/assessments/nsk1-stage1.json fork_recommendation; ASM-0342 discharge recorded there
rationale: Prevents the two live failure modes of an inconclusive that arrives with one tight bound and one confounded residual: grinding more n into a construct that cannot answer the question at any n (spend-without-information), and quietly re-running until a sub-floor residual clears a threshold somewhere (garden-of-forking-paths). The rank ordering (accept-and-record first, de-confounded redesign only if the maintainer keeps the question alive) mirrors the honesty rule that redirects follow measured reasons. Revisit condition: discharged when the maintainer's post-Stage-1 fork is decided and recorded, or superseded by the new operator spec's own sequencing clause if rank 2 is chosen.

claim: A-E2 census interpretation, sub-method ruling 1 of 3 (reserved-block 0560-0579 use: A-E2 cross-lingual census interpretation; ratifies RUN-LOG flag 1): the English m-hat operationalisation used by the committed A-E2 run - the ISOLATED-SURFACE a1-hybrid mapper decision (m-hat=0 iff the mapper abstains on the bare surface, else 1; policy sha e13dc838..., kernel-v0 manifest sha da56bf1f...) - is RATIFIED as the lawful reading of ASM-0461's 'sampled with the English-only a1-hybrid mapper' for this run, WITH a mandatory reclassification: because it abstains on only 164 of 321,180 English surfaces (intrinsic-surface collisions only; context-dependent polysemy invisible to the 119-entry lexicon), the m-hat cells are a SECOND DISCLOSED UPPER BOUND near-coincident with membership, NOT a K-A4 pricing. K-A4 (mappability collapse) is therefore UNPRICED in every cell of the census, English included; the ASM-0460 requirement that m-hat-weighted cells price K-A4 up front is discharged in letter only. Consequence, binding on successor specs: a real K-A4 pricing instrument (in-context m-hat sampling on a provenance-pinned corpus, or a polysemy-stratified discount over the value ranking, covering at least English + one high-fertility census language) is REQUIRED before any A-E1 mint-selection freeze; no A-E2 number may be quoted as mappability-priced.
backing_ref: poc/a-e2/results/summary.json sha256 90eb62834b06c3b929dc84073430eed88d974ff7de9280bd3421f7203b1e3a18; poc/a-e2/results/en-mhat.jsonl sha256 d9a4de33d08f1e4563ac39733602a4eea4bbca004957696ae1c3a6b556891010; poc/a-e2/RUN-LOG.md sha256 287b2982a2290a24f8ec362010edaae6798656603021ede0868dce42290f0297; registry/assessments/a-e2-census.json; ASM-0460; ASM-0461
rationale: The in-context (M0a-style) alternative reading was unlawful at census time: the on-box TinyStories copy is not byte-identical to the M0a-pinned corpus (19,447,282 B / 21,989 stories vs pinned 19,432,980 B / 21,990) and fail-closed provenance forbids substituting unpinned bytes, so the isolated-surface decision was the only executable $0 operationalisation - ratifying it preserves the run's validity without pretending it measures more than it does. The reclassification is forced by the measured abstention rate: a 0.05% discount cannot price a risk that B-E0 measured as the BINDING term on covered English (unmapped-word 2,068,450 dominant abstain cause), and letting a near-no-op stand in for K-A4 pricing would be exactly the overclaim the census quarantine exists to prevent. Revisit condition: superseded by the K-A4 pricing instrument's result, or reset at any A-candidate freeze.

claim: A-E2 census interpretation, sub-method ruling 2 of 3 (ratifies RUN-LOG flag 2): the usage-share weight vector used by the committed A-E2 run (raw web-content-share inputs en 0.520 / es 0.055 / ja 0.045 / fi 0.005, renormalised over the 4 census languages to en 0.832 / es 0.088 / ja 0.072 / fi 0.008) is RATIFIED as a STIPULATED-exploratory HARSH-ENDPOINT SENSITIVITY ARM ONLY - illustrative, English-dominant, not a measurement of any workload. Binding consequences: (1) no gate read, verdict, spend decision, or headline quote on the compression track may rest on either weight arm alone - the uniform-vs-usage-share SPAN must be carried whole; (2) the freeze-time workload mix is a maintainer ratification act (workload-mix discipline), and until it happens every A-E2-derived margin is span-valued; (3) the renormalisation direction is noted as conservative-harsh for idea A (concentrating 0.832 on the near-zero-value English cell while omitting the roughly one-third of web content in other, mostly fertility-bearing, languages), so the arm is a floor-shaped endpoint, not a central estimate.
backing_ref: poc/a-e2/results/summary.json sha256 90eb62834b06c3b929dc84073430eed88d974ff7de9280bd3421f7203b1e3a18 (weight_arms block, tag STIPULATED-exploratory); poc/a-e2/RUN-LOG.md sha256 287b2982a2290a24f8ec362010edaae6798656603021ede0868dce42290f0297; registry/assessments/a-e2-census.json; ASM-0461
rationale: ASM-0461 pins the arm's existence but not its values, and no measured workload mix exists anywhere in the programme - so the only honest standing for ANY concrete vector is a declared sensitivity endpoint; ratifying this one keeps the committed run quotable while the span-reporting rule prevents the ~2x weight-arm leverage (blended @10k roughly halves under the English-dominant arm) from being smuggled into a decision as if it were data. The harsh direction is the right one for an endpoint that will sit under kill-side scrutiny. Revisit condition: superseded by a maintainer-ratified workload mix at any A-candidate freeze.

claim: A-E2 census interpretation, sub-method ruling 3 of 3 (ratifies RUN-LOG flag 3): the run-added 'wordlike' surface filter (mint candidates restricted to surfaces with >= 1 alphabetic character and no digit; numerator-only - the savings denominator stays over ALL listed surfaces) is RATIFIED AND ADOPTED as the DECISION-RELEVANT mint-candidate surface universe for all gate reads and headline quotes on the compression track; the unfiltered 'all' cells are retained as disclosure-only (they size the numeric-artifact inflation: e.g. SmolLM2 en @10k 10.65% -> 5.90% under the filter). Decision-facing A-E2 headline under this ruling: blended uniform membership wordlike @10k = 33.4-41.7% (SmolLM2-135M, lower/upper ASM-0462 bracket) and 18.5-24.0% (Qwen2.5-0.5B); harshest lawful cell (Qwen2.5 | usage-share | m-hat | wordlike) = 4.79% lower / 7.06% upper.
backing_ref: poc/a-e2/results/summary.json sha256 90eb62834b06c3b929dc84073430eed88d974ff7de9280bd3421f7203b1e3a18 (surface_filter_note + headline_blended_@10k); registry/assessments/a-e2-census.json; ASM-0461; ASM-0462
rationale: Digit-bearing/degenerate surfaces ('0000', '00,000') that float to the top of the raw value ranking are frequency-list construction artifacts, not mintable concepts under the programme's concept notion - a concept kernel cannot honestly claim their BPE expense as harvestable value, and leaving them in would inflate exactly the cells (English, usage-share) that kill-side scrutiny weights most. The numerator-only construction makes the filter strictly conservative (it can only lower reported savings), so adopting it tightens honesty at zero risk of optimism. Revisit condition: reset at any A-candidate freeze if a principled multi-word/phrase or alternative candidate-universe definition supersedes it.

claim: A-E2 K-A2 gate status (interpretive; nothing frozen, the 5% floor is the STIPULATED sanity default that resets at freeze): K-A2 DOES NOT FIRE anywhere on the 16-cell census grid - under ASM-0462 the kill may fire only off the UPPER bracket, and the minimum upper bracket @10k concepts across all cells is 7.06% (Qwen2.5 | usage-share | m-hat | wordlike), >= the 5% floor, with the decision-facing (ASM-0562 wordlike, uniform) cells clearing kill-side by 4.8x (Qwen2.5, 24.0%) to 8.3x (SmolLM2, 41.7%). Go-side (lower-bracket) readings clear the floor in 15 of 16 cells; the single harshest cell straddles the floor BETWEEN brackets (4.79% lower / 7.06% upper), which per ASM-0462's between-brackets clause CONDITIONALLY ARMS the A-F1-before-mint requirement: if the freeze-time consumption currency is a high-CJK-coverage Qwen-like tokenizer AND the maintainer-ratified workload mix is English-dominant, A-F1 (cross-lingual alignment) must be designed before any mint decision. Ladder consequence per ASM-0463: the 'after the A-E2 readout' condition on A-F0 is satisfied - A-F0 (cached-prefix mint economics, ~$5-10, already maintainer-cleared) is the next rung; A-E1 additionally acquires the ASM-0560 K-A4-pricing prerequisite. All of this is a membership-upper-bound reading and licenses no achievable-savings claim (K-A3/consumption nulls remain live downstream kills).
backing_ref: poc/a-e2/results/summary.json sha256 90eb62834b06c3b929dc84073430eed88d974ff7de9280bd3421f7203b1e3a18; registry/assessments/a-e2-census.json; ASM-0460; ASM-0462; ASM-0463
rationale: The bracketing asymmetry was fixed in advance precisely so this readout could be read mechanically: kill only off upper brackets (conservative-valid: aligned value cannot exceed pooling), go only off lower brackets (conservative-valid: aligned value cannot be below unaligned per-language). Applying it yields a clean no-fire with one honestly-recorded straddle cell rather than either an overclaimed all-clear or a floor-shopped kill; arming A-F1 conditionally (on the exact currency+mix combination that produces the straddle) spends alignment-design effort only in the world where it is decision-relevant. Recording the gate status as a registered assumption makes the census's decision consequence citable without re-deriving it from the grid. Revisit condition: superseded by the freeze-time K-A2 floor and margins of any A-candidate prereg, or by the K-A4 pricing instrument materially moving the harshest cells.

claim: Sizing expectation for the compression track, load-bearing for nothing: a REAL K-A4 mappability pricing (in-context, polysemy-aware) is expected to discount the A-E2 membership band materially - plausibly enough to pull the harshest grid cells to or below the K-A2 floor, while leaving the uniform/high-fertility cells clear. Direction is extrapolated from B-E0's measured finding that mappability (unmapped-word abstention) is the binding term on covered English; magnitude is unknown for A-E2's selection strata and for every non-English cell (zero mappability evidence exists there in either direction). Used only to justify the ASM-0560 requirement that the pricing instrument precede any A-E1 selection freeze; never a premise for any gate, verdict, or spend.
backing_ref: registry/assessments/compression-census.json (unmapped-word 2,068,450 dominant abstain cause on covered English); registry/assessments/a-e2-census.json; ASM-0560
resolution_path: The a-e2-ka4-pricing-instrument stub (registry/assessments/a-e2-census.json questions_opened): in-context m-hat sampling on a provenance-pinned corpus or a polysemy-stratified discount, at least English + one high-fertility census language; whatever it measures supersedes this band.

claim: g9-llmproxy stand-in scope + judge choice: the g9.review blinded-HUMAN review (GATE-H, ~4h) is unavailable at run time, so a SEPARATE fresh-id proxy record (g9-llmproxy) puts a pinned kernel-instance-naive LLM in the reviewer role; CROSS-VENDOR is mandatory because the 50 judged explications are Fable-authored (design-vs-judge separation), so the judge is gpt-5.6-sol effort low via npx-pinned codex-cli 0.144.1; the frozen g9 stays byte-untouched, its blinded_review_done gate can be set ONLY by the human review, and the proxy adjudicates nothing about HS-A.
rationale: Same three grounds as the f2b-transfer-llmproxy S1 precedent: reviewer identity is design scope (a different estimand), consuming g9's single review stage on the weaker instrument would exile the definitive human review to a new id, and a correction to the frozen record would be a post-hoc protocol change. Cross-vendor judging is the only assignment that does not grade the author's own model family.

claim: g9-llmproxy metric operationalisation: substitutability and cross-translatability are rendered as per-sheet blind yes/no/cannot-say judgments computed from the blinded sheet bytes alone (candidate explication re-labelled 'proposed paraphrase' to reduce tradition cueing); composite pass = MEASURED mechanical legality AND substitutable=yes AND cross_translatable=yes, cannot-say counting against pass. If the human g9.review operationalises the published metric definitions differently (e.g. translation-based cross-translatability), the proxy legs are form-analogous only; the cross-translatability leg additionally has NO content-scramble control by construction (text-intrinsic property) and is disclosed as the weakest leg.
rationale: The frozen g9 fixes the metric NAMES and the composite bar but the judged rendering must be fixed by someone before any reviewer (human or LLM) acts; fixing it here, pre-run, keeps the instrument frozen-before-data. The judged-property form is the only one executable blind per-item; the divergence risk vs the published protocol is carried as ASM-0522.

claim: g9-llmproxy severity-comparability: the proxy judge's strictness on substitutability/cross-translatability is uncalibrated to the published DeepNSM evaluation protocol, so clearing or missing the frozen +0.10 margin vs the published 0.24 point is form-preserving only; direction and size of the severity mismatch unproven; NEITHER proxy outcome bears on HS-A, the why-now argument, or authoring-cost estimates.
resolution_path: Run the human g9.review (GATE-H) on the identical 50 blinded sheets; human-composite minus proxy-composite on identical items measures the proxy severity offset directly, and the human number alone feeds the frozen g9 margin test.

claim: g9-llmproxy probe design: the deranged-explication probe pairs each sheet's word/def/examples with another sheet's paraphrase via a seed-pinned Sattolo cycle (g9lp/1|probe|20260710), making 'no' the correct substitutability answer by construction (near-synonym collision risk over the 50 distinct DeepNSM test-split words disclosed as negligible-but-nonzero); false-endorsement >= 0.30 or probe coverage < 45/50 invalidates the instrument; the probe controls the substitutability leg ONLY.
rationale: House content-scramble idiom (f2b-transfer-llmproxy deranged-gloss probe) transplanted to the review task; it is the cheapest mechanical bound on the style/familiarity channel that would fabricate a proxy-PASS, and it is constructible from the pinned sheet bytes with no gold labels.

claim: g3-llmproxy stand-in scope + judge pair: the two-independent-human g3.annotate role (GATE-H, ~8-10h, blind to hypotheses) is unsourced at run time (O-3), so a SEPARATE fresh-id proxy record fills it with a cross-family LLM pair — judge-pA gpt-5.6-sol (codex-cli 0.144.1, effort low) and judge-pB claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 (headless claude -p, truthstyle section-4.3 hardening; vendor-family overlap with the materials' authoring agents DISCLOSED, never sole gold); stateless per-item calls make the frozen all-pass-A-before-pass-B contamination rule structural; the frozen g3 stays byte-untouched and remains the only adjudicator of HS3 and the only trigger of HS2 auto-resolution / g5 pruning.
rationale: Mirrors the frozen two-annotator design so the kappa statistic keeps its frozen role; a cross-family pair is the strongest independence available without humans and gives kappa real diagnostic content (unlike the f2b single-family pair), while the disclosed judge-pB family overlap is bounded by pair status + the probe gate.

claim: g3-llmproxy bracket verdict rule: the human record deliberately left the two-annotator combination rule open, so the proxy PRE-DECLARES a two-sided bracket instead of consuming that decision — PASS-analog requires the UNION-rule necessity rate's Wilson 95% UB <= 0.10 (upper bracket), FAIL-analog requires the CONCORDANT-rule rate's Wilson 95% LB > 0.10 (lower bracket); each verdict direction must clear under the combination rule LEAST favourable to it; anything between is INCONCLUSIVE.
rationale: Any single combination rule would bias one verdict direction and would also pre-empt an open human-round decision; bracketing is mechanical, conservative in both directions, and leaves the human round's degrees of freedom untouched.

claim: g3-llmproxy estimand gap: HS3 is a claim about human ordinary usage; LLM pass-A truth-of-claim and pass-B literal condition-satisfaction judgments approximate human judgments with unproven systematic offsets (the literalism channel can push the necessity-violation rate up via hyper-literal condition failures or down via charitable reading); neither proxy verdict direction measures the human rates.
resolution_path: Run the frozen human g3 (two independent annotators) on the identical 200 instances; per-item human-vs-proxy deltas on q1/q2 measure the offset and its direction directly.

claim: g3-llmproxy escape + coverage gates: cannot-say is neither violation nor satisfaction (the pinned protocol's stated treatment); per judge per pass, cannot-say > 5% or no-label > 5% => INSTRUMENT-INVALID (the protocol's ~5% re-examination line made mechanical, fail-closed); an instance is decisive for a judge iff q1 and q2 are both in {yes,no}; n_dual_decisive >= 170 of 200 keeps both Wilson branches reachable (PASS-analog up to ~11 union violations, FAIL-analog from ~24 concordant, at the floor).
rationale: The human protocol proposed but did not freeze the cannot-say treatment; the proxy must fix it mechanically pre-run, and adopting the protocol's own proposal at its own ~5% line is the minimal-choice instantiation.

claim: g3-llmproxy probe-B construction: 30 seed-pinned instances are presented in pass-B form with the condition set of a DIFFERENT concept (concept-level Sattolo cycle over the 20 condition_set_ids), original text + bindings verbatim; 'no' is the correct answer by construction (another concept's conditions, roles largely unbound); only q2=yes counts as false satisfaction (cannot-say is non-endorsement); false-satisfaction >= 0.30 or probe coverage < 27/30 invalidates that judge's instrument; probes never enter counts.
rationale: The direct pass-B analog of the house content-scramble control: it bounds the yes-saying channel that would fabricate a low violation rate, is constructible from pinned materials without gold labels, and unbound-role artifacts are handled by counting only affirmative endorsement as failure.

claim: m0a-llmproxy stand-in scope + judge choice: the M0a pre-registration names HUMAN annotation; the current provisional P/R (strict 0.82/0.8865) were produced by a NON-BLIND, project-contexted Claude-family agent and are stamped PROVISIONAL; the proxy record replaces them with a blind, kernel-instance-naive, CROSS-VENDOR judge (gpt-5.6-sol effort low via npx-pinned codex-cli 0.144.1) over the same 300 pinned items — upgrading exactly blindness and author-family independence, never species-of-judge; cross-vendor is load-bearing because a Claude-family judge would correlate with the comparator labels and with the Claude-family mapper/gloss/judging work-products.
rationale: The agent-judged stopgap is the weakest link in the published M0a numbers (non-blind, author-adjacent); a blind cross-vendor proxy is a strict control upgrade available now, while the human pass remains the only discharger of the pre-registration — the f2b-transfer-llmproxy pattern applied to an annotation measurement.

claim: m0a-llmproxy rendering contract: per-stratum blind renderings built mechanically from pinned bytes only — concept/prime items show the passage + marked token + the mapped target's gloss WITHOUT any urn/id; abstain items show the recorded candidates as a numbered gloss list; none items show the full pinned kernel-v0 surface inventory (54 concept lemmas + 65 prime exponents, one-line senses, byte-identical across items); the stated M0a judging criteria are carried on the sheets verbatim; the judge never sees urns, stratum labels, the words kernel/NSM/mapper, or that a mapping system produced the proposed sense; per-call blinding grep audit, any hit aborts.
rationale: The human instructions embedded in annotation-sample.jsonl presuppose knowledge of what the targets denote; a blind judge must receive that denotation as gloss bytes, and gloss-rendering-without-ids is the minimal disclosure that makes the task judgeable while keeping the judge kernel-instance-naive.

claim: m0a-llmproxy estimator reuse: precision/recall formulas, stratum populations (concept 117563 / prime 504500 / abstain 181388 / none 2958417), the strict/lenient duality, and the none-stratum Clopper-Pearson recall lower bound are reused EXACTLY from the published mapper/m0 compute-pr.py + m0a-report.json, with denominators restricted to labelled items; the none-stratum thinness (50 items over 2.96M tokens = 78% of token mass) carries over UNCHANGED — the proxy fixes nothing about the sampling design.
rationale: Estimator identity is what makes the proxy numbers a drop-in replacement for the agent-judged provisionals and makes the eventual human pass a per-item comparable readout; changing the estimator would silently change the estimand.

claim: m0a-llmproxy agent-delta reading: per-stratum raw agreement between the blind proxy labels and the 2026-07-07 non-blind agent labels measures blindness effects and judge-family effects JOINTLY; within this design the two are unattributable, so the delta is reported-only and licenses no statement about which judge is closer to truth.
resolution_path: The human pass over the same 300 items: human-vs-proxy and human-vs-agent per-item agreement attribute the joint delta into judge-error components directly.

claim: m0a-llmproxy probe design: the deranged-gloss probe (20 concept + 20 prime items, per-stratum Sattolo cycle over distinct sampled targets, seed m0alp/1|probe|20260710) presents another target's gloss on the same token/context, making 'incorrect' the correct answer by construction (near-synonym collision risk disclosed); false-endorsement >= 0.30 or probe coverage < 36/40 invalidates the instrument; probes never enter labels or P/R.
rationale: Direct transplant of the f2b-transfer-llmproxy deranged-gloss control to the sense-judgment task: it bounds the gloss-endorsement leniency channel that would fabricate high proxy precision, at ~13% of the run's calls.

claim: llmproxy stand-in policy (shared, binding on g9-llmproxy, g3-llmproxy, m0a-llmproxy): per maintainer request (2026-07-10), blind kernel-instance-naive LLM judges stand in for the outstanding human annotation/review passes so the experiments run now and are human-verified later, exactly on the f2b-transfer-llmproxy model. Every proxy record: (a) takes a FRESH id and leaves the human record/pre-registration byte-untouched as the sole gold-standard adjudicator; (b) envelopes its verdict as a WEAK FEASIBILITY PROXY that substitutes for no part of the human pass, is quarantined and uncitable-as-final, and licenses ONLY programme sequencing / continued investment, never a claim upgrade; (c) never fills the human record's pins, never consumes its annotation round, and never triggers the human record's DAG consequences.
rationale: The f2b-transfer-llmproxy precedent (frozen, run, PASS, audit-confirmed, Fable-assessed) established that a fully-enveloped LLM stand-in yields real sequencing value (cheap-kill exposure, comparator pinning, operational de-risking) at near-zero cost without contaminating the human gold standard; this ASM fixes that discipline as the shared policy for the three new proxies. It falls away experiment-by-experiment as each human pass runs.

claim: llmproxy judge-mechanism catalogue (shared): the two lawful blind-judge mechanisms are (1) gpt-5.6-sol, reasoning effort low, via npx-pinned codex-cli 0.144.1 per the pinned judge-1p/truthstyle section-4.1 invocation form (empty out-of-repo workdir, user config ignored, memories + web search disabled, read-only sandbox, server-side output schema, zero-tool tripwire), and (2) claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 via headless claude -p per the truthstyle section-4.3 form (format-only pinned system prompt, tools disabled, no setting sources, no session persistence, thinking disabled, subscription auth asserted). Uniform contract: stateless per-item processes, first-valid-answer-final, <= 3 content attempts then no-label (never mapped to an escape), per-call blinding grep audit, preflight calibration first-attempt pass. The in-session Agent-tool sub-agent form is FORBIDDEN for blind judging (it injects project CLAUDE.md + auto-memory naming the project and NSM).
backing_ref: poc/truthstyle-2x2/judges-invocation.md (invocation semantics MEASURED at its 2026-07-10 staging); registry/experiments/g9-llmproxy.json, g3-llmproxy.json, m0a-llmproxy.json
rationale: Both mechanisms are already staged, measured, and hardened in this repo; reusing them byte-derived avoids new instrument risk and keeps every proxy's blinding auditable by the same mechanical checks.

claim: llmproxy tradition-familiarity direction (shared; generalises ASM-0021 beyond the f2b line): NSM literature, NSM-style explications and reductive-paraphrase conventions are plausibly in BOTH judge vendors' training data, so proxy judges may endorse familiar STYLE rather than judge content; this plausibly inflates endorsement-type statistics (g9 composite, m0a 'correct' rates) and has unproven direction on g3's violation rates; the deranged probes bound only the crudest form in each record.
backing_ref: registry/experiments/g9-llmproxy.json, g3-llmproxy.json, m0a-llmproxy.json extrapolation envelopes; ASM-0021
resolution_path: Each human pass on identical items (g9.review sheets, g3 instances, m0a annotation sample) measures the human-vs-proxy delta directly; jointly with the f2b human round these give the familiarity channel's sign and size across four task types.

claim: llmproxy comparator + non-contamination discipline (shared): every proxy pins its per-item labels so the eventual human pass yields a free per-item human-vs-proxy bias measurement; conversely, proxy outputs (labels, run logs, derived rates, verdicts) must NEVER be shown to a future human annotator/reviewer for the same items before their sheets are submitted, and proxy runs write NOTHING under the pinned human-materials trees (data/authored-explication-set/, data/instance-descriptions/, mapper/m0/ inputs); a violation contaminates the human gold standard and is a boundary-stop incident, escalate to the maintainer.
rationale: The comparator role is the proxies' most durable scientific value (the f2b assessment's finding), and it survives only if the human passes stay naive; making the quarantine an explicit registered obligation binds every future runner and coordinator, not just this design.

claim: llmproxy escape + no-label accounting policy (shared): judge escape tokens (cannot-say / unclear / none-of-these where an endorsement is being tested) count as NON-ENDORSEMENT — against the PASS-side quantity, never silently dropped (direction disclosed per record); no-label after 3 invalid content attempts is an instrument event that leaves denominators and is capped by each record's coverage gate (~5%); escapes are never converted to labels and labels are never re-sampled (first-valid-answer-final).
backing_ref: registry/experiments/g9-llmproxy.json, g3-llmproxy.json, m0a-llmproxy.json instrument gates; data/d-adj-t-llmproxy/judge-1p-invocation.md (the f2b no-label contract)
rationale: Uniform, fail-closed accounting is what makes the three proxies' statistics comparable to each other and to their human passes; the f2b contract is adopted verbatim so no proxy can gain a PASS from escape-handling degrees of freedom.

claim: truthstyle-2x2 section-7.3 blinding-audit matching rule, AMENDMENT A1 (extends ASM-0360; ratified pre-data after the second ERR_DTS_BLINDING boundary stop): on the two HARNESS-CAPTURED surfaces events.jsonl and stderr.log - and NEVER on user-prompt.txt - a case-insensitive token match is additionally excluded from being a hit iff, in the ORIGINAL (pre-lowercasing) bytes, it lies wholly inside the base62 TAIL of a maximal Anthropic-format vendor identifier literal: a byte run matching (req|msg)_[0-9A-Za-z]{20,48} whose 4-byte prefix is lowercase-exact, whose adjacent bytes on both sides (if any) are outside [0-9A-Za-z_] (maximality; a run longer than 48 fails this check and is NOT excluded - fail-closed), with the token wholly within the {20,48} tail. The rule applies uniformly to all four pinned tokens; every byte outside qualifying tails is scanned byte-identically to ASM-0360; abort-on-hit semantics, the token list, the scanned surfaces, and all other tripwires are unchanged. This supersedes IN PART the ASM-0360 side-claim that matching for the non-hex tokens is byte-identical to the original scan.
backing_ref: poc/truthstyle-2x2/blinding-audit-clarification.md section 9 (amendment A1: measured abort facts, base62 rate analysis, leak-safety argument, normative code, residual-risk census); registry/corrections/truthstyle-2x2/4-blinding-audit-amendment-a1.json; poc/truthstyle-2x2/opus-runs/20260710T151231Z/judge-p3-haiku45/items/pos-102/c1_t0/events.jsonl (retained: token nsm at offset 1959 inside request_id req_011Cctc1sK9P4SujinnsMiWU; sha256 1b8d624be5118804d7027d0b58086e6aa6328a43afeb28ca827e1a0394dd72b7)
rationale: ASM-0360 excluded coincidences only for all-hex tokens inside hex runs, on the premise that non-hex blind tokens appear only via real leakage; that premise is contradicted by Anthropic base62 identifiers (req_/msg_ + 24 mixed-case alphanumerics, two per captured p3 attempt), where case-insensitive nsm occurs with p about 7.4e-4 per id, i.e. expected about 1.2 coincidental hits per perfectly blind 808-item p3 pass (abort probability about 0.7 under the null; observed: judge-p3 died at pos-102 on exactly this shape while the item bytes and answer were clean and p1/p2 judged the same item without incident). f2b inside base62 tails with non-hex neighbours was a second latent channel closed by the same rule. The exclusion is anchored on value FORMAT plus maximality, not on JSON field names: it parses nothing, works on non-JSON stderr, never excludes a whole field or line, and never applies to user-prompt.txt, so item contamination and judge echo (which lands in text blocks, not id tails) still fire. Rejected alternatives: dropping id fields from the scan surface (larger blind spot, needs JSON parsing); scrubbing ids from provenance pre-sweep (destroys retained-bytes re-derivability); changing the pinned section-4.3 p3 capture form (inverts the control hierarchy for a detector-side defect).
resolution_path: Mechanically re-derivable: the amended blinding-sweep.py derives vendor-id spans from the SAME _vendor_id_tail_spans helper the abort predicate uses and lists every exclusion with kind (hex_run with surrounding hex run / vendor_id with the full id literal), so the auditor can verify each exclusion is a machine identifier by inspection and can independently re-run the strict, ASM-0360, and A1 scans over the retained bytes of both run dirs. Revisit condition: any clarified-scan hit, any exclusion whose literal is not a machine identifier, or any vendor id format outside the (req|msg)_{20,48} shape coincidentally spelling a token, is a boundary stop back to Fable.

claim: truthstyle-2x2 re-run discipline for the pos-102 A1 false-positive abort: judge-p1 and judge-p2 passes of run poc/truthstyle-2x2/opus-runs/20260710T151231Z STAND as complete (808/808 each) and blinding-clean; ONLY judge-p3 re-runs, as a fresh full protocol (section-8 preflight then section-3-ordered main pass over all 808 items in the frozen p3 seed order) in a NEW run dir under the A1 executor, whose sha256 (with blinding-sweep.py's) is recorded in the new run dir before launch. The aborted p3 partial (pos-001..102) is VOID for labels and retained byte-verbatim as provenance; pos-102's captured bytes are NOT scrubbed and NOT re-captured in place (under A1 they are a listed vendor_id exclusion and the sweep is GREEN over the retained bytes). After MAIN_DONE p3 the coordinator copies the byte-identical p1/p2 run-summary and preflight-status files into the new run dir (shas recorded) and finalizes there; the A1 sweep must be GREEN over BOTH run dirs before the d-ts-labels ops amendment is written. Frozen pins, seeds, judge order, prompts, and the pinned section-4.3 capture form are byte-unchanged.
backing_ref: poc/truthstyle-2x2/blinding-audit-clarification.md sections 9.1 and 9.7; poc/truthstyle-2x2/p3-rerun-plan.md; registry/corrections/truthstyle-2x2/4-blinding-audit-amendment-a1.json; data/d-ts-labels/ listing 2026-07-10 (only p1/p2 responses exist; no p3 label was ever assembled, written, or read)
rationale: ASM-0361 (void the whole campaign) is expressly one-shot and its premises do not transfer: here two passes completed cleanly under the pinned protocol with responses mechanically written at pass end, the p3 abort is a detector-side false positive independent of their content, and the A1 predicate change alters no judge-visible byte, so label distributions cannot depend on it - the final gate is uniform because the A1 sweep re-applies the same predicate over the retained p1/p2 bytes. Resuming p3 at pos-103 is refused for the ASM-0361 reason (the spec defines no resume mechanism; the void partial's labels were never persisted, so resume would need new unpinned logic to re-ingest captured events). Disclosed: during diagnosis the coordinator read the void pos-102 capture (item steamfitter, raw answer bytes no); that sample is void, enters no statistic, the item is re-judged fresh, and first-valid-answer-is-final (ASM-0241) bars outcome-conditioned re-rolls; the re-run criterion is structural, not outcome-dependent. Budget: about 102 discarded p3 calls at the measured 0.0002-0.003 USD/call, inside the frozen usd_cap 40.
resolution_path: One-shot: applies only to the judge-p3 abort of run 20260710T151231Z at pos-102. Any future mid-pass abort is its own boundary stop; this entry is not a standing resume-or-restart policy. Discharged when the new p3 run dir carries the recorded executor shas, run-summary-p3.json, and GREEN blinding-sweep.json outputs for both run dirs.

claim: A-F0 scope and identity (reserved-block 0600-0619 use: A-F0 cached-prefix mint economics; the pre-cleared fork sub-test of N-IOC section 2.3 under the ASM-0463 ladder): A-F0 measures the MINT side of the coverage-growth track ONLY — the price vector ($/concept, $/legal record, tokens by cache class, wall latency) and the cached-prefix amortization curve of minting concepts into the modelAuthored tier via a claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 definer called DIRECTLY over the Messages API with prompt caching. This transport is a NEW pipeline identity per the s1 convention (the pinned call configuration is part of pipeline identity); the s1-G claude-CLI pipeline remains a distinct, unmodified baseline. Binding honesty caps: A-F0 is NEVER quotable as end-to-end viability of idea A or of any compression mechanism; net-positive mint economics is NECESSARY-NOT-SUFFICIENT — the consumption channel (K-A3 tokenizer-vocabulary-extension null, trained-compressor null, 1-token delivery, accuracy non-inferiority) stays entirely UNMEASURED and K-A4 mappability stays UNPRICED (ASM-0560); per ASM-0463 the result serves the WHOLE coverage-growth track (idea B's re-entry lever, the linter staircase), not idea A alone, and nothing in A-F0 may displace the feasibility-synthesis section-5 critical path.
backing_ref: docs/next/io-compression-signoff.md (ASM-0460, ASM-0463); docs/next/io-compression-ideas.md sections 2.2-2.3; registry/assessments/a-e2-census.json (proceed-to-A-F0 fork resolution, ASM-0563); registry/experiments/a-f0-mint-economics.json
rationale: Fixing scope and quotability at freeze closes the two known overclaim channels: reading a cheap mint as compression viability (consumption unmeasured), and reading the fork winner as an idea-A verdict (the mechanism serves the whole coverage-growth track).

claim: A-F0 price basis (frozen conversion table; every USD DV is API usage fields x this table): claude-haiku-4-5 input $1.00/MTok, output $5.00/MTok (thinking tokens bill as output); prompt-cache read $0.10/MTok (0.1x input); prompt-cache write $1.25/MTok at the 5-minute TTL (1.25x; the 1-hour TTL at $2.00/MTok is not used); Message Batches 50% discount on all token usage — used ONLY as a derived projection multiplier in A-F0, never measured; minimum cacheable prefix on Haiku 4.5 = 4096 tokens (shorter prefixes silently do not cache); caching is a strict prefix match over tools -> system -> messages and a cache entry becomes readable only after the first response begins streaming; thinking on Haiku 4.5 is the budgeted configuration only (type=enabled, budget_tokens >= 1024 and < max_tokens; the effort parameter is unsupported on Haiku 4.5; max_tokens=0 pre-warm requests are rejected when thinking is enabled). Source note: checked 2026-07-10 against the current Claude API reference bundle available to the design harness (models/pricing table cached 2026-06-24 plus the prompt-caching reference), byte-consistent with the N-IOC section-2.2 mechanics verified against live API docs 2026-07-09. The runner MUST re-verify this table at staging preflight against the live pricing/caching docs; any discrepancy is an ops amendment BEFORE first spend; raw usage fields are always logged so every dollar figure can be reconverted under a corrected table.
backing_ref: docs/next/io-compression-ideas.md section 2.2 (caching mechanics verified 2026-07-09); registry/experiments/a-f0-mint-economics.json; analysis/a_f0_mint_economics.py (embedded table)
rationale: The DVs need a deterministic usage->USD conversion fixed before any spend, because provider billing is not observable per call; pinning the table with a mandatory preflight re-verification makes every dollar figure reproducible from logged usage fields.

claim: A-F0 decision rule and margins (frozen): baseline = s1-G $0.078 API-equivalent per legal record (MEASURED, s1 report). An arm is LAWFUL iff it processed >= 55/60 concepts AND produced >= 5 legal records. PASS (mint economics SUSTAINED) iff the best lawful arm's measured usd_per_legal_record <= 0.75 x 0.078 = $0.05850 (at least a 25% improvement margin). FAIL (K-A1-mint fires: the cached-prefix definer is dead as an economics play) iff at least one lawful arm exists AND the minimum lawful usd_per_legal_record >= $0.078. Otherwise INCONCLUSIVE (improvement below margin, or no lawful arm). The 25% margin buys out the pipeline-switch cost of carrying a second pipeline identity plus the named baseline-comparability weaknesses (ASM-0607); the FAIL side deliberately requires NO improvement at all, so the kill cannot fire on margin noise.
rationale: usd_per_legal_record self-penalizes low-yield arms; the lawfulness floor stops a 1-2-record fluke from winning best-arm; the asymmetric PASS-margin / no-improvement-FAIL split keeps both branches decidable at n=60 with ~15 expected legal records per arm under the s1-measured 26% yield.

claim: A-F0 arms and fork split (frozen): the four N-IOC section-2.3 arms, all framework-G gate-in-the-loop over the cached-prefix Messages-API transport — (a) current G output form (JSON record + minimal frame), thinking off; (b) direct-symbolic: record only, compact serialization, zero prose, thinking off; (c) = b with thinking enabled budget_tokens=1024; (d) = a with the same thinking budget. All arms run the A-F2(b) SOURCE-FED configuration (target word + pinned fetched extracts in the variable suffix), the current pipeline's provenance-keeping form: it is the conservative/pessimistic configuration for cached-prefix economics (maximal uncached suffix) and matches the s1-G baseline's input shape for a like-for-like $/legal-record comparison. The A-F2(a) word-only arm and the Batch-vs-interactive delta are DEFERRED to A-E1 (N-IOC assigns A-F2 to A-F0/A-E1 jointly; A-F0 takes the output-form x thinking fork). Time-to-first-token is dropped from the section-2.3 DV list (batch minting is throughput-bound, not TTFT-bound); wall latency per call is kept. Repair policy pinned to the volume-runner form: at most ONE gate-error-fed repair call, only for gate-failing attempted drafts. Sampling parameters omitted (API defaults), one stochastic sample per concept per arm as in s1; no determinism claim.
backing_ref: docs/next/io-compression-ideas.md sections 2.2-2.3 (arms, fork A-F2, worked estimate); registry/experiments/a-f0-mint-economics.json
rationale: Keeps A-F0 the cheapest decisive instrument: 4 cells x 60 concepts x <= 2 calls fits the ~$5-10 clearance; the deferred forks either strictly lower cost (word-only suffix, Batch discount) or need pilot scale (A-E1), so deferring them cannot flip an A-F0 kill.

claim: A-F0 sample (frozen): n=60 English concepts, identical across all four arms — 45 seed-pinned stratified draws from data/haiku-tier/inventory/inventory.jsonl (15 per band A/B/C; deterministic derivation pinned in the staged sample manifest; seed string 'a-f0/1|sample|20260710'), EXCLUDING lemmas already minted (data/haiku-tier/minted-urns.jsonl) and REQUIRING source-fetch success at staging (a fetch failure replaces the item with the next seed-order candidate in the same band; every replacement logged); plus the 15 s1 hand-authored-overlap anchors verbatim (begin, afraid, believe, break, find, death, conversation; dog, water, red, eat, sleep, house, money, music). English-only: the English framework-G pipeline (closed grounding lexicon, gates.mjs validators, pinned ref catalog) is the ONLY executable fail-closed mint path today; inventing a non-English definer inside a cost experiment would be new instrument risk with unpinnable provenance. Cross-lingual mint cost is NOT measured by A-F0 (ASM-0606 carries the expectation; A-E1 owns the measurement).
rationale: Stratified bands plus the s1 anchor set keep definer difficulty comparable to the measured baseline and give the fidelity spot-check its anchors; excluding already-minted lemmas avoids self-reference collisions against the accumulated catalog carried in the cached prefix.

claim: A-F0 amortization protocol + cache-integrity instrument gate (frozen): concurrency 1; fixed arm order a -> d -> b -> c; two prefix groups by byte identity ({a,d} and {b,c} — the output-form instruction differs between groups so they cache separately; the thinking toggle does NOT invalidate the tools/system cache tier per the documented invalidation hierarchy, so arms d and c run against a warm cache); the first call of each prefix group is the cache-writing call (no pre-warm call is needed at concurrency 1, and max_tokens=0 pre-warms are rejected when thinking is enabled). Cold-start amortization curves are defined on arms a and b ONLY (cumulative mean usd/concept at k in {1,5,10,20,40,60}); steady-state marginal usd/concept (mean over each arm's last 20 concepts) is defined on all four arms; the warm start of d/c is a disclosed consequence of the fixed order, not a distortion of the steady-state estimand. TTL economics: the 5-minute cache is expected to stay warm at the s1-measured ~4-8 s/call cadence; any idle gap > TTL surfaces as a logged cache_creation event whose cost stays IN the measured totals. INSTRUMENT GATE cache_integrity: cache_read_input_tokens > 0 on >= 90% of calls after each prefix group's first call, AND both serialized prefixes measure >= 4096 tokens via count_tokens at staging — otherwise the run measured the WRONG MECHANISM (uncached economics) and the verdict is INSTRUMENT-INVALID, never FAIL/PASS.
rationale: The amortization curve is the deliverable that lets mint cost be paired against reuse, and it is only meaningful if the cache actually engaged: the usage-field gate is the standard silent-invalidator check, and the fixed order makes the cold/warm split explicit instead of accidental.

claim: Sizing expectation for the compression track, load-bearing for nothing: per-concept MINT cost measured on the English cached-prefix definer is expected to transfer to first order to cross-lingual definer configurations (the A-E2 fi/ja/es value cells), because per-concept cost is dominated by shared-prefix cache reads and output length rather than the target word's language; second-order risks run in both directions (higher BPE fertility of non-Latin surfaces raises the uncached suffix cost; a different lexicon/catalog changes prefix size). Unmeasured; never a premise for any gate, verdict, or spend decision.
backing_ref: registry/assessments/a-e2-census.json (fi/ja/es fertility concentration, MEASURED-exploratory); docs/next/io-compression-ideas.md section 2.2
resolution_path: A-E1 mint-economics pilot: include at least one non-English definitional cell once the A-F1 alignment design and a pinned non-English definer configuration exist; its measured per-concept cost supersedes this expectation.

claim: A-F0 baseline-comparability envelope (binding on any PASS/FAIL citation): the s1-G $0.078/legal-record baseline is API-EQUIVALENT accounting from claude-CLI reported usage (subscription transport, 2026-07-07, 108-id ref catalog, n=50); A-F0 measures REAL API-billed usage on the direct Messages API at the CURRENT accumulated catalog (mint-manifest: 2,673 minted urns) with n=60. Named non-identities: (1) transport + prompt serialization differ — that difference IS the treatment under test, not a confound to remove; (2) the grown catalog changes both prefix size (raises cached mass) and possibly gate-pass rate (direction unknown: more refs = more expressible AND more ways to violate); (3) yield estimates at n=50-60 carry binomial noise (roughly +/-11pp at 26%). Consequences: the ASM-0602 PASS margin exists partly to buy these out; the FAIL branch requires no-improvement and is therefore not margin-sensitive; and NO A-F0 number may be quoted as a re-measurement of the s1-G pipeline itself.
rationale: The comparison is decision-relevant — which pipeline mints going forward is exactly the live choice — but it is not a controlled experiment on transport alone; naming the confounders at freeze prevents post-hoc re-scoping of a PASS or FAIL.

claim: A-F0 verdict/adoption split (frozen): the MECHANICAL verdict (verdict-gen over the pinned analysis script) decides economics only (ASM-0602 rule + instrument gates). The N-IOC section-2.3 ADOPTION rule — which arm configures A-E1: argmin usd_per_legal_record subject to (i) gate_pass_attempted not more than 10pp below arm (a) and (ii) no fidelity regression on the 15 overlap anchors — is applied in the designer-side interpretive assessment AFTER the verdict, with fidelity judged on the s1 0/1/2 scale (single annotator, indicative; regression = mean anchor score more than 0.25 below the s1 draft-pass mean 1.47 on the same anchors, or any 0-scored record among the winner's anchor outputs). A fidelity regression VETOES adoption of a cheaper arm regardless of a PASS; the runner never judges fidelity (mechanical-verdict-only role per the execution split).
backing_ref: docs/next/io-compression-ideas.md section 2.3 (decision rule); data/haiku-tier/s1-experiments/s1-report.md sha256 238db0ddfaca614f30886afebfee219430062ed067c9f7d2ded83a489118e5b5 (fidelity scale + 1.47 mean); registry/experiments/a-f0-mint-economics.json
rationale: Keeps the frozen mechanical rule pure and decidable while preserving the full section-2.3 constraint set; fidelity judging is interpretation, which the execution split assigns to the design side, and making its placement explicit prevents the runner from being forced to improvise a judgment.

claim: A-F0 x A-E2 pairing frame (binding on every citation of the pair): pairing mint cost against selection-side savings is INTERPRETIVE and must be stated as — measured amortized mint cost per legal record (A-F0, real USD) versus the A-E2 MEMBERSHIP UPPER BOUND on prefill-token savings (MEASURED-exploratory; K-A4 mappability UNPRICED in every cell per ASM-0560; consumption channel unmeasured; listed-word denominator; the uniform-vs-usage-share weight-arm SPAN carried whole per ASM-0561). The pairing licenses ONLY sizing statements of the form 'at the measured mint price, a 10k-concept kernel-A costs $X all-in; A-E2's membership upper bound on blended prefill savings at that budget is Y-Z%' — never a net-positive claim, never achievable savings, never end-to-end viability. Any net-positive framing requires the consumption channel measured (A-E3/F3 territory) and a real K-A4 pricing first (ASM-0560).
rationale: A-F0 makes the cost side of the ledger real while the savings side stays an upper bound; fixing the only lawful pairing sentence at freeze prevents upper-bound savings from being silently netted against measured costs as if both were achievables.

claim: NLB post-outcome-registration disclosure (downgrades the outcome-blindness framing of ASM-0424/ASM-0480 for the l3a-parse/a5-nl pair): the 0847ce0 mapper-parse diagnostic disclosed scored-outcome information (the two FK-NLB-10 dropped families all abstained - unique-maker 43/43 refused, 0 wrong - and in-scope wrong = total wrong = 8) BEFORE the ASM-0480 enrichment ratification and the final scorer/record pins. Every scorer-side edit ratified after that commit (the ASM-0480 enrichment; the design-doc 14.8 round-2 hardening) is therefore POST-OUTCOME-DISCLOSED, and the pair's freeze is post-outcome registration with respect to those disclosed quantities. The previously claimed 'the same edit would be ratified under every possible outcome' counterfactual is WITHDRAWN as unenforceable and relied on nowhere. Lawfulness rests only on the disclosed terms: (i) the FK-NLB-10 carve-out and the enrichment schema requirement pre-date the diagnostic in the commit record (924bf6b before 0847ce0, independently confirmed by the round-2 skeptic); (ii) front-end, phrasing and eval bytes were pinned pre-diagnostic and are G7-enforced at analysis time; (iii) the pipeline is deterministic, so no selection-among-stochastic-outcomes channel exists; (iv) the independent skeptic re-attack gate and the maintainer veto remain the final control. Residual designer-knowledge risk is disclosed, not claimed zero.
rationale: The independent round-2 skeptic showed the outcome-invariance claim was an after-the-fact stipulation, not an enforceable blind constraint; overclaiming an unenforceable constraint would be worse than disclosing the deviation. The register carries the honest epistemic status so the verdict layer and every consumer of these records see post-outcome registration, its bounded channels, and its remaining controls. Revisit condition: none for this pair; any future record wanting outcome-blind scorer amendment must pre-commit its amendment rule BEFORE any diagnostic runs (new ASM).

claim: NLB skeptic-round-2 scorer/lint hardening (extends the ASM-0424 lawful scorer-side edit set by exactly this named, diff-scoped edit set and no other, executed under the ASM-0620 post-outcome disclosure): (1) G0 one-row-per-arm in both pinned analysis scripts - duplicate/retry rows never resolve by log order; a re-run row must EXPLICITLY supersede every row it replaces via a top-level supersedes:[sha256 of the replaced body's sorted-keys JSON]; more than one non-superseded row per arm, or a dangling supersede target, is INSTRUMENT-INVALID in both log orders; (2) G7 harness-pin enforcement - the instrument-emitted pins_observed (engine, kot_code on a5, nlb_instrument.py, nlb_frontend.py, nlb_map.mjs, the parents' corpus digests, the phrasing corpus files eval/dev/dev-entities/probe/manifest, the lint receipt, the kot-corpus-hash/1 recipe string) must equal frozen constants byte-copied from the records on EVERY arm body, fail-closed on a missing pins block or any drift; (3) G5 existence - the deranged-lexicon arm must EXIST with well-formed integer counts over the full planned covered set before collapse is tested; a missing arm, empty metrics or absent n_covered_exact is a broken instrument, never 'perfect collapse'; (4) full counts integrity in BOTH scorers - run-level covered partition (exact+wrong+refused_parse+refused_engine == 600 l3a / 855 a5), run-level control partition (answered+acceptable+other == 270/106, refused_any == acceptable+other, acceptable == strict+parse on the mapper arm), family-key set equality with unexpected families rejected, per-family bucket partition + exact == ok, covered bucket sums == run-level twins, zero covered buckets on control families, non-negative-integer buckets; (5) nlb_lint.py defaults to --arms-profile model, under which a nonempty waived_forced_substring list is itself a blocking finding (green=False); --arms-profile deterministic-r0 is the explicit attestation pinned for this non-model pair, and the committed receipts regenerate byte-identically under it, so all corpus digests are unchanged. All edits are analysis/lint side; no front-end artifact or phrasing byte changes; no endpoint, threshold, or planned n moves.
backing_ref: docs/design-nl-boundary-l3a-parse-a5-nl.md section 14.8 items 3-6 and 11; poc/nlb-skeptic/skeptic-output.txt findings 3-6 and 11 (findings 3, 5 and part of 6 empirically reproduced); analysis/l3a_parse.py + analysis/a5_nl.py --selftest fixtures covering each failure mode including the reproduced cases
rationale: The reproduced failures showed the previous scorers could admit order-dependent duplicate outcomes, treat an absent scramble control as perfect collapse, and accept mutually inconsistent count claims, while the emitted harness pins were prose rather than gates; each hardening converts a claimed invariant of design-doc sections 6.3/14.7 into an executable fail-closed check with a selftest fixture on the exact reproduced case. Revisit condition: a successor record with a model-based arm must re-derive its own lint-profile decision (the deterministic-r0 attestation is unlawful there per ASM-0423).

claim: m0a-llmproxy judged sense surfaces: the 65 canonical one-line prime exponent glosses (poc/m0a-llmproxy/prime-glosses.json sha256 fdb004ccb495fd913c89bfe7262d3389d0d86276ff52f6657509735540cb2013) and the 119-entry none-stratum inventory rendering (poc/m0a-llmproxy/none-inventory.txt sha256 4d3374b5d73daebd033cbe87711b679ed9951846cd6ff242816aec3324786c31: 54 concept lemmas alphabetical-by-slug with the record gloss verbatim, then the 65 prime exponent lists in chart order with the authored glosses) are authored at staging as the judged sense surface for prime-stratum items and the none stratum; they are assumed to faithfully express each target's sense in one plain line — a misauthored gloss moves proxy P/R independent of mapper quality. No such canonical prime gloss surface existed before this staging (the profile-1 lexicon carries names/exponents only).
backing_ref: registry/experiments/m0a-llmproxy.json pins.artifact_hashes; poc/m0a-llmproxy/judge-invocation.md section 2; encoder/src/lexicon.ts + mapper/src/primes.ts (names/exponents mirrored)
rationale: The design (poc/m0a-llmproxy/design.md S2) pins gloss-rendered senses without an existing prime gloss artifact; authoring them once, sha-pinning them, and reusing the identical bytes for mapped items, probes and the inventory keeps the judged surface single-sourced and auditable; the eventual human pass on the same items bounds any gloss-infidelity effect jointly with the other proxy gaps.

claim: m0a-llmproxy rendering operationalisation: the marked token is rendered inline as contextBefore + '[[' + surface + ']]' + contextAfter with a template note that fragments may start/end mid-word; the judging criteria carried on the sheets are the stated M0a criteria (mapper/m0/make-agent-judgments.py docstring) with exactly the blind renaming required by the design ('the sense the mapped kernel target denotes' rendered as 'the proposed sense'; no urns, no stratum names, no mention of a mapping system); abstain answers are candidate numbers over the recorded candidate order; none answers are inventory entry numbers.
backing_ref: registry/experiments/m0a-llmproxy.json pins.artifact_hashes (prompt templates + schemas); poc/m0a-llmproxy/judge-invocation.md sections 2 and 5
rationale: The design's rendering contract (ASM-0541) fixes WHAT the judge may see; these are the byte-level choices that make two faithful runners produce identical prompts, staged and sha-pinned before freeze so no rendering degree of freedom survives into the run.

claim: g3-llmproxy rendering operationalisation: the pass-A sheet's underlining is rendered as an explicit 'underlined word: <word>' line, <word> = the instance's concept_id slug after the last colon with '-' as space (pass A may name the concept word — the human pass-A sheet shows it inside the target claim; the urn string itself never renders); pass-B bindings render one '- key = value' line per recorded key in recorded order and conditions one '- cid: text' line per recorded condition, with concept_id/concept_label/frame/gloss_verbatim withheld; both judges receive byte-identical user prompts, with judge-pA constrained by server-side schemas and judge-pB by the two format-only system prompts.
backing_ref: registry/experiments/g3-llmproxy.json pins.artifact_hashes (templates, schemas, system prompts); poc/g3-llmproxy/judge-invocation.md section 2; data/instance-descriptions/annotation-protocol.md (instructions carried verbatim)
rationale: The frozen protocol's sheet affordances (typographic underlining, two-column sheets) do not exist in a single-item text prompt; these pinned equivalents preserve the protocol's information exposure exactly (pass A shows the word, pass B withholds it) while making the rendering mechanical and byte-reproducible.

claim: g9-llmproxy rendering operationalisation: the per-sheet prompt renders exactly the five design-enumerated fields (word, def, examples, ambig_examples, candidate_explication as 'proposed paraphrase'); sheet_id and the syn synset field are WITHHELD (syn is a dictionary-source cue outside the design's field list); the template carries a fixed reading-guidance paragraph (tiny-vocabulary register, X/Y participant letters, normally/typically reading) and a cannot-say-only-if-genuinely-undecidable line; both questions are asked in ONE call under one two-field output schema.
backing_ref: registry/experiments/g9-llmproxy.json pins.artifact_hashes (prompt-template.txt + output-schema.json); poc/g9-llmproxy/judge-invocation.md section 2; poc/g9-llmproxy/design.md S2
rationale: These are the byte-level choices the design left to staging: withholding syn removes a source cue the human reviewer also does not need; the guidance paragraph mirrors the f2b judge-1p precedent so odd NSM-register wording is not scored as content failure; single-call two-field output keeps the two legs of the composite from drifting across separate samples.

claim: A-F0 keyless-channel adjudication (designer ruling on the maintainer's run-mechanism question, 2026-07-10; binds execution; amends NOTHING in the frozen record): the headless claude-CLI subscription channel (claude -p --model claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 --output-format stream-json, fresh --no-session-persistence process per call) is INVALID as an A-F0 transport, and no ops amendment can port A-F0 onto it. Grounds: (1) the pinned call configuration IS the mechanism under test and is not instantiable through the CLI wrapper — the CLI owns request serialization, tool-block composition, dynamic system-prompt sections (per-machine cwd/env/git-status content sits in the system prompt by default; the exclude flag only relocates it into the first user message), and cache_control breakpoint placement, none observable or byte-pinnable from outside, and it exposes no per-call control of thinking budget_tokens (arms c/d) or max_tokens (the --effort flag is not budgeted thinking and effort is unsupported on Haiku 4.5 per ASM-0601); (2) the amortization endpoint (sec-amortization, H-AF0-AMORT) is unidentifiable on that channel — usage cache_creation/cache_read fields aggregate the whole request, so definitional-prefix reuse cannot be separated from the CLI's own wrapper-prefix caching; (3) the cache_integrity instrument gate goes vacuous there (wrapper-prefix cache reads alone can satisfy the >= 90% cache-read-positive floor while the definitional prefix re-bills uncached on every call — the exact wrong-mechanism failure the gate exists to catch), and the prefix_min_cacheable gate requires /v1/messages/count_tokens, which the keyless channel does not expose; (4) the subscription channel yields API-EQUIVALENT projections, not API-billed dollars — pricing its usage fields by the ASM-0601 table is the s1 accounting basis, not a Batch-style derived multiplier on the mechanism's own measured usage, and it erases A-F0's registered purpose of making the cost side of the ledger real; (5) running through the CLI reverts the pipeline identity under test (ASM-0600, no-CLI-fallback clause) and collapses the registered treatment — transport + serialization differences ARE the treatment per ASM-0607 — into a CLI-variant-vs-CLI-variant comparison the frozen ASM-0602 thresholds were never sized for. Consequences: the S7 API-key hard precondition STANDS; absent key remains a BOUNDARY STOP to the maintainer; any keyless CLI-transport fork study (a-vs-b output form only; thinking arms not instantiable) would be a NEW experiment record with its own thresholds and envelope, never an A-F0 amendment, and nothing in this ruling creates one.
backing_ref: docs/next/a-f0-keyless-channel-adjudication.md; registry/experiments/a-f0-mint-economics.json (frozen_sha256 fb4147ccfa6f88936effa991616927317f6aaed98eb24b38e11984b334bf5dfc); docs/next/a-f0-mint-economics-spec.md S2/S5/S7; ASM-0600; ASM-0601; ASM-0605; ASM-0607
rationale: The freeze names the direct Messages API as the pipeline identity under test and forbids CLI fallback precisely because the CLI silently reverts that identity; the keyless channel additionally blinds both cache-side instruments and the billing basis, so an amended A-F0 on that channel would quote costs of a mechanism it never ran.
