claim: Programme-3 work-breakdown structure (process decision): Phase-0 = six literature reviews (P3-LR-NSA neurosymbolic survey, P3-LR-GNN GNN-LLM fusion, P3-LR-RULE rule-injection into transformers, P3-LR-COMP compression/dimension-pruning/interp-guided pruning, P3-LR-EVAL evaluation + matched-size/compute methodology, P3-LR-TINY tiny-model training and small-scale scaling laws) plus one direct-design task P3-MF-0 (measurement + fair-comparison framework) as the ONLY top-level beads; each Phase-0 bead names and spawns its Phase-1 architecture-design beads on completion; each Phase-1 design bead spawns its Phase-2 pre-registered experiment beads. Nothing in Programme-3 is frozen, scheduled, or run by the design document itself.
rationale: The maintainer explicitly mandated a phased hierarchical breakdown with lit-reviews first so each architecture bet is grounded in prior art before design spend, mirroring the discipline that produced reports/lit-llm-injection-priorart.md before the L0-L4 ladder.
notes: The coordinator beads this structure verbatim from section 5 of the design doc.

claim: Programme-3 inherited-constraint set (design constraints adopted from Programme-1/2 measured negatives, binding on every Programme-3 design): (1) NO real-input or natural-language usefulness claim may be made by any Programme-3 experiment unless its NL front-end has cleared an FK-NLB-3-class gate on the relevant vertical (>=0.90 retention of gold-parse covered exactness AND the S2 dangerous-wrong fail-closed gate), because both measured NL crossings failed (l3a-parse 47.6 percent safe-FAIL, a5-nl 41.6 percent dangerous-FAIL); until then all claims are formal-input-scoped. (2) Every kernel-contribution claim carries the deranged-store and aligned-non-kernel-store controls (the f2b alignment confound). (3) Every coverage number is quoted corpus/rung/kernel-state-indexed only. (4) Compression/membership figures are upper bounds, never achievable savings, until a consumption channel is measured.
rationale: These are the four failure patterns the feasibility synthesis identified as the walls the previous programme hit (NL boundary, alignment confound, coverage-index stripping, upper-bound inflation); adopting them as hard design constraints is the mechanism by which Programme-3 does not repeat them.
notes: Constraint (1) makes the FK-NLB-3 parser re-entry a shared-infrastructure dependency of Programme-3, not an optional extra.

claim: Programme-3 revision-2 north-star operationalisation (design choice, post-GPT-5.6 review; supersedes-in-part ASM-0800 items (2)-(4)). (1) W1 is a RESOURCE-CONSTRAINED FRONTIER claim, not a +/-10 percent band claim: at rung R-k a pre-registered budget vector B_k = (max canonically-packed deployment bytes; max p95 latency; max energy/query; max peak accelerator memory + host RSS) is fixed; S must (a) fit within EVERY component of B_k (energy explicitly binds, fixing the revision-1 W1/KOT-COST inconsistency), and (b) exceed EVERY pre-registered, reproducible open comparator and baseline family that fits within every component of B_k - comparators may be smaller or cheaper than S - by margin delta_k on the simultaneous one-sided 95 percent lower confidence bound (per ASM-0813). The full neural/neural-retrieval staircase/Pareto envelope across budgets is published with S plotted against it. The claim a W1 PASS licenses is verbatim 'S exceeds every pre-registered, reproducible open comparator and baseline family searched under budget B_k by >= delta_k on KOT-AI-INDEX/2-vN'; the untestable universal 'higher than all other models of the same size and compute' is retired from every claim surface. (2) 'size' := KOT-SIZE/2, six pinned figures with figure (1) primary: canonically-packed minimally-sufficient artifact bytes (pinned deterministic packing script); compressed distribution bytes (zstd-19); warm resident RAM/VRAM; cold-start working set + bytes read to first answer; index/store construction size + cost; any remote service/cache/corpus dependency (remote bytes count or the smaller-deployment claim is void). The common base image is FROZEN (content-hashed) before any architecture development; anything beyond it counts as the system's bytes. (3) 'inference cost' := KOT-COST/2, a measured RESOURCE VECTOR on pinned hardware under the P3-D-HW protocol: accelerator time + estimated ops; CPU-seconds; bytes read from storage and network; energy (RAPL/NVML, whole-run integration); peak accelerator memory AND host RSS; p50/p95 end-to-end latency; throughput under a pinned concurrency distribution; warm AND cold cache incl. startup/index-load; TTFT and inter-token latency for generative arms; output length controlled or cost-normalised per emitted token. The analytic neural-FLOP ledger is renamed the neural-FLOP DIAGNOSTIC: always reported, never binding, never citable alone as 'compute-matched' (symbolic work enters it at zero, so it is not a compute measure). Methodology borrows MLPerf inference + power discipline (REVIEW-CITED, verified by P3-LR-SYS before freeze). (4) KOT-LIFE/1 lifecycle ledger co-reported with every W1 claim: donor pretraining provenance/compute where known; architecture fine-tune + hyperparameter-search compute actually spent; store authoring/parsing/embedding/indexing/human-review costs; total cost of ownership amortised at 10^3/10^6/10^9 queries (REVIEW-CITED: Sardana et al., verified by P3-LR-SYS). The ledger does not gate W1 numerically (no non-arbitrary common unit) but feeds kill K-P3v2(6). W2 unchanged in objective; its R-2 sweep is conditional on a W1 PASS at R-2 (ASM-0817).
backing_ref: docs/next/programme-3-neurosymbolic-architecture.md sections 1.2-1.4, 2.1; poc/gpt56-review/p3-review-20260711/review.md section 1 + ranked concern 1 + technical error 7
rationale: The external GPT-5.6 review demonstrated the revision-1 +/-10 percent bands are gameable (padding into a favourable band, dodging a stronger model just outside it, empty band intersections, losing to a smaller/cheaper dominator), that the neural-FLOP ledger is not a compute measure, that W1 omitted energy while KOT-COST/1 claimed it, that raw bytes without canonical packing are a gaming surface, and that deployment-efficiency comparisons must be lifecycle-priced to be named accurately. Each is accepted and mechanised.
notes: Supersedes-in-part ASM-0800 (its items (2) size, (3) cost, (4) W1 band construction); ASM-0800 item (1) index is superseded-in-part by ASM-0811. Registry is append-only; ASM-0800 line is not edited. Any change to the budget vector composition, packing script, resource-vector membership, or ledger template is a KOT-FAIR version change that re-baselines every comparison.

claim: KOT-AI-INDEX/2 headline index design (design choice; supersedes-in-part ASM-0800 item (1)). (1) Per-benchmark scores are normalised against chance floor and a pinned ceiling (s_norm = (s - chance)/(ceiling - chance), ceiling pinned at index freeze); normalised scores macro-average first WITHIN capability domains, then across domains (HELM-like multi-scenario structure, REVIEW-CITED, verified by P3-LR-EVAL); every publication reports the domain vector AND the scalar. (2) Provisional domains: D1 linguistic competence (held-out LM loss + BLiMP + EWoK at R-0+); D2 commonsense/world (reduced MC set); D3 knowledge/factual; D4 relational/rule reasoning = the neurosymbolic diagnostics (procedural suites at R-0; CLUTRR, ProofWriter/RuleTaker, FOLIO at R-1+ with held-out rules, held-out relation compositions, held-out proof depths, lexical paraphrase splits; REVIEW-CITED: CLUTRR arXiv:1908.06177); D5 math/procedural (GSM8K; BBH/BBEH at R-3+); D6 instruction following (IFEval where above floor); D7 code at R-3+ (pinned code benchmark + a frozen, versioned LiveBench release - each refresh a new version, never silently folded in); S sealed secondary per ASM-0812. Final membership, floors and ceilings fixed by P3-D-INDEX after P3-LR-EVAL. (3) MMLU-cloze is DROPPED as a stable cross-rung component (the repo's own benchmark notes record inconsistent SmolLM2 MMLU variants); MMLU-family enters only at R-3+ as MMLU-Pro under the pinned harness. (4) The revision-1 nine-task suite is retained verbatim as KOT-SMOL-CONT/1, the 'SmolLM2 continuity index' - for host-family anchor continuity and harness calibration (P3-E-CAL) only; never the headline, never in W1. (5) Standing rules carried over: full vector (per-benchmark scores, refusal/abstention rates, covered-vs-uncovered split) always one link from any scalar; index items consumed as natural benchmark text, no hand-formalised inputs, parser inside the measured system.
backing_ref: docs/next/programme-3-neurosymbolic-architecture.md section 1.1; poc/gpt56-review/p3-review-20260711/review.md section 'The AI index' + ranked concern 2
rationale: The review showed the revision-1 index was seven correlated MC commonsense/factual tasks raw-averaged across different chance floors and variances - fine as a SmolLM2 continuity instrument, not defensible as a headline capability index - and named the missing constructs (linguistic competence, relational/rule generalisation, instruction following, calibration) and the normalisation errors. Accepted in full; the continuity suite is kept for exactly the purpose it is valid for.
notes: Supersedes-in-part ASM-0800(1). Domain membership changes are index VERSION changes. CLUTRR/HELM/LiveBench/OLLv2 citations are REVIEW-CITED until P3-LR-EVAL verifies each at source; none is load-bearing for a premise.

claim: Programme-3 revision-2 fair-comparison protocol (design choice; supersedes-in-part ASM-0801). (1) KOT-FAIR/2 opens with a formal resource/baseline THREAT MODEL (P3-D-THREAT): enumerated gaming channels (packaging, band/frontier, cost, index, store, tuning games) x mechanised counters; a channel not counterable at freeze is a documented standing limitation on every claim it touches. (2) FACTORIAL control design spanning content-type x retrieval-architecture x executor, replacing the derangement-centric list (derangement changes alignment and retrieval simultaneously and cannot localise): seed-pinned deranged addressing; label permutation; irrelevant-but-structurally-matched records; edge/relation shuffling for graph arms; aligned-non-kernel store; matched generic-RAG/tool-use control; kernel-as-text null at matched token budget. Each prereg names its cells and what each discriminates. (3) The MATCHED GENERIC NEURAL-RAG / TOOL-USE control is mandatory for any W1 claim: same corpus, same retriever/index, same resource budgets, conventional neural RAG or tool-use architecture - the aligned-non-kernel store does NOT replace it. Standing six-way attribution factorisation: kernel semantics vs structured storage vs retrieval vs deterministic execution vs retry/search vs neural-symbolic integration. (4) Comparator construction by a standing NEURAL FRONTIER-BUILDER (P3-D-FRONTIER): per budget, strongest open models at-or-below budget (pinned name+revision at freeze) + best-effort quantisation/structured-pruning/distillation + neural-retrieval variants + OPTIMISED/ADAPTIVE test-time compute (best-of-k self-consistency alone is too weak; REVIEW-CITED: ICLR-2025 test-time-compute result, verified by P3-LR-RAG) + learned/neural verifiers where budget permits; training-compute-matched from-scratch twins only where the neurosymbolic arm trains from scratch. (5) Tuning symmetry binds on TOTAL tuning compute actually spent (logged into KOT-LIFE/1) with a fixed pre-registered selection rule on a dev split disjoint from the index suite - equal config counts are insufficient. (6) SEALED EVALUATION mandatory for any W1 claim: a secondary suite produced AFTER architecture+store freeze by an independent party or pinned procedural generator, unseen by developing agents, versioned per release; for store-based systems it includes temporally-fresh facts and/or independently held-out domains; a pre-registered frozen-vs-sealed consistency band; a large gap voids the win pending diagnosis. Decontamination screening (n-gram + embedding + human spot-check, win-voiding) remains mandatory but is acknowledged NECESSARY-NOT-SUFFICIENT (misses abstraction-level leakage, error-analysis-driven authoring, sub-threshold paraphrases, donor-weight contamination, researcher adaptation).
backing_ref: docs/next/programme-3-neurosymbolic-architecture.md sections 2.0-2.3; poc/gpt56-review/p3-review-20260711/review.md sections 'Baselines need strengthening' + 'Decontamination is necessary but cannot certify fairness' + ranked concern 5
rationale: The review identified the strongest missing control (matched generic RAG over the same corpus/retriever/budgets), showed derangement alone cannot localise what mattered, showed config-count tuning symmetry is not compute symmetry, and showed decontamination screening cannot certify fairness. All accepted and mechanised; the threat model makes the gaming-channel analysis itself a frozen auditable artifact.
notes: Supersedes-in-part ASM-0801 (baseline ladder and controls list). The residual unresolved unfairness channels (closed-corpus contamination, lifecycle incommensurability, baseline-search completeness, residual packaging gaming) are standing limitations stated on every W1 claim, per the corrected section 7.3.

claim: Programme-3 statistical house rules (design choice, correcting two revision-1 errors flagged by the external review). (1) A margin-delta superiority claim requires the one-sided 95 percent LOWER CONFIDENCE BOUND on the difference itself to clear the margin: LCB95(INDEX(S) - INDEX(B)) > delta_k. A point estimate above delta with an LCB merely above zero is NOT a margin win and is never reported as one. (2) Multiplicity: with multiple comparators (and domains), simultaneous confidence bounds / family-wise error control across the pre-registered comparator set; the FWER procedure is pinned in the frozen analysis plan. (3) Resampling: hierarchical bootstrap across benchmark families and items, preserving paired per-item predictions. (4) Non-inferiority vs equivalence: a 'retains capability' claim is NON-INFERIORITY and uses a single one-sided test / one-sided CI against the pre-registered non-inferiority margin; TOST (two one-sided tests) is an EQUIVALENCE procedure reserved for genuine equivalence claims. All revision-1 'TOST non-inferiority' usages (including the H-DD central claim) are corrected accordingly. (5) Carried over: one primary endpoint per experiment; kill criteria verbatim in the prereg; mechanical verdict generation + cross-vendor audit.
backing_ref: docs/next/programme-3-neurosymbolic-architecture.md section 2.5; poc/gpt56-review/p3-review-20260711/review.md section 'Statistical specification needs correction' + technical errors 1 and 2
rationale: The review is simply correct on both points: the revision-1 W1 statistics (point above delta, LCB above zero) do not establish a margin-delta win, and TOST was mislabelled as a non-inferiority procedure. Corrected before anything freezes.
notes: Binding on every Programme-3 prereg. No experiment has been run under the erroneous specification; nothing needs re-analysis.

claim: NL-boundary DEPENDENCY rule (design constraint; strengthens ASM-0808 item (1) from a claims rule to a dependency rule, per the review's ranked concern 3). Every Programme-3 experiment stage whose store is ADDRESSED FROM NATURAL INPUT - verifier-loop NL legs, GNN retrieval from NL queries, KV rule-firing keyed from NL, constrained decoding over NL prompts, H-DD store reinjection on NL tasks, H-PS end-to-end runs - is BLOCKED by the NLB gate (P3-E-NLB-1 clearing 0.90 retention + the S2 dangerous-wrong fail-closed criterion per vertical), OR runs on ORACLE inputs (gold parses / gold subgraphs / gold record addresses) and is labelled 'oracle-diagnostic' in its prereg, licensing NO W1 claim and no real-input claim of any kind. Additionally: (a) P3-D-NLB is itself blocked by the semantic-parsing / program-synthesis / selective-prediction literature review P3-LR-PARSE - the measured parser failure (l3a-parse 47.6 percent retention; a5-nl 41.6 percent + S2 kill fired) is not to be re-attempted with the same approach blind; (b) P3-E-NLB-1, coverage censuses, and G1 computations do NOT wait for index calibration (P3-E-CAL) - they make no index claim and gate everything else.
backing_ref: docs/next/programme-3-neurosymbolic-architecture.md sections 0, 3.3a, 4, 5; poc/gpt56-review/p3-review-20260711/review.md sections 'Honesty and programme risks' (NL wall not encoded in dependencies) + section 3 (sequencing) + ranked concern 3
rationale: The review showed revision 1 acknowledged the NL wall rhetorically but let GNN retrieval, KV rules, constrained decoding, and H-DD reinjection reach Phase-2 without demonstrated query-to-symbol grounding. The wall is now encoded as blocking edges in the WBS, with the oracle-diagnostic label as the only alternative path, and the parser redesign is lit-review-gated so the measured failure is not repeated.
notes: MEASURED basis restated within envelopes: l3a-parse FAIL (safe) and a5-nl FAIL (dangerous, S2 fired). The N=2 redesign-cycle bound on NLB attempts lives in K-P3v2(2) (ASM-0818).

claim: Design-space amendments (scoping decision; extends ASM-0802). (1) NEW FAMILY H-PS, NL-to-program-synthesis, first-class: parse natural input into a small executable DSL (the kernel's closed grammar or a thin typed layer over it); execute deterministically on the microsecond engine; generate the surface answer FROM the checked result; abstain with CALIBRATED confidence when parse confidence is low (selective prediction; the abstention policy is part of the measured product). Designed by P3-D-PS, blocked by P3-LR-PARSE + P3-LR-NTP; its front-end, if it clears NLB, becomes the shared front-end. Evidence status: cautioned - l3a-parse/a5-nl are its measured-FAIL prior art, which is exactly why it is lit-review-gated. (2) H-RULE corrections: constrained decoding is TRANSPORT, not inference - the engine must have already derived the valid continuation set (H-RULE-CD = engine-derived continuation sets delivered via masking; without the executor it is only an output grammar); KV/bottleneck provenance claims require causal ablation, intervention, or executor traces - attention maps and named units do NOT qualify; the family endpoint vs H-VL is PARETO-FRONTIER dominance over (covered-slice accuracy, coverage reached, p95 latency variance, resource vector), not cost parity alone. (3) H-GNN falsifier rescoped: 'fusion <= text-of-graph on tiny graphs' does NOT kill the family (text is often superior for tiny graphs); the kill is a flat-or-text-favouring curve across a pre-registered subgraph-size x reasoning-depth sweep at matched information and resource budgets, on held-out relation compositions and proof depths; controls = nodes-without-edges, shuffled edges/labels, MLP/DeepSets/plain-Transformer encoders on the same inputs, matched text serialisation, oracle-vs-NL-extracted subgraph (NL leg NLB-gated); four separately instrumented stages: retrieval, graph construction, graph encoding, LM use; endpoint is causal behavioural improvement, never delivery alone. (4) H-GU protocol: >= 3 widths or token budgets; the pre-registered read is the DIRECTION of the interface effect across the sweep (shrinking-to-zero or flat-negative kills; growing-with-scale promotes even from a negative base); variants in scope, each a separate arm: proof-trace distillation, verifier-guided training, synthetic curriculum over rule depth + paraphrase diversity, learned routing, conditional-compute/MoE with a symbolic expert.
backing_ref: docs/next/programme-3-neurosymbolic-architecture.md sections 3.2, 3.3, 3.3a, 3.5; poc/gpt56-review/p3-review-20260711/review.md section 2 'Architecture bets' + technical errors 6 and 8
rationale: Adopts the review's per-family corrections: the missing program-synthesis design attacks the measured NL wall directly instead of hoping rules emerge in a hidden layer; the CD-as-inference and attention-as-provenance errors are corrected; the GNN and ground-up falsifiers are rescoped so cheap negatives do not over-kill families whose value is scale- or depth-dependent.
notes: Extends ASM-0802's enumeration; the placement-test ordering CD -> KV -> {AD, ACT} -> HL stands. ASM-0805/0806 resolvers are rescoped accordingly (sweep-based kill; Pareto endpoint) without editing those append-only entries.

claim: H-DD reformulation (design choice; amends the RESOLUTION PATH of the EXTRAPOLATION ASM-0804 without altering its registered hypothesis text; answers review ranked concern 4 + technical errors 3, 4, 5, 6). (1) INVARIANCE: arbitrary per-layer orthogonal rotation is NOT generally function-preserving through nonlinearities, normalisation, residual connections, attention-head structure, and tied embeddings; P3-D-DD's first deliverable is the derived EXACT invariance group of each chosen donor architecture (which basis changes are absorbable, where, with proofs or numerical-tolerance verification per site); every normalisation step is restricted to that group. (2) GEOMETRY: SAE/dictionary features are overcomplete and non-orthogonal; matching them to kernel concepts does NOT yield a removable orthogonal coordinate block; the alignment step is reframed as identifying a concept-associated subspace or parameter set whose causal role is TESTED, with the identification method an experimental variable (mapper caution applies: proxy precision ~0.71, m0a-llmproxy, weak proxy). (3) PARAMETERISATION: dropping 80 percent of hidden width is NOT a 5x smaller dense transformer (attention/MLP matrices scale ~quadratically with width - up to ~25x on those blocks - embeddings ~linearly); every size claim carries an explicit parameterisation (which matrices shrink how, at what precision) AND is verified as measured KOT-SIZE/2 figure-(1) bytes of the packed artifact, never inferred from a width fraction; where the mechanism is rank or parameter pruning it is named as such; the maintainer's 5x target is restated as a BYTES target at matched index retention. (4) PROVENANCE: attention weights are not causal provenance; all H-DD attribution uses ablation/patching. (5) CAUSAL GATE P3-E-DD-0 runs BEFORE any arm experiment: identify the purported concept subspace/parameter set -> ablate/patch on concept-relevant vs irrelevant tasks (effect must be selective) -> test stability across layers, prompts, donors (SmolLM2-135M + Pythia-160M) -> show store reinjection SPECIFICALLY restores the ablated behaviour (NL reinjection NLB-gated per ASM-0814; oracle-addressed = oracle-diagnostic) -> only then structural pruning + distillation. (6) COMPARATORS: the arm experiment P3-E-DD-1 compares against the strongest published-practice RETRAINED structured-pruning + knowledge-distillation baselines at equal bytes and equal recovery compute (pruning-without-recovery was too weak); primary endpoint is non-inferiority via single one-sided CI per ASM-0813(4) - not TOST - plus superiority over both the random-subspace and retrained-generic-pruning arms; (i) ~ (iii)/(iv) kills the distinctive claim even if compression succeeds generically.
backing_ref: docs/next/programme-3-neurosymbolic-architecture.md section 3.5; poc/gpt56-review/p3-review-20260711/review.md section 2 'Dimension drop' + ranked concern 4 + technical errors 3-6
rationale: The review identified four technical errors in the revision-1 H-DD formulation (rotation invariance, SAE orthogonality, width-to-size arithmetic, attention provenance). Each is acknowledged as an error and corrected; the cheap causal gate ensures no pruning run happens on an untested causal story; the retrained comparator ensures a win cannot be manufactured against a strawman.
notes: ASM-0804 remains the registered EXTRAPOLATION and remains never-a-premise; this entry changes only how it gets resolved. Registry is append-only; ASM-0804 is not edited.

claim: Programme-3 gate ladder (design choice; supersedes-in-part ASM-0803's rung-only progression). Every family progresses through five INFORMATION-GAINING GATES, cheapest first, so failures localise: G1 coverage-ceiling - compute Delta_max <= sum_b w_b * kappa_b * (1 - a_b_cov) (w_b = index weight, kappa_b = store-covered fraction of benchmark b's items, a_b_cov = baseline accuracy on covered items); if even perfect covered-item correction cannot reach delta_k the index-mover claim is killed on paper, no GPU (mandatory first step for H-VL, H-RULE, and store-side H-DD; computed in P3-D-POWER). G2 oracle-interface - with gold parses/subgraphs/record addresses, does the mechanism help at all (oracle-diagnostic label, no W1 claim, per ASM-0814); a G2 fail localises to the mechanism. G3 boundary-degradation - how much of G2 survives realistic NL mapping through the NLB-gated front-end, with the P3-D-ORACLE decomposition attributing the loss across parse / retrieval-addressing / execution / generation; a G3 fail localises to the boundary. G4 full-system-vs-frontier - the W1 attempt against F(B_k) with the full framework + sealed eval. G5 scaling - the W1 margin held or improved across >= 2 sizes. Compute rungs R-0..R-4 remain as compute HOMES; promotion requires the current gate's pre-registered primary; a family failing the SAME gate at two consecutive rungs (or failing G1/G2 outright) is closed at scope. SEQUENCING: P3-E-NLB-1, coverage censuses, and G1 computations do not wait for P3-E-CAL; every G4 experiment requires the frozen KOT-FAIR/2 + P3-E-CAL PASS + the G4 block (P3-D-THREAT, P3-D-BASE, P3-D-FRONTIER, P3-D-HW, P3-D-RAGC). W2-CONDITIONALITY (fixing the review-flagged inconsistency): the W2 Pareto sweep inside P3-E-SCALE-2 runs ONLY conditional on W1 passing at R-2; any compression curve produced before a W1 PASS is labelled DIAGNOSTIC, never W2.
backing_ref: docs/next/programme-3-neurosymbolic-architecture.md sections 1.4, 3.1, 4; poc/gpt56-review/p3-review-20260711/review.md section 3 'Scaling ladder and cost approach'
rationale: The review showed the rung-only ladder lets an architecture fail ambiguously (graph encoder vs retrieval vs NL parse) and that the coverage bound is computable without GPU spend; the gate structure kills failures earlier, localises them, and encodes the review's sequencing corrections (NLB/coverage independent of calibration; W2 conditional on W1).
notes: Supersedes-in-part ASM-0803 (rung discipline and W2 timing); the rung table, compute homes, and promotion-spend gating in ASM-0803 stand. Given measured external coverage (~0 on external censuses; m0b 0.3542 friendliest-corpus), G1 is expected to bind hard and early - that is its purpose.

claim: Programme-level kill K-P3v2 (design choice; supersedes-in-part ASM-0803's K-P3, replacing its subjective escape hatch and soft default). The competitiveness programme is KILLED if ANY of six pre-registered quantitative conditions holds at its named checkpoint: (1) COVERAGE CEILING - after the P3-D-POWER analysis including any funded coverage-growth ingestion wave completed by the R-1 checkpoint, Delta_max < delta_1 for EVERY store-dependent family on the frozen R-1 suite. (2) NL BOUNDARY - P3-E-NLB-1 and its redesigns fail 0.90 retention OR the S2 fail-closed safety gate on BOTH verticals after N = 2 pre-registered redesign cycles beyond the first attempt. (3) FRONTIER - no family's G4 result exceeds the neural/RAG frontier F(B_k) at BOTH R-1 and R-2 (W1 fails everywhere through R-2 with frontier comparators properly built). (4) ATTRIBUTION COLLAPSE - every measured family gain vanishes against the matched generic-store / generic-RAG controls. (5) BOUNDARY NON-SURVIVAL - every family's G2 gain fails to survive G3 at the pre-registered retention margin across the N = 2 NLB redesign cycles. (6) LIFECYCLE DOMINATION - the KOT-LIFE/1 ledger shows S dominated in total cost of ownership by a frontier baseline at ALL THREE pinned amortisation volumes (10^3/10^6/10^9 queries) for every family that passed G4. DEFAULT ON KILL: the competitiveness programme TERMINATES; restarting requires a NEW proposal with new evidence or a new mechanism class - not an open-ended maintainer fork. A maintainer overrule is an explicit new on-record decision, not this design's escape hatch. Surviving single mechanisms are handed back as SCOPED instruments with their envelopes, not as a continued competitiveness claim.
backing_ref: docs/next/programme-3-neurosymbolic-architecture.md section 7.1; poc/gpt56-review/p3-review-20260711/review.md section 5 'The programme-level kill is too soft and too subjective'
rationale: The revision-1 kill turned on analysts' attribution judgement ('misses due to fundamentals rather than instrument failure') - a wide subjective escape hatch - and defaulted to an open-ended fork. The review's six quantitative conditions are adopted verbatim in substance, with N=2 pinned for the NLB redesign bound and termination as the default.
notes: Supersedes-in-part ASM-0803's K-P3. Conditions (1)-(6) are evaluated by the analyst role from mechanical inputs (Delta_max tables, NLB verdicts, G4 verdicts, control splits, KOT-LIFE/1 ledgers); none requires an attribution judgement.

claim: Programme-3 revision-2 work-breakdown structure (process decision; supersedes ASM-0807). PHASE-0 = TEN literature reviews + one direct-design task, all parallel: P3-LR-EVAL (evaluation/AI-index methodology: HELM, composite-index statistics, contamination + sealed-eval practice, proxy-rung validity, benchmark floors/ceilings); P3-LR-SYS (lifecycle cost, MLPerf-style systems benchmarking, storage/I-O economics, energy measurement); P3-LR-RAG (RAG, structured retrieval, knowledge graphs, tool-using neural baselines, adaptive test-time compute); P3-LR-PARSE (semantic parsing, program synthesis, grammar induction, uncertainty/selective prediction); P3-LR-NTP (neural theorem proving, differentiable logic, neural algorithmic reasoning, proof-trace distillation); P3-LR-FUSE (GNN-LLM fusion + neurosymbolic integration survey; absorbs revision-1 P3-LR-NSA + P3-LR-GNN); P3-LR-RULE (rule-injection into transformers); P3-LR-SURG (model compression, dimension/structured pruning, distillation, the exact invariances of transformer model surgery / causal representation analysis); P3-LR-STORE (store authoring/maintenance/provenance + knowledge-update economics); P3-LR-TINY (tiny-model training + small-scale scaling laws); P3-MF-0 (measurement + fair-comparison framework draft). PHASE-1 adds the eight review-named design tasks alongside the family designs: P3-D-THREAT (formal resource/baseline threat model); P3-D-FRONTIER (neural frontier-builder optimising baselines per budget); P3-D-POWER (coverage x max-gain power analysis, the G1 instrument); P3-D-SEAL (independent sealed benchmark); P3-D-RAGC (matched generic-RAG control); P3-D-ORACLE (oracle-to-NL error decomposition); P3-D-HW (hardware repeatability + cold/warm-cache protocol); P3-D-LIFE (lifecycle cost amortisation); plus P3-D-INDEX, P3-D-BASE, P3-D-NLB (now blocked by P3-LR-PARSE), P3-D-PS (new family), P3-D-VL (blocked by P3-D-POWER), P3-D-GNN, P3-D-RULE, P3-D-DD, P3-D-GU. PHASE-2 blocking invariants: every G4/W1-relevant experiment is blocked by the G4 BLOCK = {P3-D-INDEX frozen, P3-D-THREAT, P3-D-BASE, P3-D-FRONTIER, P3-D-HW, P3-D-RAGC, P3-E-CAL PASS}; P3-E-NLB-1, coverage censuses/G1, and oracle-diagnostic G2 stages are EXEMPT from P3-E-CAL; every natural-input store-touching stage is blocked by P3-E-NLB-1 or runs oracle-labelled per ASM-0814; P3-E-DD-1 is blocked by P3-E-DD-0 PASS; P3-E-SCALE-2 requires >= 1 Phase-2 survivor + maintainer gate + the G4 block. Full dependency graph as drawn in the design document section 5. Nothing is created/frozen/run by the design document; the coordinator beads it.
rationale: The review required expanding the lit-review layer (RAG baselines, semantic parsing, theorem proving, model-surgery invariances, lifecycle/systems benchmarking, contamination/sealed-eval statistics, store economics) and named eight missing design tasks; the dependency graph now encodes the NL/coverage wall (ASM-0814), the gate ladder (ASM-0817), and the measurement-first invariant, so the coordinator can bead it directly.
notes: Supersedes ASM-0807. Bead identifiers are design-document names; the coordinator assigns bd ids at creation.

claim: KOT-DECOMP/1 stage taxonomy (design choice; the P3-D-ORACLE deliverable, revised per the independent design review of 2026-07-11). (1) Canonical stage superset PARSE (.a intent/frame selection, .b entity/schema linking, .c role/direction binding, .d serialization), RETRIEVE, CONSTRUCT, ENCODE, EXECUTE, RENDER, plus the non-spliceable terminal consumer; every family instantiates an ordered subset; PARSE sub-stages are mandatory wherever a parser exists (the granularity that makes the measured a5-nl PARSE.c mechanism expressible). (2) Per-family boundary set B(F) = the ordered stages whose input regime differs between the family's G2 (oracle-input) and G3 (natural-input) runs; stages outside B(F) are attributed zero by construction. (3) ENCODE is probe-instrumented and splice-limited: a gold encoding of a learned encoder is undefined, so its upstream splice is 'encoder applied to the gold-constructed input' and the encode-vs-use separation comes from the delivery probe versus the endpoint (the nsk1 lesson as instrumentation). (4) TERMINAL RESIDUAL, corrected from the reviewed draft's over-identification: L_term = M_ceiling - M(S, FULL-ORACLE) is NOT identified as LM-use loss; it bundles genuine use/integration failure, irreducible task error and label ambiguity, capacity limits, non-spliceable executor/renderer error, and ceiling misspecification; it is published as an UNATTRIBUTED residual (an upper bound on USE loss); a large L_term with a passing delivery probe is CONSISTENT WITH the nsk1 delivery-without-integration signature and licenses only 'integration-limited (unattributed residual)' language, never 'USE is responsible for X'.
backing_ref: docs/next/design/ORACLE.md sections 3 and 4.4; docs/next/lit/PARSE.md section 8; poc/gpt56-review/rev-dORACLE-20260711/last-message.json ('USE residual is not identified')
rationale: The sub-stage granularity is what makes the measured a5-nl ROLE_DIR mechanism a nameable stage; the external reviewer showed the drafted residual claim assigned an unidentified mixture to USE, so the residual is demoted to an explicit upper bound with its confounds named and the claim language barred.
notes: Registers the reviewed ORACLE.md draft's proposed ASM-ORC-1 with the review's correction applied. Stage names deliberately avoid the S1/S2 kill-criteria namespace.

claim: KOT-DECOMP/1 attribution semantics (design choice; corrects the review's ranked concern 1 - the gate consumes mechanism-effect survival, so decomposing raw system performance alone was the wrong estimand). (1) ESTIMAND: for family F with mechanism system S and its pre-registered no-mechanism control C (the family's factorial control cell), the decomposed quantity is the mechanism effect gamma(c) = M(S,c) - M(C,c); the CONTROL LADDER IS MANDATORY - every mandatory configuration runs on BOTH arms; a stage in B(F) that C does not instantiate splices to identity on C (M(C,.) is constant across that splice, computed once per equivalence class and disclosed as degenerate). (2) Mandatory configuration set per family x vertical, each on both arms: FULL-REAL; the prefix ladder PREFIX(1..K) in pipeline order (PREFIX(0)=FULL-REAL, PREFIX(K)=FULL-ORACLE); single-stage REPAIR splices; the registered G2-ANCHOR configuration. (3) ANCHOR SEGMENTATION (corrects 'FULL-ORACLE is not consistently G2'): the family prereg must order B(F) so the G2-anchor stage set is a prefix ending at index j_anchor; the telescoped sum over j=1..j_anchor of dgamma_prefix(j) equals gamma(G2-ANCHOR) - gamma(FULL-REAL) = gamma_G2 - gamma_G3 and is THE G2-to-G3 mechanism-loss decomposition; rungs beyond j_anchor (e.g. RENDER where H-PS puts it in B(F)) decompose gamma(FULL-ORACLE) - gamma(G2-ANCHOR) and are labelled beyond-anchor diagnostic, never counted as G2-to-G3 loss. (4) PREFIX-CONDITIONING GAP (corrects the interaction over-claim): I(j) = dgamma_prefix(j) - dgamma_repair(j) measures conditioning on the ENTIRE gold upstream prefix, not a general interaction decomposition; it cannot name which upstream stage interacts; I(1) = 0 by construction; a small max |I(j)| is necessary evidence against pipeline-order non-additivity, never sufficient for additivity; single-stage claim language is gated on theta_int, and a flagged interaction is localised only by targeted pair splices or the 2^K factorial (mandatory escalation on CPU-deterministic verticals, optional elsewhere). (5) The S-only ladder over M(S,c) is co-published as SECONDARY 'system endpoint degradation' diagnostics, explicitly separate from the sigma/gamma attribution. (6) Interventional authority: where per-item trace and splice disagree, the splice attribution is authoritative. (7) Mechanical S2 (dangerous-wrong) attribution: an event at FULL-REAL is attributed to stage j iff REPAIR(j) flips that item to correct-or-abstain; multiple flips go to a joint bucket (never split fractionally); no single flip attributes to the smallest flipping prefix; S2 attribution never relies on stage confidence, only on splice flips.
backing_ref: docs/next/design/ORACLE.md sections 4, 7.2, 7.6; poc/gpt56-review/rev-dORACLE-20260711/last-message.json ranked concern 1 + 'Interaction statistic is incomplete'; docs/next/programme-3-neurosymbolic-architecture.md section 7.1 (K-P3v2(5))
rationale: The reviewer proved the drafted ladder could not tell whether sigma fell because S degraded or C improved (the control ladder was optional); making the control ladder mandatory and telescoping gamma at the registered G2 anchor makes the K-P3v2(5) quantity itself the decomposed object, and the interaction statistic is renamed and bounded to exactly what it measures.
notes: Registers the reviewed draft's proposed ASM-ORC-2 with the review's estimand, anchor, and interaction corrections applied. sigma itself and its governance live in ASM-0824; statistics in ASM-0826.

claim: KOT-DECOMP/1 oracle-artifact provenance rules (design choice). (1) Pin-before-run: every oracle corpus (gold parses, gold record maps, gold subgraphs, gold engine results, canonical surface forms) is content-hashed and pinned in the family prereg BEFORE any splice run; growing or editing an oracle corpus after first use is a new corpus version and restarts that family's ladder. (2) Blind authoring: gold artifacts are authored blind to any system output, from the item + store only; the existing frozen l3a/a5 gold parses and the f2b record-item map are REUSED, not re-authored. (3) Gold quality measured, not assumed: new gold corpora require dual independent authoring on a pre-registered sample (>= 10 percent or 100 items, whichever is larger) with agreement >= the pre-registered bar (default 0.95 artifact-exactness; family preregs may only tighten); disagreements adjudicated and logged; a corpus failing the bar is not an oracle (instrument gate IG-4). (4) Answerability pre-tagging: every eval item carries an independent pre-scoring tag answerable-covered | answerable-uncovered | ambiguous | unanswerable assigned by the gold-authoring pass; for ambiguous/unanswerable the gold PARSE artifact is ABSTAIN and for answerable-uncovered the gold EXECUTE artifact is the fail-closed refusal, so correct abstentions are creditable and a stage is never penalised for correctly refusing. (5) Coverage disclosure: items lacking a defined gold artifact at stage j are excluded from stage-j splice cells with published count and reason; an exclusion rate > 20 percent in any cell voids that cell's attribution claim (IG-5). (6) No oracle leakage: real-regime stages are bit-isolated from oracle corpora at run time; a real arm that could read gold is ERR_ORACLE_LEAK, fail-closed. (7) Labelling: every result row, plot, and table carries input_regime (full-real | prefix:<stage> | repair:<stage> | full-oracle) AND arm (S | C); full-real is always the first column; quoting any oracle-regime number without its label anywhere in the repo is a claims-lint violation.
backing_ref: docs/next/design/ORACLE.md section 5; docs/next/lit/PARSE.md sections 4, 5d (abstention-credit lineage: amp-2024, dte-2023, triagesql-2020)
rationale: Oracle corpora are the protocol's measurement standard: unpinned, system-informed, or unmeasured-quality gold silently converts a diagnostic into a leaderboard; the abstain-credit tagging is what stops a correct refusal being scored as a stage failure (should-abstain detection is measured-hard).
notes: Registers the reviewed draft's proposed ASM-ORC-3 unchanged in substance, plus the arm label required by ASM-0821's mandatory control ladder.

claim: KOT-DECOMP/1 instrumentation contract (implementation choices; adds the review-requested schema/adapter and determinism-realism corrections). (1) Harness home poc/oracle/ (harness code stays out of encoder/); zero new runtime deps; nice -n 10, checkpointed. (2) Stage interface run_stage(stage_id, item_id, upstream_artifact, config) -> artifact | ERR_*; the harness owns the splice and never calls a real stage at an oracled position. (3) VERSIONED ARTIFACT SCHEMAS: every stage's input/output artifact conforms to a versioned per-stage schema (kot-stage-artifact/1:<stage>) pinned in the family prereg; schema change = instrument version change. (4) FAMILY ADAPTERS: each participating family ships an adapter module mapping its implementation onto the stage interface; where an existing implementation cannot expose a boundary (e.g. a legacy parser without separable PARSE.a-d, or a tightly fused learned stage) the adapter either reconstructs the boundary with a published method or the prereg declares those sub-stages non-spliceable and attribution stays at the coarser granularity - non-spliceable boundaries are published limitations, never synthetically split. (5) Config and trace schemas carry {family, vertical, arm: S|C, stage_map, regimes, oracle_corpus_hashes, endpoint_id, seed(s), n_items, item_corpus_hash} and per-item per-stage {in_hash, out_hash, outcome OK|ABSTAIN|ERR_*, diverged_from_gold, confidence, cost_us|tokens} plus endpoint {answered, correct, dangerous_wrong}. (6) DETERMINISM, realistically scoped: deterministic stages byte-reproducible; LM stages endpoint-exact ONLY under pinned deterministic-kernel enforcement (greedy or pinned-seed decoding + deterministic backend flags + pinned hardware/driver); absent that enforcement the prereg registers a reproduction tolerance band and IG-2 judges the 1 percent twice-run sample against whichever applies. (7) Fail-closed error set ERR_ORACLE_LEAK, ERR_GOLD_MISSING, ERR_STAGE_CONTRACT, ERR_CONFIG_HASH: run aborts, no partial report. (8) (stage, in_hash) caching de-duplicates identical sub-computations across configs; cache hit rates are MEASURED and published per run - assumed savings never enter a budget. (9) NL corpora: K >= 3 phrasings per item with per-phrasing perturbation-class tags (label-verbatim | synonym | structural-paraphrase | noise), phrasing source disjoint from any calibration set; K = 1 legacy corpora usable only for the shakedown, disclosed as below field practice; the ITEM is the statistical unit and every LM-pass cost estimate multiplies by K.
backing_ref: docs/next/design/ORACLE.md section 6; docs/next/lit/PARSE.md section 6.5; poc/gpt56-review/rev-dORACLE-20260711/last-message.json ('no versioned artifact schemas or family adapters', 'Stochastic hierarchy is underspecified', 'K>=3 phrasings appears not to be multiplied')
rationale: The reviewer flagged the generic interface, the unrealistic seed-pinned exact-reproducibility assumption on nondeterministic GPU kernels, and the missing K-multiplier; each is fixed at the contract level so no family prereg can inherit the defect.
notes: Registers the reviewed draft's proposed ASM-ORC-4 with the review's corrections applied.

claim: KOT-DECOMP/1 governance and gate feed (design choice; corrects the review's ranked concern 3 - the reviewed draft invented a FULL-REAL exemption class the design cannot grant itself). (1) sigma = gamma_G3 / gamma_G2 with gamma_G2 = gamma(G2-ANCHOR) and gamma_G3 = gamma(FULL-REAL) per ASM-0821, defined only when the one-sided 95 percent LCB of gamma_G2 > 0; K-P3v2(5)'s 'G2 gain fails to survive G3 at the pre-registered retention margin' is evaluated as UCB95(sigma) < sigma_min with sigma_min set in the family prereg. (2) THE BOUNDARY-DIAGNOSTIC EXEMPTION IS A PROPOSAL, NOT A GRANT: programme rev-2's sequencing exemptions name P3-E-NLB-1, the G1 censuses, and oracle-labelled G2 only; this entry REQUESTS a programme/ASM-0814 amendment creating a boundary-diagnostic class for FULL-REAL runs of this instrument (licensing only the measured retention/sigma/attribution vector - never an index, usefulness, or competitive claim) and bundles the P3-E-CAL sequencing exemption for decomposition runs into the same request; the request has NO EFFECT until an explicit coordinator/maintainer ratification decision is recorded as an amendment. FAIL-CLOSED MEANWHILE: (a) configurations in which every boundary-crossing store-addressing stage is oracled run now as ordinary oracle-diagnostic under ASM-0814; (b) FULL-REAL and partially-real-boundary cells are populated ONLY by replaying frozen, already-measured per-item artifacts of registered runs (no new natural-input execution); a missing artifact excludes that cell with disclosure and never triggers re-execution; (c) any new-family FULL-REAL leg waits for per-vertical NLB clearance or the ratified amendment. (3) KOT-DECOMP/1 SUPPLEMENTS KOT-FAIR/2: the attribution annex is a mandatory co-publication of every G4 attempt by a store-touching family, but it does NOT satisfy or replace KOT-FAIR/2's factorial content-type x retrieval-architecture x executor controls or the matched generic-RAG comparator - those remain separately mandatory. (4) Every configuration with >= 1 oracled stage stays oracle-diagnostic: no W1 claim, no real-input claim of any kind; no oracle number travels unlabelled (claims-lint pattern rider on the shakedown bead). (5) RESOURCE ACCOUNTING: actual CPU/GPU hours of ALL splice runs enter the family's tuning/development audit trail (KOT-FAIR/2 tuning-symmetry ledger); oracle authoring, dual annotation, adjudication, and K-phrasing production hours enter KOT-LIFE/1; any FULL-REAL figure feeding a G4 reading carries the complete KOT-COST/2 warm/cold resource vector, not only cost_us|tokens; 1-2B (R-3) decomposition reruns are explicitly budgeted in the family's R-3 prereg from the gated R-3 allocation, never absorbed silently.
backing_ref: docs/next/design/ORACLE.md sections 7.2, 9, 10; docs/next/programme-3-neurosymbolic-architecture.md lines 194-201 and 881-889 (ASM-0814/0817 exemption lists); poc/gpt56-review/rev-dORACLE-20260711/last-message.json ranked concern 3 + 'KOT-FAIR/2 and resources'
rationale: The reviewer showed the draft's boundary-diagnostic class conflicted with the programme rule that every natural-input store-touching leg is NLB-blocked or oracle-labelled, and that the design was granting itself the exemption; this entry converts the exemption into a ratification request with a fail-closed interim rule, and closes the review's resource-accounting gaps at the governance level.
notes: Registers the reviewed draft's proposed ASM-ORC-5, restructured per the review. ASM-0814's W1 prohibition is not weakened anywhere; ratification is a coordinator/maintainer act, not this design's.

claim: KOT-DECOMP/1's own falsifier: the P3-E-ORACLE-0 known-answer bars (design choice; corrects the review's ranked concern 2 - the legacy-vertical bars alone were near-tautological). KA-1 (paired ceiling, corrected): FULL-ORACLE reproduces gold-parse covered exactness on the PAIRED NL item slices - 527/527 on the l3a family/world NL slice and 855/855 on a5-nl; the 600/600 figure is the parent FORMAL slice and is checked separately as an engine regression, never as the paired shakedown cell. KA-2 (regression only, disclosed as near-true-by-construction): >= 95 percent of the measured G2-to-G3 loss attributes to the PARSE stage family on both legacy verticals - on this system PARSE is the only boundary-crossing fallible stage, so KA-2 validates plumbing and cannot validate multi-stage causal attribution. KA-3 (with a numeric tolerance, previously deferred): sub-stage attribution on l3a-parse reproduces the measured first-divergence breakdown 228/48/40/41 with each sub-stage's attributed share within +/- 0.10 absolute of the measured share and frame-miss remaining the modal category. KA-4 (bound direction corrected): the S2 rule attributes >= 90 percent of a5-nl's 43 dangerous-wrong events to PARSE.c, AND the PARSE.c repair splice drives the post-repair dangerous-wrong one-sided 95 percent UCB below 0.02 on that vertical - establishing <= 2 percent requires the UCB below the bar; an LB below the bar proves nothing (the S2 KILL direction, LB >= bar, is a different test). KA-5: instrument gates IG-1..IG-5 all pass on the legacy K=1 corpora (disclosed below field practice, shakedown-only). KA-6 (the decisive falsifier, added per review): a SYNTHETIC factorial fault-injection pipeline - deterministic, CPU-only, with a paired control arm and fully known ground truth - containing (a) independently injected faults in >= 2 distinct stages at known marginal rates, (b) one pure two-stage interaction that flips only when BOTH stages are repaired, (c) one harmless artifact divergence (artifact differs from gold, endpoint unaffected), (d) one downstream stage whose apparent (trace-visible) error is caused upstream, (e) planted dangerous-wrong events with known causal stages, (f) one no-effect oracle splice; PASS requires exact recovery of the planted marginal attributions, JOINT (not single-stage) claim language for the planted interaction, zero attribution to the harmless divergence and to the no-effect splice, upstream attribution of the upstream-caused error against the misleading trace label, and 100 percent planted-S2 recovery; the gamma (paired-arm) path runs end-to-end on this pipeline. A FAIL on KA-2, KA-3, KA-4, or KA-6 falsifies KOT-DECOMP/1 (not the architectures) and returns it for redesign before any family G3 run consumes it.
backing_ref: docs/next/design/ORACLE.md section 8; registry/verdicts/l3a-parse.json (parse_stage_breakdown 228/48/40/41; 251/527 retention), registry/verdicts/a5-nl.json (43/855 S2 events); poc/gpt56-review/rev-dORACLE-20260711/last-message.json ranked concern 2 + 'Wrong confidence-bound direction' + 'Incompatible shakedown sample' + 'KA-3 has no actual tolerance'
rationale: The reviewer showed KA-2 is nearly true by construction on the legacy verticals and named the exact synthetic-pipeline properties a decisive cheap falsifier needs; KA-6 adopts that list verbatim, and the KA-1/KA-3/KA-4 arithmetic and bound-direction defects are corrected as flagged.
notes: Registers the reviewed draft's proposed ASM-ORC-6 with the review's corrections. The shakedown bead P3-E-ORACLE-0 remains a coordinator creation; nothing runs from this entry.

claim: KOT-DECOMP/1 statistical specification (design choice; corrects the review-named power and hierarchy defects). (1) Endpoint discipline: each family x vertical names ONE pre-registered endpoint scalar M and always co-reports the selective triple - coverage, selective risk P(wrong | answered), and dangerous-wrong rate. (2) Per-stage risk-coverage curves + AUACC wherever a stage emits confidence; a scalar operating point is never the only view. (3) UNCERTAINTY: paired BCa hierarchical bootstrap on the gamma quantities with the ITEM as the top-level resampling cluster; K phrasings stay nested within their item and are never resampled as independent units; where an LM stage samples (non-greedy), >= 2 registered seeds and seed enters as a registered variance component at its own level. (4) POWER, endpoint-typed (the blanket 'n approx 500-1000 suffices' claim is WITHDRAWN): paired binary endpoints compute McNemar power at the pre-registered target (default: detect stage loss 0.05 at 80 percent power, one-sided 5 percent) from a PRE-REGISTERED discordant-pair-rate estimate sourced from the shakedown or a pilot; required n scales inversely with the discordant rate and can exceed 1000. Continuous endpoints (graph F1, AUACC) use paired-difference power from a pre-registered SD estimate or simulation-based power via the hierarchical bootstrap; McNemar is never applied to non-binary endpoints. The ITEM count is the power unit - K phrasings do not multiply effective n. The legacy 527/855 corpora are measured decision-grade anchors for their own verticals only, not a general sufficiency claim. (5) MULTIPLICITY: sigma is the only gate-feeding estimand and carries the one-sided CI; the stage-loss vectors are diagnostic, receive no FWER correction, and every report states this.
backing_ref: docs/next/design/ORACLE.md section 7; registry/verdicts/l3a-parse.json + registry/verdicts/a5-nl.json envelopes; poc/gpt56-review/rev-dORACLE-20260711/last-message.json ('Power claim is unsupported', 'Stochastic hierarchy is underspecified'); house statistics per ASM-0813
rationale: The reviewer showed the drafted power claim was unsupported (McNemar n depends on the discordant-pair rate and does not cover continuous endpoints) and the phrasing/seed hierarchy was unspecified; this entry pins the estimator hierarchy and forces every family prereg to compute its own n from its endpoint type.
notes: Registers the statistical half of the reviewed draft's proposed ASM-ORC-2/ORC-5 content, corrected. Complements ASM-0813 (house statistics) without editing it.

claim: FRONT/1 budget-vector rule (design choice; operationalises ASM-0810/ASM-0812 for the frontier-builder). (1) GRAMMAR: B_k = (bytes_max = KOT-SIZE/2 canonically-packed minimal-artifact bytes; p95_latency_max end-to-end under the pinned P3-D-HW concurrency distribution, warm and cold both admissibility-checked; energy_per_query_max whole-run integrated; mem_max = peak accelerator memory + host RSS). (2) ENERGY FAIL-CLOSED RULE (adopts MF0 section-8.1's measurable-else-MISSING clause + the SYS.md proposed W1 admissibility rule as ONE binding rule): energy is always reported per arm with its declared measurement boundary, or MISSING where no counter exists (never modeled from TDP or utilisation); it is a BINDING admissibility component of B_k only where every compared arm carries a measurement at the same declared boundary, from the same instrument class, on the same rig; a MISSING or boundary-mismatched cell excludes the energy dimension from that comparison (disclosed in the consuming prereg and verdict) and B_k binds on the remaining components. (3) DERIVATION: one primary budget vector per rung, derived mechanically from the rung's named anchor model (R-1 SmolLM2-135M, R-2 SmolLM2-360M, R-3 SmolLM2-1.7B; name+revision pinned by P3-D-BASE) at multiplier x1.0 on every component, pinned at RUNG ENTRY before any candidate system S is measured at that rung; additional budget vectors registrable only before first S measurement; thereafter the budget set is immutable for that index version; an S that does not fit fails ADMISSIBILITY - no post-hoc budget inflation and no S-derived budgets.
backing_ref: docs/next/design/FRONT.md sections 1.1-1.2; docs/next/design/MF0.md section 8.1; docs/next/lit/SYS.md sections 2-3; poc/gpt56-review/rev-dFRONT-20260711/last-message.json ranked concern 2 (energy rule)
rationale: Anchor-derived budgets are computable before any architecture exists and counter the budget-shopping channel; the external review required ONE fail-closed energy rule reconciling revision-1 FRONT's universally-binding energy component with MF0's measurable-else-MISSING clause and the measured absence of CPU-side RAPL on the current box - the same-boundary/same-instrument rule is the only energy comparison the surveyed instruments can honestly support.
notes: Registered by the rev-dFRONT-20260711 review revision; fresh block ASM-0850-0879 claimed (the concurrent rev-dMF0 and KOT-DECOMP revision waves are consuming the 0820-0849 block). Replaces FRONT.md's provisional in-doc id P3F-A1.

claim: FRONT/1 F(B_k) semantics, comparator search space, and builder pipeline (design choice; operationalises ASM-0812 clauses i-iii and v). (1) F(B_k) = comparators built from the pinned grid or admitted by nomination, admissible on every MEASURED component of B_k warm and cold, pinned (config/weights/seeds/decoding/index/verifier hashes) at frontier freeze; F(B_k) is content-hashed; any change is a frontier VERSION bump that re-baselines W1 at that rung. (2) ENVELOPE REPORTING: the KOT-COST/2 resource vector is never scalarised - publish the non-dominated set in the FULL resource space plus explicitly named 2-D projections (index x bytes; index x p95 latency; index x peak accelerator memory; index x energy only where the ASM-0850 energy rule makes that dimension binding); dominated and inadmissible points reported, never dropped. (3) STATISTICS INTERFACE: W1 = simultaneous LCB95(INDEX(S)-INDEX(C)) > delta_k over every C in F(B_k), FWER-controlled; NO claim that a max-t procedure loses negligible power however large F is - the comparator covariance is estimated by the hierarchical bootstrap in the pinned analysis plan and P3-D-POWER sizes experiments under the realised |F(B_k)| multiplicity. (4) ENUMERATION: eligibility by measured canonically-packed bytes under the transform grid, never parameter count; S0 may drop a candidate on ANALYTIC bytes only when the estimate exceeds bytes_max x kappa_pack (pre-registered safety factor, proposed 1.25); candidates within the factor are canonically PACKED before any exclusion; all drops logged. (5) GRID: C1 open-weight donors (roster pinned by P3-D-BASE); C2 quantisation ladder (llama.cpp k-quants Q8_0/Q5_K_M/Q4_K_M/Q3_K_M + one pinned calibration int4) applied symmetrically across arms; C2 structured pruning at {12.5,25,50}% ONLY as Minitron-class width(+depth) pruning with KD retraining + teacher correction and Sheared-LLaMA-style recovery-data re-mixing per the landed P3-LR-SURG, with pruned entries OPTIONAL and never load-bearing for a frontier freeze until (a) an R-1 pilot benchmark of the candidate recipe on the actual 135M/160M-class donors passes and (b) the distillation-scaling-law calculation at donor scale (or a KD-vs-continued-pretraining recovery pilot) is done; small-donor caution binding: published big-model retention figures are never extrapolated down; C2 distillation capped by T_k, R-2+ by default; C3 retrieval arms per ASM-0853; C4 adaptive test-time compute per ASM-0852; C5 from-scratch twins only where S itself trains from scratch. Composed R-1 grid is COUNTED, not guessed: ~14 donors x 5 quant levels (incl. none) = ~70 static bases, byte-filtered to ~20-30, x up-to-3 retrieval cells x 2 TTC = <= ~180 composed candidates plus <= ~12 optional pruned/distilled entries. (6) PIPELINE (SEVEN stages S0-S6): enumerate; screen on a pinned ~10% stratified dev mini-suite by successive halving; optimise under ESTIMATED admissibility (bytes measured by packing, latency/energy/memory via a pinned cheap estimator); measure on the P3-D-HW rig; admit on MEASURED figures with a fall-back selection rule (if a family's S2-selected config fails measured admissibility, selection falls back to that family's best admissible logged config, re-measured - never re-opened search); envelope; pin. (7) LEAKAGE + SELECTION: all builder selection on a dev split disjoint from the frozen suite and every sealed release; fixed selection rule = dev-split index restricted to that rung's above-floor components, ties broken by lower measured cost (energy where boundary-admissible, then p95). (8) NL DISCIPLINE: comparators consume natural benchmark text (ASM-0808 applied); no gold parses, subgraphs, addresses or passages; oracle-diagnostic comparators labelled per ASM-0814 never enter F(B_k); the NLB gate binds the kernel front-end only, never the frontier. (9) NOMINATION WINDOW: any party may nominate an open reproducible comparator until freeze; the builder admits it or publishes a one-line refusal reason; post-freeze nominations queue for the next frontier version.
backing_ref: docs/next/design/FRONT.md sections 1.3, 2, 3, 6, 7.2; docs/next/lit/SURG.md section 1; poc/gpt56-review/rev-dFRONT-20260711/last-message.json ranked concern 2 + other-errors list
rationale: Turns ASM-0812's frontier clause into an executable, auditable instrument; adopts the external review's named corrections exactly: seven-stage count, packed-bytes-before-exclusion at S0, estimated-screen-then-measured-fallback ordering for S2/S4, full-vector frontier reporting in place of a scalarised cost axis, removal of the unsupported max-t negligible-power claim, counted grid arithmetic, and incorporation of the now-landed P3-LR-SURG recovery recipe and small-model warnings before pruning enters the frozen search space.
notes: Registered by the rev-dFRONT-20260711 review revision. Replaces FRONT.md's provisional in-doc id P3F-A2.

claim: FRONT/1 adaptive test-time-compute recipe + tuning-compute meter (design choice; operationalises ASM-0812 clause iv and its tuning-symmetry clause; resolves RAG.md sections 7.6/8 OPEN items). (1) AGGREGATION SEMANTICS PER COMPONENT CLASS (class pinned per index component by P3-D-INDEX at freeze; no undefined majority voting): SA short-answer/MC components - agreement = modal share of canonicalised answers under the suite's own pinned normaliser, full ladder P0-P3 eligible; OG open-generation/instruction-following components - majority vote UNDEFINED, the agreement stage is replaced by a pinned mean-logprob confidence proxy, eligible policies are P0 and verifier/PRM-scored best-of-N (P2/P3, the verifier IS the aggregation), self-consistency ineligible; LL LM-loss/perplexity components - deterministic single computation, always P0, TTC does not apply; ABSTENTION emitted only where the index scores an abstention channel, else the P0 answer. (2) LADDER: P0 greedy/single sample; P1 self-consistency N in {4,16,64} adaptive; P2 best-of-N + outcome verifier; P3 PRM-weighted BoN/beam with auto-labels - P2/P3 eligible iff the verifier trains within T_k AND verifier bytes fit bytes_max. (3) Self-charging agreement escalation as the difficulty estimator (n0 = 4 samples, pinned threshold tau; the estimator is stage one of the policy so its compute is charged by construction); base-success~0 fallback to P0 below a pinned floor tau_0. (4) Verifier/PRM training + auto-labelling compute on KOT-LIFE/1, verifier weights on KOT-SIZE/2, verifier inference on KOT-COST/2 - no free component. (5) NON-MATH CALIBRATION GATE per domain x component class before any TTC arm enters a frozen F(B_k): dev-split LCB95(gain over P0) > 0 at that rung or the arm freezes as P0; no published TTC figure is ever assumed to transfer. (6) Full N-sweep decay curves published for every P2/P3 arm. (7) T_k METER: tuning symmetry binds on total tuning compute as a RESOURCE VECTOR T_k = (accelerator-hours, CPU-core-hours), each component separately capped per arm - GPU-hour-only caps left CPU-heavy indexing/search uncapped, corrected here. The unit of an arm is MECHANICAL: a closed ARM TABLE of ledger keys registered at rung entry alongside B_k (R-1 comparator keys: C1-quant, C2-prune, C2-distil, C3-RAG-matched, C3-RAG-free, C4-TTC; a C5-twin key only at rungs where a from-scratch S is live; plus one key for S); every tuning/search/recovery/verifier-training job carries exactly one key before launch; an unattributable job is billed to the largest-spend arm (fail-closed); adding a key after rung entry is a frontier VERSION bump, so family boundaries cannot multiply allowance. Proposed values, maintainer-adjustable and final at KOT-FAIR/2 freeze: T_1 = (4 GPU-h, 40 CPU-core-h) per arm; T_2 = (20 GPU-h, 200 CPU-core-h) per arm; T_3 = (80 GPU-h, 600 CPU-core-h) per arm. An S that spends more than T_k has not won; a comparator arm that exhausts T_k freezes at its best logged config; donor pretraining compute is inherited and declared on KOT-LIFE/1, never netted out and never counted against T_k.
backing_ref: docs/next/design/FRONT.md sections 3.3, 4; docs/next/lit/RAG.md sections 4-8; docs/next/lit/EVAL.md section 7; poc/gpt56-review/rev-dFRONT-20260711/last-message.json ranked concern 2 (caps/units) + other-errors (TTC semantics)
rationale: The external review showed the revision-1 meter was not dimensionally closed (GPU-hour caps claiming to cover CPU-hours; vaguely bounded families multiplying allowance) and that majority-agreement TTC semantics were undefined for open generation, instruction following and LM-loss components; the resource-vector cap with a closed mechanical arm table and the per-class aggregation semantics make both executable and auditable.
notes: Registered by the rev-dFRONT-20260711 review revision. Replaces FRONT.md's provisional in-doc ids P3F-A3 and P3F-A3-T.

claim: FRONT/1 matched-retrieval binding defaults (design choice; resolves RAG.md section 6 OPEN rules 1-5; inherited verbatim by P3-D-RAGC, amendable only by registered change). RULE 1 - one pinned source snapshot per vertical; per-arm indexed objects derived by pinned seed-pinned automated scripts; information parity enforced as four MEASURED checks and no further (source parity, byte-cap parity, decontamination-screen parity, effort disclosure) + the standing residual-asymmetry disclosure carried verbatim on every consuming claim. RULE 2 - CORRECTED per the 2026-07-11 external review: TWO mandatory retrieval cells, BOTH members of F(B_k) whenever S uses a store, each with a distinct claim licence: (a) the SHARED/MATCHED cell - one pinned retriever+index (BM25 + one pinned small dense embedder) over the SAME pinned corpus snapshot at the SAME budgets, over a common text serialisation of every arm's objects - IS the programme's stipulated same-corpus/same-retriever-index/same-budgets control (ASM-0812 clause iii verbatim; KOT-FAIR/2 programme section 2.3 / MF0 section 8.2) and is a REQUIRED member of the W1 control set AND of F(B_k): a W1 claim that has not beaten this cell is void; its structural handicap for arms whose native retrieval is not dense-over-text is DISCLOSED beside its result, never grounds for exclusion; (b) the NATIVE cell - each arm retrieves the way its architecture natively does (kernel addressing for S; the RAG arm's best in-budget retriever) under the same resource budgets - ADDITIONALLY enters F(B_k) as the strongest-system frontier point; it never replaces the shared/matched cell. Every consuming prereg names which cell licenses which sentence. RULE 3 - pinned per rung: max retrieved tokens per query identical across arms, max context length, assembly order = retrieval-score-descending, position-shuffle control cell in RAGC's factorial. RULE 4 - store-vs-store comparisons: identical generator checkpoint, decoding params, retry budget, abstention accounting, tool-executor semantics; tuning <= T_k per arm; the free-corpus whole-system arm is exempt with the narrower better-baseline-exists licence, no attribution licence. RULE 5 - every construction pass booked as build compute in KOT-LIFE/1 and artifact bytes in KOT-SIZE/2 on every arm identically; derivation/construction pipelines content-hash-frozen BEFORE any frozen-suite item is drawn; decontamination screen applies to construction inputs and outputs; a store or index whose construction post-dates suite exposure is VOID for W1 use.
backing_ref: docs/next/design/FRONT.md sections 2.3, 5; docs/next/programme-3-neurosymbolic-architecture.md section 2.3; docs/next/design/MF0.md section 8.2; docs/next/lit/RAG.md section 6; poc/gpt56-review/rev-dFRONT-20260711/last-message.json ranked concern 1
rationale: Revision 1 of FRONT put only the architecture-native cell into F(B_k) and demoted the shared/matched cell to an attribution instrument, contradicting KOT-FAIR/2's binding same-corpus/same-retriever/same-budgets contract; the review's correction is adopted exactly - shared/matched satisfies ASM-0812 and participates in the required W1 control set and the frontier, native additionally enters as the strongest-system point.
notes: Registered by the rev-dFRONT-20260711 review revision. Replaces FRONT.md's provisional in-doc id P3F-A4.

claim: FRONT/1 builder instrument gates, weak-builder counters, and compute plan (design choice; extends the ASM-0812 threat model with the weak-builder-gaming channel). F-CAL ACCEPTANCE GATES, pre-registered as P3-E-FCAL, co-scheduled with P3-E-CAL: A1 monotonicity SMOKE TEST (builder's best optimised entry non-inferior to the untouched anchor baseline, single one-sided CI; necessary but NOT decisive - the anchor is itself available to the builder, so passing is near-tautological); A2 external reproduction (two pinned model x quantisation-method pairs reproduce published retention within a pre-registered tolerance band); A3 repeatability (two independent builder runs, different seeds same grid, agree within a pre-registered band and select the same frozen configs or documented ties); A4 BLINDED CHALLENGER/REGRET GATE (the decisive weak-builder falsifier, added on review): after the builder freezes its search result at a rung, a small challenger set (proposed 5-10 configurations) is nominated by parties independent of that builder run (maintainer + external referee + any open nominator), blind to the builder's selected configs, drawn from published recipes/leaderboards and from candidates the S1 screen discarded; each challenger is evaluated ONCE under the same rung dev budget on the same rig; PASS iff dev-index(builder's selected frontier best) >= dev-index(best challenger) - epsilon_A4 (pre-registered regret margin); a challenger win by more than epsilon_A4 falsifies the frontier as under-searched, and the gate simultaneously bounds successive-halving screening noise (a true-best discarded on the noisy 10% proxy resurfaces as a challenger). FAIL-CLOSED: if A1, A2 or A4 fails after one pre-registered repair cycle, no W1 experiment may cite F(B_k) - every G4 [EXP] stays blocked; A3 failure forces selection-rule redesign before freeze. DEPENDENCY ENFORCEMENT (closing the review's G4 wiring gap): a REQUIRED registered amendment adds P3-T-FRONT-IMPL and P3-E-FCAL PASS to the programme section-5 G4 blocking set, and the bead graph carries the corresponding dep edges; until that amendment lands, no G4 prereg may freeze. COMPUTE PLAN (planning estimates, final at KOT-FAIR/2 freeze; closed rung arithmetic): R-1 builder total <= ~40 GPU-h (screen ~4 + measurement ~12 + 6 comparator arms x T_1 accelerator component 4 GPU-h = 24); R-2 <= ~150 GPU-h (6 x 20 + ~30 screen/measure); R-3 <= ~600 GPU-h (6 x 80 + ~120 measurement), feasible ONLY inside a secured AIRR-Gateway/TRC-class allocation per the programme's R-3 gating - if no allocation materialises the R-3 frontier does not freeze and every R-3 W1 claim stays blocked, so the 100M-2B span is CONDITIONAL and disclosed, not assumed; graceful degradation to a disclosed weaker frontier version (C1 x quantisation + BM25 RAG + P1 self-consistency, CPU/single-free-GPU) if no external GPU materialises.
backing_ref: docs/next/design/FRONT.md sections 7.3, 8, 10, 13; poc/gpt56-review/rev-dFRONT-20260711/last-message.json ranked concern 3 + ranked concern 2 (budget arithmetic)
rationale: The external review showed a consistently weak, under-searched frontier can pass A1-A3 (A1 near-tautological, A2 plumbing, A3 repeatability) and that the prose fail-closed rule had no dependency enforcement in the programme's G4 blocking set; the blinded challenger/regret gate directly falsifies weak-builder gaming, the blocking-set amendment makes the rule mechanical, and the rung arithmetic now sums to its own caps including the previously missing R-3 tier.
notes: Registered by the rev-dFRONT-20260711 review revision. Replaces FRONT.md's provisional in-doc id P3F-A5 (and its -annex).

claim: KOT-FAIR/2 freeze graph (corrects MF0 draft 1's incoherent freeze ownership, GPT-5.6 rev-dMF0 ranked concern 1): the framework freezes only via a dedicated INTEGRATION/FREEZE step P3-D-FAIR, blocked by ALL component designs — P3-D-INDEX (index pins + kot-pack/1 + kot-decon/1 + analysis constants), P3-D-HW, P3-D-LIFE, P3-D-SEAL, P3-D-FRONTIER, P3-D-RAGC, P3-D-THREAT — plus P3-E-CAL PASS and maintainer sign-off. P3-D-INDEX owns only the index piece and does not silently absorb the other components; no G4/W1 experiment or P3-E-CAL execution launches from the MF0 draft.
rationale: Draft 1 said P3-D-INDEX freezes the whole framework while its dependency list omitted P3-D-HW/LIFE/SEAL/FRONTIER/RAGC and its freeze conditions required mostly lit inputs rather than completed designs; the review required an explicit integration/freeze bead blocked by all component designs.
notes: Registered by the MF0 draft-2 revision (GPT-5.6 review rev-dMF0-20260711 applied); fresh ASM-0880..0909 block, append-only. Renumbered twice pre-citation: three concurrent Phase-1 review-revision writers collided on the 0820 block and the FRONT/1 revision holds 0850+.

claim: W1 resource admissibility (corrects MF0 draft 1's non-binding 'full resource vector', rev-dMF0 ranked concern 2): B_k has PRIMARY components (max canonically-packed serving bytes; max p95 service-time latency, plus max p95 latency under the pinned open-loop load where an offered-load claim is made; max energy/query at the declared boundary; max peak accelerator memory + host RSS) and HARD SECONDARY CEILINGS registered in the same prereg on every other measured KOT-COST/2 component (CPU-seconds/query, accelerator-time/query, bytes read from storage+network/query, TTFT/TPOT for generative arms, min throughput under pinned load), set generously (draft 3x the strongest fitting comparator) to exclude resource laundering; exceeding ANY ceiling is inadmissible. ENERGY: a W1 run executes on ONE common platform for all arms with a common measurable energy boundary at which energy binds; if no measurable boundary exists the claim must carry the explicit ENERGY-EXCLUDED label on its claim surface, never a silent drop. ADMISSIBILITY UNDER UNCERTAINTY: membership of F(B_k) and S's own admissibility are decided on UCB95 of each measured resource component over the repeatability repetitions, fail-closed when the UCB straddles a ceiling.
backing_ref: docs/next/design/MF0.md sections 4.1, 8.1; poc/gpt56-review/rev-dMF0-20260711/last-message.json concern 2 + 'Resource admissibility ignores measurement uncertainty'
rationale: Draft 1's B_k constrained only bytes/p95/energy/memory while KOT-COST/2 recorded more, so a system could consume far more CPU/IO/network/accelerator work and still win W1; dropping energy whenever unmeasurable contradicted the parent's 'energy explicitly binds' W1; point-estimate membership made frontier composition unstable near ceilings.
notes: Registered by the MF0 draft-2 revision (GPT-5.6 review rev-dMF0-20260711 applied); fresh ASM-0880..0909 block, append-only. Renumbered twice pre-citation: three concurrent Phase-1 review-revision writers collided on the 0820 block and the FRONT/1 revision holds 0850+.

claim: Driver legality, corrected (rev-dMF0 'Open-loop/SingleStream contradiction'): closed-loop SingleStream-style drivers are VALID for isolated per-query service-time percentiles and every such figure is labelled 'service-time (closed-loop, unloaded)'; open-loop pinned seeded-schedule drivers with latency measured from intended issue time are MANDATORY for any latency-under-offered-load or throughput-conditioned percentile claim, for which closed-loop next-query-on-completion drivers are prohibited (coordinated omission). The two claim types are never conflated; rules symmetric across arms.
rationale: Draft 1 prohibited closed-loop drivers for every percentile claim while mandating MLPerf SingleStream, which is closed-loop by definition; SYS.md's own reading distinguishes unloaded service-time (closed-loop legitimate) from latency-under-load (open-loop required).
notes: Registered by the MF0 draft-2 revision (GPT-5.6 review rev-dMF0-20260711 applied); fresh ASM-0880..0909 block, append-only. Renumbered twice pre-citation: three concurrent Phase-1 review-revision writers collided on the 0820 block and the FRONT/1 revision holds 0850+.

claim: Below-floor exclusion criterion, corrected inferential direction (rev-dMF0 'below-floor rule uses the wrong inferential direction'): a component is below-floor at a rung iff UCB95(s - chance) < Delta_disc for the LARGEST calibration anchor at that rung, where Delta_disc is a registered minimum discriminative span set from P3-E-CAL calibration data BEFORE any campaign result (draft: the span required to move the component's domain score by one registered delta-quantum). Exclusion requires the data to DEMONSTRATE non-discrimination at controlled confidence; 'not proven above chance' (LCB<=0) is not an exclusion ground. Below-floor components report in the vector, are excluded from the scalar, and the exclusion list is part of the index version.
rationale: Draft 1's LCB95(s-chance)<=0 rule meant 'not proven above chance', which excludes underpowered components and retains statistically significant but practically useless ones; the review required a minimum discriminative effect with a UCB-fails-threshold test.
notes: Registered by the MF0 draft-2 revision (GPT-5.6 review rev-dMF0-20260711 applied); fresh ASM-0880..0909 block, append-only. Renumbered twice pre-citation: three concurrent Phase-1 review-revision writers collided on the 0820 block and the FRONT/1 revision holds 0850+.

claim: G1 Delta_max validity preconditions (rev-dMF0 'G1 is not yet a genuinely cheap, decisive bound'): (1) per-benchmark HEADROOM clamp — Delta-tilde_max,b = min(kappa_b*(1-a_b_cov)/(ceiling_b-chance_b), 1-min(s-tilde_b_B,1)) so covered-item gain cannot exceed the clamped scalar's available headroom; (2) kappa_b=0 => Delta-tilde_max,b=0 with a_b_cov undefined-and-unused; (3) uncertainty by census mode — a complete finite census of a frozen suite has no counting uncertainty, a sampled census carries sampling error, an automated census carries measured classifier error; each propagates with a_b_cov estimation error to UCB95(Delta_max), and the kill compares UCB95(Delta_max) < delta_k; (4) coverage-label false-negative protection — 'covered' = answerable-from-the-store; uncertain items count as COVERED, an automated labeller must have its false-negative rate measured on an adjudicated audit sample and folded into the upper bound on kappa_b, and the kappa adjudication protocol + inter-annotator agreement + human-hours accounting are frozen by P3-D-POWER BEFORE any G1 kill fires; (5) manual formalisation used to validate the labeller is an oracle diagnostic (ASM-0814 label) with logged human cost, never a capability input. Until (1)-(5) are frozen, G1 outputs are provisional and kill nothing.
rationale: The review found the draft-1 bound lacked the clamping/headroom term, left a_cov undefined at zero coverage, mis-assigned counting uncertainty to complete censuses, and above all ignored that coverage labels are semantic judgements whose false negatives shrink kappa and invalidate the UPPER bound; the kill instrument must err in the killed family's favour.
notes: Registered by the MF0 draft-2 revision (GPT-5.6 review rev-dMF0-20260711 applied); fresh ASM-0880..0909 block, append-only. Renumbered twice pre-citation: three concurrent Phase-1 review-revision writers collided on the 0820 block and the FRONT/1 revision holds 0850+.
