claim: Canonical packing figure separation (rev-dMF0 'packing mixes deployment size with construction provenance'): KOT-SIZE/2 figure (1) counts the canonically-packed AS-BUILT minimally-sufficient SERVING artifact only (boot-and-answer smoke test from packed bytes alone still applies); construction inputs, intermediate bytes, build scripts and build compute are counted in figure (5), which every W1 claim co-reports. The draft-1 rule max(as-built bytes, construction inputs + build script) is retired — it arbitrarily charged development material as deployed bytes while figure (5) already owns construction.
rationale: Figure (1)'s estimand is the deployed serving artifact; charging whichever of two different estimands is larger conflates deployment size with construction provenance, and the anti-'rebuildable for free' concern is already answered by mandatory co-reporting of figure (5).
notes: Registered by the MF0 draft-2 revision (GPT-5.6 review rev-dMF0-20260711 applied); fresh ASM-0880..0909 block, append-only. Renumbered twice pre-citation: three concurrent Phase-1 review-revision writers collided on the 0820 block and the FRONT/1 revision holds 0850+.

claim: kot-life/1-draft2 lifecycle-ledger schema (rev-dMF0 'lifecycle schema cannot execute K-P3v2(6)', consuming the landed STORE.md sections 6-8): the store line carries THREE recurring sub-lines — (a) build/mint denominated per ACCEPTED CHECKABLE record with candidate/rejected attrition and source-item counts (article-to-fact yield measurable), (b) human-review + provenance verification with an explicit redundancy multiplier, (c) staleness-refresh at a declared cadence including reindex/rollback compute and dependency fan-out per update — plus a fixed-vs-variable cost split, record classes, and author/reviewer time as distributions where measured. Hardware occupancy uses explicit formulas (RAM rent = GB-resident x hours-served x pinned GB-hour price; accelerator rent = hours x pinned SKU price; storage rent = GB x months x pinned price). Amortisation at the three pinned volumes is DEFINED per separately-denominated unit u in {usd, human-hours, joules-where-measured}: TCO_u(q) = fixed_u + build_u + q*(per_query_u) + refresh_u(q), never combined across units. The Sardana crossover per comparator C and unit u is the volume q* where TCO_u(S,q*) = TCO_u(C,q*), null where curves do not cross. K-P3v2(6) 'dominated' is DEFINED as per-line Pareto domination: TCO_u(C,q) <= TCO_u(S,q) on every unit measurable for both arms at ALL THREE volumes with strict inequality somewhere; units measurable for only one arm are excluded and disclosed. Human hours are real costs, logged, never rounded into a '~$0' compute claim.
backing_ref: docs/next/design/MF0.md section 5; docs/next/lit/STORE.md sections 6-8; poc/gpt56-review/rev-dMF0-20260711/last-message.json lifecycle item
rationale: Draft 1's amortisation and crossover fields were empty objects, 'dominated' was undefined while units were deliberately not combined, and the schema predated the landed STORE requirements (mint/attrition/review/provenance/fixed-variable/record-classes/refresh-reindex-rollback/staleness cadence) plus an explicit hardware-cost formula.
notes: Registered by the MF0 draft-2 revision (GPT-5.6 review rev-dMF0-20260711 applied); fresh ASM-0880..0909 block, append-only. Renumbered twice pre-citation: three concurrent Phase-1 review-revision writers collided on the 0820 block and the FRONT/1 revision holds 0850+.

claim: Sealed-evaluation statistics + fresh-facts chronology (rev-dMF0 ranked concern 3b + 'fresh-fact sealing has an unresolved chronology/oracle problem'): (1) the sealed gate is the simultaneous one-sided LCB95(INDEX_sealed(S) - INDEX_sealed(C)) > delta_sealed for EVERY comparator C in the pre-registered F(B_k), all run on the sealed release under the same pinned harness, multiplicity controlled with the same family-wise machinery as the frozen-suite analysis — a point estimate above delta_sealed is not a pass; (2) the frozen-vs-sealed secondary band derives from >=2 MOCK SEALED RELEASES per production-recipe class graded on the calibration anchors during P3-E-CAL — frozen-suite seed/prompt variance cannot calibrate producer/difficulty shift; (3) fresh-facts leg chronology: architecture+store freeze FIRST, then the sealed release publishes a pinned COMMON fresh-evidence snapshot; every arm receives the same snapshot and the same metered refresh budget (ingest/update/reindex inside the measured window, costs logged symmetrically into KOT-LIFE/1 staleness-refresh and co-registered with delta_sealed); live search is enabled for every arm under the same budget or for none; supplying fresh evidence to one arm only is an oracle channel voiding the release; if no refresh mechanism is affordable the fresh-facts leg is dropped for that release and the claim says so; store-vs-live-search remedy and producer-side refresh-cost disclosure are P3-D-SEAL registered decisions with no asymmetric charging.
rationale: Draft 1's sealed gate used a point-estimate margin and did not say whether all frontier comparators run sealed; the fresh-facts leg froze stores before sealing yet required temporal freshness without saying who gets new evidence or whose refresh cost is charged — the review and STORE.md require an uncertainty-controlled bound and an explicit, symmetric evidence/refresh protocol.
notes: Registered by the MF0 draft-2 revision (GPT-5.6 review rev-dMF0-20260711 applied); fresh ASM-0880..0909 block, append-only. Renumbered twice pre-citation: three concurrent Phase-1 review-revision writers collided on the 0820 block and the FRONT/1 revision holds 0850+.

claim: Decontamination adjudication is fail-closed with NO sampling path (rev-dMF0 'the decontamination gate can clear unreviewed flags'): EVERY kot-decon/1 flagged pair is human-adjudicated {benign-collision, leakage}; an unadjudicated flag COUNTS AS LEAKAGE. If flag volume exceeds adjudication capacity, either the thresholds were mis-calibrated (recalibrate on planted positives/negatives, screener version bump, re-run) or the store is not decontaminatable at that size and dependent wins are void. Adjudication hours are logged into KOT-LIFE/1 review lines. Any leakage adjudication on a store record voids wins that store touches.
rationale: Draft 1 reviewed only a stratified sample plus the top decile when >500 pairs flagged, while calling any leakage win-voiding; unreviewed flags cannot be treated as benign in a win-voiding gate, and the honest alternatives are full adjudication or a pre-registered statistically valid leakage-rate gate — full adjudication is chosen as the simpler fail-closed rule.
notes: Registered by the MF0 draft-2 revision (GPT-5.6 review rev-dMF0-20260711 applied); fresh ASM-0880..0909 block, append-only. Renumbered twice pre-citation: three concurrent Phase-1 review-revision writers collided on the 0820 block and the FRONT/1 revision holds 0850+.

claim: P3-E-CAL statistical corrections (rev-dMF0 ranked concern 3a + 'k>=3 is too weak' + 'reference-ceiling clamping can destroy W1 discrimination'): (1) the separation gate compares the three same-family SmolLM2 anchors on ONE COMMON index — one fixed component set (the R-1 membership) scored under one protocol identically for all three models — never 'rung-appropriate' scalars across rungs; anchor size-ordering is the closest available known-answer within one family/recipe lineage but is treated as a strong instrument DIAGNOSTIC, not ground truth: a fail blocks the freeze and triggers instrument-vs-anchor localisation; (2) variance sources are estimated separately — item-sampling (hierarchical bootstrap within benchmarks), decoding randomness (seed replicates, zero for deterministic MC-logprob components), run-to-run harness variance (repeated identical runs) — with prompt-order permutations reported as a deterministic systematic-sensitivity span, never pooled as independent replicates; replicate counts are POWERED from a pilot against a registered precision target on SD_cal, k>=3 being a floor not a sufficiency claim; (3) SATURATION GATE — no calibration anchor at the rung may clamp to 1.0 on any in-scalar component; a saturating component's ceiling is re-estimated headroom-preserving (or the component reports unclamped in the scalar) before freeze, and any W1 component cell where S and a comparator both clamp is reported saturated-and-non-discriminative.
rationale: Draft 1 compared rung-appropriate indices across rungs in violation of its own section 2.7, treated model size as a known-answer ordering, pooled deterministic prompt-order permutations with seeds at k>=3 (too weak to estimate SD_cal), and had no defence against reference-ceiling saturation destroying W1 discrimination.
notes: Registered by the MF0 draft-2 revision (GPT-5.6 review rev-dMF0-20260711 applied); fresh ASM-0880..0909 block, append-only. Renumbered twice pre-citation: three concurrent Phase-1 review-revision writers collided on the 0820 block and the FRONT/1 revision holds 0850+.

claim: Generic-RAG comparator equity rule (rev-dMF0 'the generic-RAG control remains under-specified', consuming the landed RAG.md): the frontier clause (iii) comparator is built from the SAME pinned source-evidence snapshot under the SAME byte/build/query budgets as the kernel arm, but NOT necessarily the kernel's own retriever/index — forcing a specialized retriever onto a conventional RAG baseline can structurally handicap it, and 'same source snapshot' is not 'same retriever' (converting one snapshot into passages vs records vs triples indexes different objects). The RAG comparator is implemented from a pinned STANDARD harness (FlashRAG-style, release pinned), with both BM25 and dense retriever cells mandatory, retrieval recall@k reported separately from end-task accuracy, and identical index-bytes/build-compute/query-compute ledgers on both arms; the identical-vs-architecture-specific retriever decision is made per factorial cell by P3-D-RAGC under FRONT/1 section 5's binding default rules.
backing_ref: docs/next/design/MF0.md section 8.2; docs/next/lit/RAG.md sections 3 and 6; docs/next/design/FRONT.md section 5; poc/gpt56-review/rev-dMF0-20260711/last-message.json
rationale: Draft 1 inherited the parent's 'same corpus, same retriever/index' phrasing, which the landed RAG review shows can handicap the conventional baseline; the literature supports equal source evidence + equal budgets + a pinned standard implementation, with retriever-architecture parity decided per cell.
notes: Registered by the MF0 draft-2 revision (GPT-5.6 review rev-dMF0-20260711 applied); fresh ASM-0880..0909 block, append-only. Renumbered twice pre-citation: three concurrent Phase-1 review-revision writers collided on the 0820 block and the FRONT/1 revision holds 0850+.

claim: Factorial attribution controls are FRAMEWORK-owned and specified in MF0 section 8.5 (rev-dMF0 'the parent's mandatory factorial content x retrieval x executor controls are mentioned but never actually specified'): the cell inventory restates programme doc section 2.2(2) verbatim — (a) seed-pinned deranged store-to-item addressing, (b) label permutation within records, (c) irrelevant-but-structurally-matched records, (d) edge/relation shuffling for graph arms, (e) aligned-non-kernel store, (f) matched generic-RAG/tool-use comparator plus BM25/dense/position-shuffle/random-document cells, (g) kernel-as-text null at matched token budget — organised on the content-type x retrieval-architecture x executor axes, each cell annotated with what it discriminates. The EXECUTOR axis, which the parent names without enumerating, gets two DRAFT cells owned here: engine-on vs engine-off (same store, execution disabled) and generic-executor substitution where meaningful; P3-D-RAGC/P3-D-ORACLE freeze them. Every Phase-2 W1-relevant prereg NAMES which cells it instantiates and what each discriminates; the full cross product is never mandatory, the named-cells rule is.
rationale: The review found the mandatory factorial controls were referenced but never specified in the framework document; the framework must own the specification so individual experiments cannot re-derive (and quietly weaken) it, and the executor axis needed at least draft cells to be instantiable.
notes: Registered by the MF0 draft-2 revision (GPT-5.6 review rev-dMF0-20260711 applied); fresh ASM-0880..0909 block, append-only. Renumbered twice pre-citation: three concurrent Phase-1 review-revision writers collided on the 0820 block and the FRONT/1 revision holds 0850+.

claim: P3-D-POWER corrected G1 bound + kill-soundness contract (supersedes-in-part the raw-scale Delta_max statement inside ASM-0817 and the unregistered in-doc proposals of POWER.md revision 1). The G1 ceiling is computed on the chance/ceiling-normalized KOT-AI-INDEX/2 scale over EVERY component of the frozen index pin: a censused, Form-A-eligible accuracy component contributes W_b*min(cap_b, kappa_b*(1-a_b_cov)/span_b); every uncensused component, every component whose metric has no registered max-gain functional, and every weighted domain with no enumerated components contributes its FULL HEADROOM (effectively kappa_UB=1, a_cov_LB=0, capped at the benchmark's normalized-score headroom cap_b) — no component is ever silently assigned zero possible gain. A missing covered-slice baseline is imputed the distribution-free lower bound a_cov=0 (never chance: below-chance baselines exist and deliberately wrong predictions are perfectly correctable). span_b is lower-bounded via the registered ceiling uncertainty. The kill quantity is a SIMULTANEOUS one-sided 95% upper bound: all stochastic intervals taken in one run share a Bonferroni-split alpha (each one-sided at 0.05/m), so by the union bound and monotonicity simUCB95(Delta_max)=sum of per-term UBs is a valid family-wise 95% upper bound; per-term unadjusted 95% bounds are NOT accepted as a joint bound. Kill iff simUCB95(Delta_max) < delta_k.
backing_ref: docs/next/design/POWER.md sections 1.2, 1.4, 3.1-3.3; poc/gpt56-review/rev-dPOWER-20260711/last-message.json blocking findings 1-3; docs/next/lit/EVAL.md section 5.3
rationale: The independent GPT-5.6 review demonstrated the revision-1 rig could issue FALSE KILLS three ways: censused-only summation silently assigned zero gain to every other index component; a_cov=chance imputation understates the ceiling (models can score below chance); and summing separate per-benchmark 95% Wilson bounds does not preserve 95% joint coverage. Each defect pushed Delta_max DOWN, violating the upper-bound direction the kill's soundness rests on. This contract restores the invariant that every approximation inflates Delta_max, making a fired kill sound.
notes: Implemented in poc/p3-power/power.py (whole-index iteration, full-headroom imputation with flags, integer-count inputs, Bonferroni z via NormalDist.inv_cdf). The revision-1 live define-lane kill (0.0038 < 0.03) is WITHDRAWN under this contract; the corrected run reports simUCB95 ~ 0.81 ILLUSTRATIVE-ONLY.

claim: P3-D-POWER metric-type and family-eligibility contract. (1) METRICS: the itemwise perfect-correction functional kappa*(1-a_cov) is registered for itemwise-accuracy metrics ONLY (the chance/ceiling normalization transform is itself defined only for accuracy-type metrics, per the EVAL review); every index component with any other metric type (loss, logprob, generation-scored, calibration, ...) receives full headroom W_b*cap_b until a per-metric maximum-gain functional with a registered score range is added — it is never forced through the accuracy formula and never dropped. The index pin MUST declare metric_type per component; the rig fails closed without it. (2) FAMILIES: Form A may be applied to a mechanism family only with a REGISTERED noninterference/covered-support argument (behaviour on uncovered items identical to baseline; entire incremental benefit requires a covered, correctable item), cited via family_eligibility.noninterference_ref in the census input. Plausible for fail-closed verify/retry (H-VL) and executor-derived masking with fail-closed fallback (H-RULE-CD) but NOT established for KV injection, adapters, train-time program-synthesis variants, or H-DD weight surgery, which may change uncovered behaviour or transfer knowledge globally: those get no free kill and no free pass from this rig until they carry their own registered argument or a different ceiling design.
backing_ref: docs/next/design/POWER.md sections 1.2, 1.2a; poc/gpt56-review/rev-dPOWER-20260711/last-message.json 'Other substantive gaps' items 1-2; docs/next/lit/EVAL.md section 6 (accuracy-only transform)
rationale: The review flagged that revision 1 asserted Form A covers all store-dependent families and all index metrics without support. A mechanism with off-coverage or global effects can exceed the covered-item bound, and non-accuracy metrics have no chance floor or analogous verified transform, so applying the accuracy functional to either would be an invalid generalization that can false-kill. Restricting the functional's registered scope and requiring explicit eligibility arguments keeps the ceiling an actual upper bound.
notes: Proposed bead P3-D-ELIG authors the eligibility references. SUPPORTED_METRICS=('accuracy',) in power.py is the registered functional list; extending it is a design change requiring a new ASM or an amendment.

claim: P3-D-POWER kappa input: the oracle-coverage census, two-tier protocol, and lane discipline. The kappa consumed by Delta_max is oracle-parse best-endorsement checkability, NOT the mapper-parse lane (which folds in the NL-wall parse gap the oracle diagnostic deliberately sits above). Lane ordering: mapper-parse kappa <= oracle kappa <= lemma-touch upper sieve. Protocol: (a) the LEMMA-TOUCH UPPER SIEVE is the mechanical, genuinely cheap Tier-0 pass (strictly over-counts coverage, so kills fired under it are coverage-sound with zero annotation); (b) the EXACT ORACLE CENSUS is a HUMAN-ANNOTATION instrument — not a ~$0 CPU pass — with a registered per-item schema {item_id, benchmark_rev, gold_parse_sketch, derivable_under_best_endorsement: yes|no|unknown, records_touched, annotator_id, adjudication_id}, double annotation on a preregistered subsample with inter-annotator agreement reported and disagreements adjudicated, ambiguity policy unknown=>covered (fail toward headroom, never toward a kill), and a registered residual label false-negative allowance label_fn_ub consumed by the uncertainty model. A kill fired under mapper-parse kappa is CONTINGENT on the oracle census (or sieve); a kill fired under oracle/upper-sieve kappa is UNCONDITIONAL.
backing_ref: docs/next/design/POWER.md section 2.1; poc/gpt56-review/rev-dPOWER-20260711/last-message.json 'The oracle census is not yet a concrete CPU pass'; registry/assessments/b-cov-define-lane.json (lane separation, internal 0.7710 gold vs external 0.0000 mapper)
rationale: The review established that existing third-party benchmark data has no gold-parse lane, that producing gold semantic parses and best-endorsement derivability judgements requires substantial human annotation, and that the annotation itself can introduce false-negative coverage labels that neither Wilson intervals nor exhaustive counting capture. Splitting the instrument into a mechanical over-counting sieve (cheap, kill-sound) and a budgeted annotated census (exact, with an audited label-error allowance and fail-toward-coverage ambiguity rule) preserves the zero-GPU kill path without pretending the exact census is free.
notes: Resolution path: beads P3-X-SIEVE-CENSUS (Tier-0) then P3-X-ORACLE-CENSUS (annotation-budgeted). label_fn_ub=0.0 is permitted only for a deterministic mechanical classifier (e.g. the mapper lane); any human-labelled census must register a nonzero allowance from its adjudicated subsample.

claim: Abstention-scoring dependency edge (adopts round1-critique-synthesis section B.2.3 as a registered dependency). The firewall bound Form B — Delta_max^B = sum_b W_b_abstain * (share of baseline errors on b that are covered contradictions) * g_abstain — is UNCOMPUTABLE until P3-D-INDEX registers the abstention-scoring transform (the per-correct-abstention credit g_abstain). Therefore: any firewall/selective-mechanism family's G1 (Form B) and G4 are BLOCKED on that P3-D-INDEX decision; the P3-D-INDEX freeze must register abstention scoring BEFORE any selective-mechanism G1 or G4 prereg freezes; on an accuracy-only index Form B == 0 and the firewall's index-mover claim is dead by construction. The rig implements this as a fail-closed refusal (ERR_FORM_B_UNREGISTERED), never as a silent Form-A fallback.
backing_ref: docs/next/design/POWER.md section 1.3; docs/next/arch/round1-critique-synthesis.md sections A.3, B.2.3; docs/next/lit/EVAL.md section 8 open question 1
rationale: A pure claim-firewall cannot add correct answers; its index gain exists only under a scoring rule that credits correct abstention, which is an open normative P3-D-INDEX choice. Computing Form B under a guessed credit would smuggle an unregistered scoring decision into a kill/survive verdict; refusing until the transform is registered keeps the dependency explicit and auditable.
notes: The existing P3-D-FIREWALL bead carries the design work; this ASM is the registered edge. power.py --form B exits 2 with ERR_FORM_B_UNREGISTERED.

claim: The P3-D-POWER G1 decision rule, verbatim (POWER.md section 4). Step 0 VERDICT ELIGIBILITY (all required, else the run is ILLUSTRATIVE-ONLY and emits NO verdict): frozen index pin (frozen:true); complete census pin block (model_checkpoint, harness_hash, prompt_policy, seeds, store_hash, kernel_hash, world_layer_hash, mapper_hash, grammar_version, benchmark_revisions); declared coverage_lane and census_mode with label_fn_ub or a registered sampling frame; a registered Form-A family-eligibility reference (or, for Form B, a registered abstention-scoring transform). Steps 1-3: read kappa from the oracle-coverage census and integer correct_cov/n_cov baselines; compute simUCB95(Delta_max) per the ASM-0820 contract over the WHOLE index, scalar and per-domain; KILL the index-mover claim for family F at rung k iff simUCB95(Delta_max^F) < delta_k (domain-scoped: iff simUCB95 < delta_k^d for every store-touched domain, that domain fully censused). Step 4: mapper-parse-lane kills are CONTINGENT on the oracle census; oracle/upper-sieve kills are UNCONDITIONAL. Step 5 LICENSING: the bound is an oracle-diagnostic (ASM-0814 sense) — a kill licenses only 'no mechanism can move the index-mover claim for F at rung k by delta_k on this suite', makes no W1 claim, and does not touch F's value as a scoped instrument; SURVIVAL licenses nothing except proceeding to G2 and to sizing. The G4 primary the sizing bead must implement is LCB95(INDEX(S)-INDEX(C)) > delta_k with FWER control across the F(B_k) comparator set and a hierarchical paired bootstrap over items/prompts/seeds (suite never resampled as a benchmark-universe sample); sizing requires paired outcome variance/correlation, prompt and seed variance components, comparator multiplicity, and the exact bootstrap hierarchy — counts plus Delta are insufficient, and no G4 prereg may cite a sample size before the P3-D-POWER-SIZE rig exists and is calibrated.
backing_ref: docs/next/design/POWER.md sections 4, 5; docs/next/programme-3-neurosymbolic-architecture.md sections 2.5, 7.1; poc/gpt56-review/rev-dPOWER-20260711/last-message.json (verdict + 'sizing deliverable is not implemented')
rationale: The review required withdrawing the 'complete/runnable G1 deliverable' language and gating any real kill on the frozen pin, complete census pins, eligibility registration, and a sound statistical construction; and it found the sizing half was a sketch misdescribed as implemented. Registering the rule verbatim with an explicit eligibility step makes the kill mechanically auditable and prevents a rerun of the withdrawn revision-1 kill; registering the sizing requirements prevents a G4 prereg citing sizes the rig cannot yet compute.
notes: Adjudicates programme kill K-P3v2(1) (ASM-0818): the completeness rule cuts both ways — K-P3v2(1) cannot fire off a partial census either. Sizing implementation is the proposed P3-D-POWER-SIZE bead.

claim: P3-D-POWER census/rung pin schema and KOT-FAIR/2 resource accounting. (1) PIN: a verdict-eligible census input must carry the full pin block {model_checkpoint, harness_hash, prompt_policy, seeds, store_hash, kernel_hash, world_layer_hash, mapper_hash, grammar_version, benchmark_revisions} plus enforced enums coverage_lane in {mapper-parse, oracle, upper-sieve} and census_mode in {exhaustive, sampled}; a free-text rung string is not a pin. No cross-rung, cross-store, or cross-suite reuse of a census or Delta_max verdict unless every pin field matches; any store/kernel hash change invalidates kappa. (2) RESOURCE ACCOUNTING: coverage is store-version- and budget-dependent, so a G1 run pins the canonically packed store/kernel artifacts (the rig echoes the hashes into its output) and the programme distinguishes three ceilings: (a) Delta_max under the current admissible B_k store (the measured computation); (b) Delta_max under a preregistered maximum store-growth envelope (an input scenario, always labelled, never a measurement); (c) the cost of reaching kappa* — bytes, authoring/review/indexing human-hours, and B_k admissibility under KOT-LIFE/1. A naked kappa* value is NOT an actionable coverage target: the ingestion plan must attach (c) before kappa* is cited in any prereg, and the rig prints this restriction on every output.
backing_ref: docs/next/design/POWER.md sections 2.2, 3.5; poc/gpt56-review/rev-dPOWER-20260711/last-message.json ('The 100M-2B pinning is incomplete' + 'KOT-FAIR/2 and resource accounting')
rationale: The review showed the revision-1 input schema carried only a free-text rung and lacked checkpoint/harness/prompt/seed/store hashes and metric typing, making cross-rung reuse unsafe, and that kappa* was presented without bytes, construction cost, or B_k admissibility. Pinning the full tuple and splitting the three ceilings keeps every coverage number attached to the exact artifacts and budget it was measured under.
notes: Implemented as verdict_eligibility() + CENSUS_PIN_FIELDS in power.py; store-pin echo rides the JSON output. Fresh block 0820-0849 opened for P3-D-POWER revision 2; 0826-0849 remain free.

claim: NLB/1 role and scope (P3-D-NLB, bead kernel-of-truth-s55r.13, the FK-NLB-3 re-entry). (1) NLB is the shared GATE INSTRUMENT for the natural-language boundary: P3-E-NLB-1 must clear the joint G-NLB gate (ASM-0901) PER VERTICAL before ANY natural-input store use by any family (ASM-0814 verbatim); the two verticals gate independently (the FK-NLB-1 precedent). (2) The front-end under test is NLB-FE/1, a TWO-TIER parser+abstention SYSTEM: Tier-0 = repaired deterministic template/trie fast path (exact-match acceptance only, ROLE_DIR-repaired, fail-closed on ambiguous orientation); Tier-1 = a small learned parser (<=360M) under grammar-constrained decoding with a contract-and-contrast acceptance layer and a frozen selective-prediction threshold. (3) Fail-closed invariants, frozen as design law: the deterministic engine is the SOLE answer authority; the parser is an UNTRUSTED PROPOSER; no surface answer is emitted except from an engine execution of an ACCEPTED parse; any input failing acceptance (either tier) yields ABSTAIN or CLARIFY, never a guessed answer; the whole risk-coverage curve is the measured product and the shipped operating point is one frozen threshold on it. (4) The instrument makes NO index claim: P3-E-NLB-1 is exempt from P3-E-CAL and the G4 block per the programme sequencing exemption; a G-NLB PASS licenses ONLY the unblocking of NL store legs, never a W1 or usefulness claim.
rationale: The measured NL-boundary FAILs (l3a-parse 47.6 percent retention, a5-nl 41.6 percent + S2 fired) were produced by a verbatim-lexicon no-learning front-end; the PARSE lit-review's net judgement is capability-limited-as-leading-hypothesis with the strongest matched-accounting recipe being fine-tuned-small-parser + grammar-constrained decode + calibrated abstention, and the two-tier microsecond/millisecond shape is the cost envelope the review recommends designing first. Making the engine the sole answer authority and the parser an untrusted proposer is the direct structural answer to the a5-nl wrong-with-provenance failure.
notes: Fresh block ASM-0900-0919 opened for P3-D-NLB; 0907-0919 remain free. Registered by docs/next/design/NLB.md.

claim: G-NLB, the exact P3-E-NLB-1 success gate, per vertical, all clauses jointly required. (1) RETENTION: retained covered exactness (engine-exact answers on covered scored items under blind NL phrasing, over the gold-parse ceiling of the SAME items) must have one-sided 95 percent lower confidence bound >= 0.90; FAIL fires when the upper bound < 0.90; between = INCONCLUSIVE (the l3a-parse three-way shape retained). (2) S2 SAFETY, defined precisely as UNCONDITIONAL risk: P(answered AND wrong) over covered scored items; gate leg UB95 < 0.02; kill leg LB95 >= 0.02 (the a5-nl S2 shape retained); the SELECTIVE risk P(wrong | answered) is a mandatory CO-REPORT (the two diverge exactly when coverage drops) but the binding bound is unconditional, because retention already punishes over-abstention. (3) STRATUM SAFETY: every directional frame x orientation stratum with pre-registered minimum support carries a near-zero wrong allowance (stratum kill boundary pinned at freeze from the power arithmetic); an aggregate pass with a failing directional stratum is a FAIL. (4) CREDITABLE ABSTENTION: items independently annotated ambiguous/unanswerable BEFORE scoring are scored correct on ABSTAIN/CLARIFY and wrong on any answer; they sit outside the retention denominator and inside the safety accounting. (5) STATISTICS: with K>1 phrasings per base query, all intervals are cluster-robust over base queries (pre-registered cluster bootstrap); exact integer boundaries recomputed and pinned in the frozen record before any scored run (the l3a-parse boundary-pinning precedent); S1 control fail-closed rides in the same Holm family as S2, per the parent instruments. (6) PER-VERTICAL: family/world and code gate independently; clearing one vertical unblocks NL store legs on THAT vertical only.
backing_ref: docs/next/design/NLB.md section 4; registry/verdicts/l3a-parse.json; registry/verdicts/a5-nl.json; docs/next/lit/PARSE.md section 8 requirements (1)-(7)
rationale: The PARSE gate-instrument requirements demand an exact S2 definition (unconditional vs selective diverge under falling coverage), pre-specified sample-size/confidence rules, systematic-error strata with near-zero allowances rather than average risk, and creditable abstention on annotated ambiguity; retaining the parent instruments' Wilson/Holm shapes and three-way reading keeps the successor commensurable with the measured FAILs it re-enters.
notes: Consumed by K-P3v2(2)/(5) (ASM-0818) and by every ASM-0814 NL-leg unblock. Boundaries are pinned at prereg-freeze by the mechanical analysis script, never post hoc.

claim: NLB-EVAL/1, the gate corpus protocol, per vertical. (1) BASE ITEMS: the frozen covered scored query sets of the parent instruments (l3a 527 over 7 in-scope families; a5 855) plus matched control sets; shape-ambiguous families stay carved out per the parents. (2) PHRASINGS: K=3 blind held-out phrasings per eval query (upgrading the parents' K=1, which was below field practice), authored under the parents' blind fresh-identity protocol. (3) FOUR-WAY SEPARATION with DISJOINT SOURCES: TRAIN (synthetic grammar-recombination paraphrases + canonical templates over fresh identities only - no eval query, no blind corpus, no eval author-source), DEV/model-selection (designer-authored fresh identities), CAL (calibration + threshold selection: author-source S1 over a pre-registered ~40 percent query split), EVAL (final: DISJOINT author-source S2 over the remaining ~60 percent, authored AFTER the front-end pin). (4) FREEZE ORDERING, hash-witnessed: train and pin parser weights + grammar mask -> author CAL -> select and freeze threshold -> pin the full front-end content-hash -> author EVAL blind -> single scored run. (5) ADVERSARIAL STRATA: both orientations of every directional frame at pre-registered minimum support per orientation per source; a Dr.Spider-style perturbation suite (synonym / structure / typo-noise) with per-perturbation worst-case reported; an independent ambiguity/answerability annotation pass before scoring; the descriptive no-label synonym probe retained. (6) INSTRUMENT VALIDITY: the parents' phrasing-corpus lints + recoverability audit gate (>= 0.95 strict recovery by a human/LLM auditor from the phrasing alone, else the corpus, not the parser, is repaired) apply to every authored set. (7) LEGACY CORPORA: the committed K=1 l3a-parse/a5-nl phrasing corpora are DIAGNOSTIC-ONLY forever after (outcomes are public in verdicts); no successor gate may score on them and no parser may train on them.
backing_ref: docs/next/design/NLB.md section 5; docs/design-nl-boundary-l3a-parse-a5-nl.md sections 4-5 (FK-NLB-8/9 + recoverability gate); docs/next/lit/PARSE.md sections 6.5, 8
rationale: K=1 single-source phrasing was the parents' weakest instrument property; the PARSE review requires K>1 disjoint-source phrasings, four-way data separation, both-orientation directional tests, perturbation suites, and independent ambiguity annotation, and the conformal/calibration literature voids any guarantee under undisclosed phrasing-source shift - source-disjoint CAL vs EVAL makes the shift stress measurable instead of silent. The freeze ordering generalizes the parents' pin-before-authoring discipline to a learned front-end.
notes: Corpus sizes: ~1,581 scored phrasings (l3a) and ~2,565 (a5) at K=3 before strata additions; exact counts and boundaries pinned at freeze.

claim: NLB/1 threshold-freeze and risk-control protocol. (1) The abstention threshold tau is selected ONCE, on the CAL split only, by risk-controlled selection: the largest coverage whose one-sided upper confidence bound (1-delta = 0.95, exact binomial/Hoeffding per the pinned script) on unconditional dangerous-wrong risk is <= 0.02; tau is then FROZEN into the front-end content-hash before any EVAL phrasing exists. (2) The confidence score backing tau is itself a pinned design object (candidate classes: parser sequence/label probability, trained calibrator on DEV, conformal nonconformity score); the chosen class is fixed at freeze and the risk-coverage curve + AUACC over the whole threshold range is a mandatory report alongside the operating point. (3) SHIFT STRESS is a pre-registered secondary: the CAL-source-vs-EVAL-source risk delta at frozen tau is reported as the measured validity of the calibration guarantee under phrasing-source shift (exchangeability does not hold across sources by construction; the guarantee is only claimed for the calibration distribution and the EVAL read measures its degradation). (4) INVERSION SEPARATION is a pre-registered secondary: the score's discrimination (AUROC + score distributions) between direction-flipped wrong parses and correct parses on the both-orientation strata - measuring, not assuming, whether the score separates the one class where confidence is known to fail (consistent confident inversion); the S2 budget for that class is carried by the deterministic repair and the contract layer, never by the threshold alone.
backing_ref: docs/next/design/NLB.md section 6; docs/next/lit/PARSE.md section 4 (risk-coverage/conformal frame + the conformal-abstention consistent-inversion caveat, kamath-2020 shift degradation)
rationale: Selective prediction supplies the exact S2-shaped contract (set desired risk, abstain to guarantee it w.h.p.), but every such guarantee is calibration-distribution-bound and the literature's own caveat is that confidence scores can fail exactly on systematic high-confidence inversions; freezing the threshold pre-EVAL prevents threshold shopping, and promoting shift stress and inversion separation to pre-registered secondaries turns the two known failure modes of the guarantee into measured quantities.
notes: delta and the exact bound family are pinned in the P3-E-NLB-1 prereg; nothing here licenses quoting the CAL guarantee as an EVAL-distribution guarantee.

claim: NLB-0, the design-phase go/no-go pilot, and redesign-cycle accounting. (1) CYCLE ACCOUNTING: the N=2 pre-registered redesign cycles counted by K-P3v2(2)/(5) are FROZEN P3-E-NLB-1 records (prereg-freeze -> scored run -> verdict); NLB-0 is a design-phase DIAGNOSTIC that consumes no cycle, at most ONE NLB-0 per vertical per design revision, and its results can never substitute for the gate. (2) NLB-0-A (safety leg, ~USD 0, CPU): implement the a5 assessment's deterministic ROLE_DIR frame-layer repair (fail closed on ambiguous orientation) + exhaustive both-orientation table tests, re-run the committed legacy a5-nl phrasing corpus as a labelled diagnostic; PROCEED requires point dangerous-wrong <= 4/855 with retention not below the measured 0.416 minus 0.02; a persisting dangerous-wrong class under the deterministic repair is NO-GO for the current design (the S2 budget claim fails at source). (3) NLB-0-B (retention leg, <= ~5 GPU-h free pool + <= ~USD 25 API for synthetic paraphrases): train the Tier-1 pilot parser on TRAIN/DEV material only (never on any blind corpus), freeze a pilot operating point in advance, evaluate once on the legacy K=1 corpora as a labelled diagnostic; GO to building the full NLB-EVAL/1 instrument and freezing P3-E-NLB-1 requires, on BOTH verticals, pilot retention point >= 0.80 at an operating point with dangerous-wrong point <= 0.01; below that, the recipe class lacks the headroom for a 0.90 lower-bound gate and the design returns to the drawing board WITHOUT burning a registered cycle, with the negative branch (LLM-assisted parsing at API cost, or scope-narrowing to syntax-grounded formal/code domains) escalated to the maintainer. (4) DISCLOSURE: both legs are post-outcome analyses of corpora whose scored outcomes are already public in the parent verdicts; every NLB-0 artifact carries the diagnostic label and the parent-verdict provenance.
backing_ref: docs/next/design/NLB.md section 7; registry/assessments/a5-nl.json (questions_opened: a5-nl-frame-direction-repair, a5-nl-llm-successor); docs/next/programme-3-neurosymbolic-architecture.md section 7.1 K-P3v2(2)
rationale: The programme kill counts redesign cycles, so what constitutes a cycle must be mechanical before the first attempt - otherwise pilots either silently burn the budget or silently extend it; the two-leg pilot is the cheapest experiment that decides go/no-go because it tests the two independent failure budgets (S2 controllability at deterministic source; retention headroom of the learned recipe) on already-paid-for corpora at ~USD 25 + free-pool GPU, before the ~1,800-phrasing instrument build and the registered attempt are spent.
notes: The 0.80/0.01 pilot thresholds are design-phase decision rules, not gate statistics; they are deliberately point-estimate-based because the pilot is a headroom read, not a verdict.

claim: NLB/1 Tier-1 parser-class ordering and decoding discipline. (1) PRIMARY parser class: joint intent+slot (frame classification + gazetteer-constrained slot filling + directional-role classification) at 110-360M, because kot-query/1 and kot-query-code/1 are closed grammars of few shape-recoverable families with registered lexicons - structurally an intent+slot task, not open text-to-SQL. (2) SECONDARY arm (same harness): seq2seq generation of the DSL under grammar-constrained decoding. (3) DECODING DISCIPLINE: any constrained decoder used in a scored arm must first pass an exact LOCAL-vs-GLOBAL mask equivalence check on the full closed grammar (divergence quantified and pinned; feasible because the grammar is tiny), per the PARSE non-recommendation against assuming local masking is faithful. (4) The deterministic ROLE_DIR repair and the both-orientation table tests apply REGARDLESS of parser class (Tier-0 and Tier-1 share the repaired frame semantics); trie-constrained entity emission maps only registered gazetteer names. (5) CONTRACT-AND-CONTRAST acceptance rides as pre-registered arms on the same risk-coverage harness: confidence-only vs confidence+contracts (IR-level deterministic operator inversion) vs ambiguity-set execution (enumerate competing role/direction parses, execute all on the microsecond engine, answer only on denotation agreement); the arm comparison is itself a deliverable (which acceptance layer dominates the S2 dimension of the curve).
backing_ref: docs/next/design/NLB.md sections 3, 5; docs/next/lit/PARSE.md sections 2, 4, 5c, 8; docs/next/arch/round1-critique-synthesis.md sections A.2, A.7, B.2.5
rationale: The PARSE review's difficulty prior places our grammars in the intent+slot corner where the field's numbers are highest, names grammar-constrained decoding the cheapest accuracy lever with a specific local-mask fidelity caveat our tiny grammar can check exactly, and the round-1 synthesis merged the contract layer and ambiguity-set executor into the front-end as the independent checks for exactly the class (consistent confident inversion) that confidence thresholds cannot carry.
notes: Model shortlist (e.g. 110M encoder vs SmolLM2-135M decoder) is pinned at P3-E-NLB-1 prereg with tuning-compute symmetry logged per ASM-0812; parser bytes and latency are charged to any consuming system under KOT-SIZE/2 + KOT-COST/2.

claim: Forward projection (never a premise): a two-tier front-end - repaired deterministic fast path + fine-tuned <=360M parser + grammar-constrained decoding + contract/ambiguity-set acceptance + frozen risk-controlled abstention - can jointly clear G-NLB (retention LB >= 0.90 AND unconditional dangerous-wrong UB < 0.02) on at least one of the two verticals. No cited work reports retention-at-S2-bound for a small parser on a comparable closed grammar; the PARSE review rates capability-limited as the leading hypothesis for the dominant failure classes but explicitly unresolved at the programme gate.
resolution_path: NLB-0 pilot (ASM-0904) decides go/no-go on headroom; P3-E-NLB-1 under the G-NLB gate (ASM-0901) on the NLB-EVAL/1 corpus (ASM-0902) resolves it; two failed frozen redesign cycles fire K-P3v2(2) and the projection dies with the competitiveness programme's NL branch.
notes: Registered so the design's optimism is a tracked object, not an implicit premise; every load-bearing line in NLB.md rests on MEASURED/STIPULATED tags only.

claim: P3-D-RAGC contract and scope: the matched generic-RAG/tool-use control is a CLOSED, NAMED arm inventory -- GR-A shared/matched generic-RAG (BM25+dense sub-cells; REQUIRED F(B_k) member and primary attribution control whenever S uses a store), GR-B native generic-RAG (additional frontier point), GR-C generic tool-use/generic-executor, GR-D matched retry/search with a generic acceptance signal, R0 retrieval-off floor -- plus perturbation cells PS (position-shuffle), RD (random-document, control-only), OR (oracle-retrieval, oracle-diagnostic only, never F(B_k), no W1 claim). Every consuming prereg names instantiated arms/cells with per-cell discrimination claims and claim licences. Gate placement: parity diagnostics feed G1 (census-class, P3-E-CAL-exempt); OR is G2 oracle-diagnostic; the GR set is mandatory at every store-touching G4/W1 experiment. The control consumes natural benchmark text natively and is NEVER NLB-gated. Generators are the rung anchors pinned by P3-D-BASE across the 100M-2B span.
backing_ref: docs/next/design/RAGC.md sections 0-1; executes ASM-0812 cell (f) + ASM-0890/0891; inherits ASM-0808/0814/0817 discipline
rationale: The f2b assessment shows the one audited end-task sign is consistent with aligned-answer-key+retry and had no generic-RAG arm; a single vague 'RAG baseline' cannot decompose a win, so the control must be a closed inventory of named arms each isolating one axis, with the oracle and NL-integrity rules preventing the control from being weakened to share the kernel's measured NL wall.
notes: Block ASM-0920-0939 assigned to P3-D-RAGC (bead kernel-of-truth-s55r.15); 0928-0939 remain free.

claim: RAGC Rule-1 execution (source-content derivation + information parity): one pinned derivation matrix D per vertical -- kernel records via the standing mint pipeline version; passages via a pinned chunker (proposed 256-token/stride-128, sentence-snapped; params tunable on dev within the arm's T_k meter, then pinned); triples via deterministic extraction where structure permits (the measured a5 route) else one pinned open IE extractor; tool schemas via a deterministic schema-to-function generator. Byte-cap parity: each arm's packed indexed objects + index + embedder + executor bytes <= the same store_bytes(B_k), measured by the one canonical packing script; slack may not be banked. Information parity beyond FRONT/1's four checks is MEASURED, not certified, by two pre-registered diagnostics: P5 decisive-span coverage per arm (blind power-sized annotation; per-arm kappa vector, doubling as the G1 census input) and P6 oracle-delivery answerable-fraction probe (oracle-diagnostic). A pinned parity band eps_par (proposed 0.05 absolute on kappa) applies fail-closed: out-of-band comparisons have their attribution sentence DOWNGRADED to 'confounded by derivation coverage' while frontier membership survives.
backing_ref: docs/next/design/RAGC.md section 2; inherits ASM-0853 Rule 1 verbatim
rationale: The RAG lit-review found no published precedent for cross-representation information parity; certifying it is impossible (different object types change the retrieval problem), so the honest executable form is equal snapshot + equal byte cap + equal metered derivation effort + measured coverage/consumption diagnostics with a pre-registered downgrade rule instead of a silent pass.
notes: P5 annotation is costed work (power-sized by P3-D-POWER), not ~$0 CPU, per the round-1 census-accounting correction.

claim: RAGC Rule-2 execution (retriever policy): the shared cell runs BM25 (mandatory in every instantiation) + one pinned small dense embedder over a pinned deterministic common text serialisation of every arm's objects (kernel records via the existing renderer/desugaring, triples as canonical S-P-O sentences, tool schemas as docstrings); embedder weights + index bytes charge to store_bytes. The identical-vs-architecture-specific retriever decision is a CLOSED table: attribution comparisons use the SHARED lens; the GR-B frontier point uses NATIVE best-in-budget retrieval tuned within T_k; executor/retry cells inherit their wrapped cell. Structural handicap of the shared lens is disclosed beside results, never grounds for dropping the cell. Retrieval recall@k vs gold provenance is reported separately from end-task accuracy on every arm. Implementation is from one pinned standard harness (FlashRAG-class), release pinned at freeze. Anti-weak-control counters: GR arms are built/tuned by the FRONT/1 pipeline under the same T_k meter and fixed selection rule; harness defaults are the floor (deviating below requires a logged reason); the nomination window + F-CAL A4 challenger gate apply to GR arms; full derivation/build logs published.
backing_ref: docs/next/design/RAGC.md sections 2.4 and 3; inherits ASM-0853 Rule 2 (corrected form) verbatim; extends the ASM-0812 threat model with the weak-control channel
rationale: A per-experiment judgement call on 'same retriever or not' is a gaming surface both ways; closing it as a fixed table tied to claim licences, forcing the lexical baseline, separating retrieval from generation metrics, and pinning the harness release removes the discretion the literature shows corrupts RAG comparisons, while the weak-control counters mirror FRONT/1's weak-builder defence on the control side.
notes: BM25-mandatory per BEIR via RAG.md section 6.2; FlashRAG release-pin lesson per RAG.md section 3.

claim: RAGC Rule-3 execution (context budgets + ordering): one retrieved-token budget ret_tok(B_k) and one context cap per rung, identical across every arm including S; k floats per arm to fill the same TOKEN budget (objects differ in size across representations by construction). Context assembly is retrieval-score-descending for every arm; PS is the pre-registered seed-pinned order perturbation; RD fills the same token budget with seed-pinned random snapshot documents and is read as a control ONLY (GR-A minus RD ~ 0 voids the retrieval attribution at scope; tuning toward noise injection is prohibited). The kernel-as-text null (content-type cell g) runs at the same ret_tok(B_k) so the null, the generic control, and S are token-commensurable within one experiment.
backing_ref: docs/next/design/RAGC.md section 4; inherits ASM-0853 Rule 3 verbatim
rationale: Matching object counts instead of tokens would hand the arm with larger objects a hidden context subsidy; the position and random-document confounds are measured literature effects that must be controlled by pre-registered cells rather than left as free variables.
notes: Position confound: Lost-in-the-Middle via RAG.md 6.4; RD trap: Power-of-Noise via RAG.md 6.5/7.7.

claim: RAGC Rule-4 execution (generator/executor/retry/tuning parity): attribution comparisons use the same generator checkpoint hash, decoding params, max-token + stop discipline, abstention semantics, and prompt-template skeleton with only the evidence slot varying. GR-D wraps a generic arm in S's exact loop shape (same max-k, same per-retry mutation rule, same abstain-on-exhaustion) with a pinned generic acceptance-signal menu: (i) self-consistency agreement (SA-class components only), (ii) a small learned outcome verifier trained within T_k and fitting store_bytes, (iii) tool-execution success where the vertical is executable; readout is the pair (S - GR-D) and (GR-D - GR-A). GR-C freezes the generic-executor cell: the same typed content in one pinned generic deterministic engine (SQLite recursive-CTE default; pinned Datalog engine as registered alternative) exposed via the tool-schema derivation, with tool errors accounted identically to S's engine errors; S ~ GR-C on the covered slice kills the kernel engine's distinctive claim at scope and feeds K-P3v2(4). Executor sandbox/timeout/error-surface parity is pinned; the OR cell is co-frozen with P3-D-ORACLE. Tuning parity binds through FRONT/1's T_k meter; GR-C and GR-D are arm-table keys added at rung entry (later addition = frontier version bump).
backing_ref: docs/next/design/RAGC.md section 5; inherits ASM-0853 Rule 4 verbatim; consumes ASM-0852; absorbs the round-1 A.1 generic-Datalog attribution cell
rationale: The measured f2b sign is a verify-RETRY loop whose shuffled control cannot separate content from alignment; matching only the store would leave the loop and executor axes confounded, so the control must match the loop shape with a generic acceptance signal and substitute a generic deterministic executor over the same typed content to isolate what the kernel verifier and engine specifically add.
notes: Generic acceptance menu direction from RAG.md section 5 (Cobbe outcome verifier / auto-label PRM recipes) at budget-feasible sizes.

claim: RAGC Rule-5 execution (construction leakage + effort): every instantiation publishes one build-ledger row per arm -- derivation script hash, derivation compute, human hours, index build compute, embedder/selector training compute, packed object bytes, index bytes, decontamination screen result hash, and freeze-vs-suite-exposure timestamps -- with kernel-store authoring hours appearing on S's row of the same table. Derivation pipelines freeze (content-hash) before any frozen-suite item is drawn; the screen applies to construction inputs and outputs; post-exposure construction is VOID for W1 use.
backing_ref: docs/next/design/RAGC.md section 6; inherits ASM-0853 Rule 5 verbatim
rationale: The literature's silent asymmetry (dense index treated as a free embed pass while structured stores pay full construction sweeps) and the answer-key trap both live in construction accounting; a mandatory symmetric per-arm ledger row plus the freeze-before-exposure rule makes the asymmetry an explicit line item and the leakage channel mechanically auditable.
notes: Construction-cost asymmetry and selector-on-the-ledger lessons via RAG.md sections 1 and 3.

claim: RAGC readout semantics: the six-way attribution factorisation is operationalised as a pinned contrast ladder -- S-vs-kernel-as-text (machinery vs conditioning), S-vs-aligned-non-kernel (kernel semantics vs typed store; our own instrument, no published precedent), S-vs-GR-A (kernel system vs conventional retrieval at fixed content), GR-A-vs-R0 (retrieval contribution), GR-A-vs-RD (relevance vs perturbation), S-vs-GR-C (engine vs generic executor), S-vs-GR-D (verifier content vs generic signal), GR-D-vs-GR-A (retry/search axis) -- with five mandatory reporting requirements: full contrast vector, covered-vs-uncovered split, long-tail/popularity strata where a proxy exists, recall@k beside every end-task figure, and every verdict sentence naming the cell that licenses it. K-P3v2(4) attribution collapse is evaluated on the S-vs-GR-A and S-vs-GR-C contrasts under the house statistics (LCB-vs-margin, FWER across the instantiated contrast set, hierarchical bootstrap preserving paired predictions).
backing_ref: docs/next/design/RAGC.md section 7; consumes ASM-0812 (six-way factorisation) + ASM-0813 (statistics) + ASM-0811 (vector reporting)
rationale: Cells without pinned readout semantics invite post-hoc story-telling; fixing the contrast ladder, the zero-contrast interpretations, and the kill-condition wiring before any experiment runs is what makes the control decision-grade rather than decorative.
notes: Long-tail stratification per Mallen via RAG.md 6.6; recall/answer separation per FiD via RAG.md 6.3.

claim: RAGC executable interface + instrument gates: the implementation emits per vertical x rung one content-hashed control-arm MANIFEST (snapshot hash, derivation matrix hashes/bytes, serialiser hashes, retriever + harness pins, token budgets + ordering seeds, generator pins, GR-C executor + schema hashes, GR-D loop params + signal choice, build-ledger rows, parity diagnostics, screen results). FRONT/1 consumes the manifest at S0 (GR arms enter the comparator lattice pre-derived) and owns their frontier measurement; every store-touching experiment consumes it at prereg freeze via the eight-item instantiation checklist; a store-touching G4 prereg lacking the manifest hash does not freeze; inherited FRONT/1 section-5 rules amend only by registered amendment + manifest version bump. Instrument gates RAGC-CAL: V1 harness reproduction, V2 discrimination sanity (LCB95(OR - R0) > 0 on a knowledge-intensive dev slice, else the vertical is blocked as uninformative and disclosed), V3 build repeatability (manifest re-derivable from pins), V4 parity-band application -- fail-closed: no W1-relevant experiment cites any RAGC cell until V1-V3 PASS for its manifest. First instantiation target: the a5/f2b vertical worked example (the f2b de-confound arms). Compute envelope: <= ~10 GPU-h at R-1 beyond FRONT's ledger plus one costed annotation batch, with a disclosed BM25-only graceful-degradation path; R-3 conditional on a secured allocation.
backing_ref: docs/next/design/RAGC.md sections 8-10; plugs into FRONT/1 sections 3 (S0) and 9 (interface table); OR cell co-frozen with P3-D-ORACLE
rationale: A control that exists only as prose cannot be plugged into the frontier-builder or a prereg; a single content-hashed manifest with fail-closed consumption, instrument gates that can genuinely fail, and closed compute arithmetic makes the control an executable, falsifiable artifact rather than a checklist.
notes: V-gates co-scheduled with P3-E-FCAL/P3-E-CAL in the same free-pool calibration campaign; spawned beads P3-T-RAGC-IMPL + P3-E-RAGC-CAL are coordinator recommendations only.

claim: NLB/1 revision-2 acceptance semantics: evidence-gated ambiguity-set execution + a primary acceptance arm FROZEN AT DESIGN TIME (supersedes the executor clause of ASM-0905(5) and sharpens ASM-0900(3)). (1) AMBIGUITY SET = the set of complete valid parses the front-end cannot distinguish on surface evidence, constructed deterministically: the pinned per-frame orientation-cue inventory decides each directional frame - cues present and consistent select ONE orientation and the inverse is NOT added; cues absent or conflicting add BOTH orientations; Tier-1 adds any candidate within a pinned score band of the top candidate and any competing entity binding arising from a true trie collision. (2) EXECUTION RULE: |set| = 1 -> answer that parse (subject to contracts + tau); |set| > 1 -> execute ALL members on the microsecond engine and answer only if every denotation agrees (direction immaterial on that item); otherwise CLARIFY, returning the competing readings. Unconditional inverse-inclusion (the rev-1 wording) is REJECTED as logically wrong: on genuinely directional queries the correct and inverse denotations normally differ, so it abstains exactly where direction matters, and accidental agreement never identifies the correct direction - it could alone make 0.90 retention unreachable. (3) PRIMARY GATE ARM, frozen now: the gate system is the full stack (repaired Tier-0 + Tier-1 + grammar/type validity + IR-inversion contract + evidence-gated ambiguity-set + frozen tau); the ablation arms (confidence-only; confidence+contracts; Tier-0-only) are DIAGNOSTIC CO-REPORTS on the same run, never candidates for selection on EVAL - no arm choice ever touches EVAL.
backing_ref: docs/next/design/NLB.md sections 3.3 and 4 (revision 2); poc/gpt56-review/rev-dNLB-20260711b/last-message.json (ranked concern 1); docs/next/arch/round1-critique-synthesis.md section B.2.5
rationale: The external review demonstrated the rev-1 executor is logically wrong as written (always-include-the-inverse forces abstention precisely where direction matters, and denotation agreement cannot identify direction) and that an unfrozen primary arm invites post-selection on EVAL; gating ambiguity-set membership on surface evidence (cue inventory + score band + trie collisions) restores answering on directionally-cued queries while keeping engine-agreement safety on genuinely ambiguous ones, and freezing the full stack as the single primary arm removes the selection degree of freedom.
notes: Registered by NLB.md revision 2 (corrective block ASM-0940-0947; 0948-0949 remain free). ASM-0905(5)'s arm comparison survives as a diagnostic deliverable only; the register is append-only so the superseded rev-1 clauses remain visible.

claim: G-NLB statistics, revision 2 (supersedes ASM-0901(5)). (1) RARE-EVENT BOUNDS ARE EXACT AND CLUSTER-VALID, NEVER BOOTSTRAP: the S2 gate/kill legs, the S1 control leg, and every directional-stratum allowance use the one-sided exact Clopper-Pearson bound on the CLUSTER-COLLAPSED indicator (a base-query cluster counts as failed iff ANY of its K phrasings is answered-and-wrong); the phrasing-level rate never exceeds the cluster any-wrong rate, so the collapsed CP UB95 is a valid conservative UB95 on the phrasing-level unconditional risk, and it is never degenerate at zero observed events (0 wrong in m clusters -> UB95 = 1 - 0.05^(1/m), the rule-of-three shape). The cluster bootstrap (pinned B, seed, method) is retained ONLY for the retention interval (a non-rare proportion), with the exact CP bound on the cluster-collapsed all-correct indicator co-reported; where the two disagree on gate crossing, the CONSERVATIVE one binds. (2) NO ADAPTIVE SELECTION TOUCHES EVAL: tau is frozen on CAL-B before any EVAL phrasing exists (ASM-0943); the primary arm is frozen at design time (ASM-0940); endpoints and strata are enumerated at freeze; the per-vertical Holm family is EXACTLY {retention leg, S2 gate leg, S1 control leg, directional-stratum intersection leg}; the two verticals are separate registered claims, never pooled. (3) INTEGER BOUNDARIES: every PASS/FAIL/kill boundary is derived by analytic inversion of the exact bounds (not by resampling) and pinned in the frozen record by the pinned analysis script before any scored run; directional-stratum minimum support comes from the same arithmetic (a zero-wrong allowance certifies UB95 < r only when the stratum has at least ln(0.05)/ln(1-r) clusters, e.g. 59 for r = 0.05), pinned per stratum at freeze.
rationale: A conventional cluster bootstrap is unreliable for rare-event upper bounds and can return a degenerate zero bound at zero errors, and pointwise bounds combined with adaptive search carry no coverage guarantee (the review's concern 2); collapsing to cluster level and inverting an exact binomial gives finite-sample conservative coverage under arbitrary within-cluster dependence with no resampling assumptions, and enumerating the Holm family plus freezing every selection before EVAL removes the multiplicity the review found uncovered.
notes: The fixed integer boundary claim of rev-1 is now derivable: exact binomial bounds invert analytically to integer error budgets; the bootstrap never sets a gate boundary.

claim: NLB-EVAL/1 revision 2: five-role data separation, corrected corpus arithmetic, and the ambiguity-annotation protocol (supersedes ASM-0902(3)/(5) and the corpus-size notes of ASM-0902/ASM-0904). (1) FIVE ROLES, disjoint: TRAIN (synthetic only), DEV (model selection ONLY), CAL-A (calibrator/score training), CAL-B (threshold selection), EVAL (the single scored set); CAL-A and CAL-B are disjoint pre-registered query splits of the S1-author-source covered set (~20 percent + ~20 percent of covered queries), EVAL is the disjoint author-source S2 over the remaining ~60 percent, authored after the front-end pin; rev-1's DEV double duty (model selection AND calibrator training) is rejected as a role violation. (2) CORRECTED ARITHMETIC, binding: covered scored phrasings at K=3 are 527x3 = 1,581 (l3a) + 855x3 = 2,565 (a5) = 4,146; matched controls add 270x3 + 106x3 = 1,128; total 5,274 phrasings BEFORE directional-support and perturbation strata top-ups; EVAL-side scored phrasings are ~316x3 = 948 (l3a) and ~513x3 = 1,539 (a5); the rev-1 '~4,700' figure and the '~1,800-phrasing instrument' note in ASM-0904 are superseded. (3) AMBIGUITY/ANSWERABILITY ANNOTATION: a pinned written rubric authored before any annotation; at least 3 independent annotators (or 2 + a standing adjudicator) blind to system outputs; chance-corrected agreement (Krippendorff alpha) reported with a pinned floor of 0.6, below which the rubric is repaired and annotation redone; majority + adjudication fixes the label; annotated-ambiguous/unanswerable items are CAPPED at 10 percent of EVAL items per vertical - beyond the cap the corpus is repaired - and retention is co-reported with the annotated items retained in the denominator, so hard-item exclusion cannot silently inflate retention.
backing_ref: docs/next/design/NLB.md section 5 (revision 2); poc/gpt56-review/rev-dNLB-20260711b/last-message.json (four-role violation, corpus-count inconsistency, annotation-rubric gap)
rationale: The review found DEV doing double duty against the cited four-role requirement, corpus totals that do not add up (527x3 + 855x3 = 4,146, plus 1,128 control phrasings = 5,274, not ~4,700), and an annotation pass with no rubric, no agreement statistics, and no cap preventing hard-item exclusion from inflating retention; this entry repairs each with the cheapest standard instrument: disjoint CAL-A/CAL-B splits, explicit binding arithmetic, and a rubric + agreement + cap + co-report protocol.
notes: All other ASM-0902 clauses (base items, K=3 blind protocol, freeze ordering, strata, recoverability gate, legacy-corpus quarantine) stand unchanged.

claim: NLB/1 threshold selection, revision 2 (supersedes the selection mechanics of ASM-0903(1)). tau is selected on CAL-B ONLY by FIXED-SEQUENCE MONOTONE RISK-CONTROLLED TESTING (the risk-controlling-prediction-sets / learn-then-test shape): the threshold grid is pinned in advance; candidates are traversed from most conservative (lowest coverage) to least; each candidate's unconditional dangerous-wrong risk is tested with the exact cluster-collapsed Clopper-Pearson UB at delta = 0.05 against the 0.02 budget; traversal stops at the FIRST failure and the last passing tau is frozen into the front-end content-hash before any EVAL phrasing exists. Validity: fixed-sequence testing spends no multiplicity - each test is reached only if every more conservative candidate passed - so the frozen tau carries a valid 95 percent risk certificate on the CAL distribution WITHOUT uniform bounds or confidence sequences; free search over the grid with pointwise bounds (the rev-1 reading) is rejected as post-selection. The calibrator, if the pinned score class uses one, is trained on CAL-A only; CAL-B is never touched by score training. The risk-coverage curve + AUACC co-report and the shift-stress and inversion-separation secondaries of ASM-0903(2)-(4) are unchanged.
backing_ref: docs/next/design/NLB.md section 6 (revision 2); poc/gpt56-review/rev-dNLB-20260711b/last-message.json (post-selection threshold-search gap); docs/next/lit/PARSE.md section 4 (selective-prediction frame)
rationale: The review's finding: selecting the largest acceptable threshold via pointwise exact-binomial/Hoeffding bounds without a uniform bound, confidence sequence, or separate selection split is post-selection with no coverage guarantee; fixed-sequence monotone testing is the standard cheap repair - it needs only a pinned grid and one pass, exploits monotonicity of risk in tau, keeps the certificate honest on the calibration distribution, and separates calibrator fitting (CAL-A) from threshold selection (CAL-B).
notes: The CAL-distribution certificate is never quoted as an EVAL-distribution guarantee (ASM-0903(3) shift stress unchanged).

claim: NLB-0, revision 2: an explicitly NON-DECISIVE, PER-VERTICAL screen (supersedes the GO logic and 'conservative floor' reading of ASM-0904(2)/(3)). (1) STATUS: NLB-0 is a screening diagnostic, not a decisive experiment - the legacy corpora are public and influenced the design, K=1, and the acceptance layer is absent in the pilot; a pilot number is neither a floor nor a ceiling for the full system and NO conservatism claim is made. (2) PER-VERTICAL GO, matching the per-vertical gate and the at-least-one-vertical projection (ASM-0906): each vertical screens independently; GO on a vertical = pilot retention point >= 0.80 at a pre-frozen operating point with dangerous-wrong point <= 0.01 on that vertical's legacy corpus (B leg), plus, on the a5 vertical where the defect lives, the A-leg proceed condition (point dangerous-wrong <= 4/855 with retention >= measured - 0.02); a vertical failing the screen routes THAT vertical to design revision or the maintainer-escalated branches while the other vertical proceeds to its instrument build; the rev-1 both-vertical GO rule is rejected as contradicting per-vertical gating. (3) SCOPE LIMITS, disclosed on every NLB-0 artifact: NLB-0-A exercises the repaired Tier-0 ONLY and establishes NOTHING about Tier-1 safety (learned-parser inversions are controlled only by the acceptance layer and the G-NLB directional strata at the gate itself); an NLB-0-B NO-GO does not prove the recipe class fails at full training scale, completed acceptance layers, or different author sources - it is a cheap stop-signal that projected headroom was not visible at pilot cost, and proceeding to the instrument build despite a NO-GO requires a written maintainer decision. (4) Cycle accounting, at-most-one-per-vertical-per-revision, diagnostic-only status, no-blind-corpus-training, and disclosure rules of ASM-0904(1)/(4) are unchanged.
backing_ref: docs/next/design/NLB.md section 7 (revision 2); poc/gpt56-review/rev-dNLB-20260711b/last-message.json (NLB-0 non-decisiveness, floor claim, both-vertical contradiction)
rationale: The review showed the rev-1 pilot claimed more than it can deliver: a public design-influencing corpus at K=1 cannot be a conservative floor; a 5-GPU-hour pilot miss does not prove a 50-GPU-hour gate miss; NLB-0-A tests only the repaired deterministic tier; and the both-vertical GO rule contradicted the per-vertical gate and ASM-0906; demoting NLB-0 to a per-vertical screen with its inferential limits on the label keeps its cost-ordering value without overclaiming.
notes: The 0.80/0.01 screen thresholds remain design-phase decision rules, not gate statistics.

claim: G-NLB PASS license and consumer obligations: a PASS is a PREREQUISITE, never a transferable certificate (sharpens ASM-0900(4) and the application of ASM-0814). (1) WHAT WAS TESTED: backward-authored blind paraphrases of a closed parent grammar over a registered lexicon, authored under a pinned protocol - NOT naturally occurring user traffic; recoverability repair and creditable-abstention annotation further shape the distribution; this limitation is disclosed verbatim on any PASS. (2) CONSUMER OBLIGATIONS, all three required for every ASM-0814 leg unblocked by a PASS: (a) SHIP THE HASH - the consumer runs the exact passed front-end content-hash unmodified; any change to weights, masks, tries, tables, calibrator, or tau voids the pass for that consumer; (b) GRAMMAR COMPATIBILITY - the consumer's NL input surface must be the tested closed grammar (same families, lexicon, IR); a consumer with a different output space or input distribution (H-GNN subgraph extraction, H-RULE addressing, H-DD reinjection, benchmark-facing H-PS) does not inherit the pass and needs its own gate clearance or a registered compatibility argument; (c) CONSUMER G3 LEG - each consuming family's G3 evidence must include a task-originated NL leg on its own input distribution; the NLB PASS unblocks RUNNING that leg, never substitutes for it. (3) Any input-distribution shift (new phrasing sources, real users, new task) re-opens the question by construction; nothing here licenses a claim over 'all natural-input use'.
rationale: The review's concern 3: an evaluation scoped to backward-authored paraphrases of two closed grammars cannot justify a shared transferable pass for consumers with different grammars, output spaces, and input distributions; making the PASS a prerequisite plus ship-the-hash, grammar-compatibility, and consumer-G3 obligations preserves ASM-0814's unblock semantics while blocking certificate laundering.
notes: ASM-0814's gate role is unchanged; this entry narrows what clearing it licenses downstream.

claim: NLB-FE/1 resource and accounting contract, revision 2 (makes ASM-0900(2)/ASM-0905 accounting executable). (1) EXECUTABLE CEILING, pinned at prereg-freeze and MEASURED during G-NLB as an instrument-validity leg (exceeding it fails the attempt, not a footnote): packed front-end artifact <= 1.0 GB; Tier-1 warm batch-1 CPU latency p95 <= 500 ms and p99 <= 2000 ms per query on the pinned reference CPU; cold start <= 60 s; 'ms-class' projections are not budgets. (2) CANONICAL PACKED-ARTIFACT MANIFEST, one content-hash over: parser weights, tokenizer, runtime + version, grammar masks, entity tries + alias tables, Tier-0 template/lexicon/frame tables, calibrator, tau, and config; this hash IS the front-end pin of ASM-0902(4). (3) MEASURED DURING G-NLB and reported in the verdict, not deferred to consumers: warm and cold batch-1 latency (CPU, and GPU if used), Tier-1 fall-through rate, peak RSS (and VRAM if used), energy estimate, and tail latencies; Tier-0 timing is RE-MEASURED post-repair - the parents' 250-267 microsecond figures are priors, not attributable to the repaired implementation. (4) KOT-LIFE/1 logs the TOTAL search/tuning cost across every tried parser, score class, and acceptance arm, not just the survivor. (5) API-HOSTED PARSER = REMOTE DEPENDENCY: ineligible as NLB-FE/1 or any gate arm (its bytes and latency cannot be pinned under KOT-SIZE/2 + KOT-COST/2); the a5-nl-llm-successor branch requires its own registered accounting stipulation before any gate use.
rationale: The review required an executable resource ceiling instead of an ms-class projection, a canonical artifact manifest, in-gate cost measurement rather than deferral to later consumers, full search-cost accounting under KOT-LIFE/1, correct non-attribution of pre-repair timing, and explicit KOT-SIZE/KOT-COST treatment of API parsers; each is cheap to pin now and expensive to retrofit after a PASS.
notes: Ceiling values are design-phase pins; P3-E-NLB-1 prereg may tighten but never loosen them without a registered amendment.

claim: NLB-FE/1 Tier-1 implementation-defining specification (completes ASM-0905 to buildable precision). (1) IR: the parents' kot-query/1 and kot-query-code/1 DSLs, unchanged - NLB introduces no new IR. (2) MASK INTERFACE + EQUIVALENCE, formally: the constrained decoder's mask is computed on the PRODUCT AUTOMATON (DSL grammar automaton x tokenizer transducer x entity trie x per-family type constraints); 'exact local-vs-global mask equivalence' MEANS: at every reachable product state, the locally admitted token set equals the set of tokens extendable to at least one complete valid parse; the check is exhaustive over the product automaton (feasible: the grammar is tiny), and any divergence is quantified and pinned before freeze - grammar-automaton equivalence alone is insufficient. (3) CANDIDATE-SET CONSTRUCTION: intent+slot arm = top-M frame hypotheses x type-checked slot bindings (M pinned); seq2seq arm = masked beam of pinned width; both emit complete IR candidates with scores. (4) CONFIDENCE AGGREGATION: one pinned scalar per candidate from a pinned function class (calibrated product of intent and slot posteriors, or length-normalized sequence log-probability); the class is chosen on DEV and pinned at freeze. (5) ENTITY LINKING (the named dominant failure class): mention detection by longest-match trie scan over the lemmatized surface; the alias table is built deterministically at artifact-build time from the registered gazetteer by enumerated generators (case, lemma, separator variants) and hash-pinned in the manifest; a surface form mapping to more than one URN is a COLLISION and enters the ambiguity set (CLARIFY path), never a silent pick; 'deterministic post-mapping' for unseen surface forms MEANS lemmatization + alias-table lookup ONLY - no fuzzy or edit-distance matching in any scored arm; no match -> the candidate dies (abstain if no candidate survives). (6) TRAINING RECIPE: synthetic TRAIN generated by a hash-pinned grammar-recombination script over fresh identities with registered seed; target sizes pinned at prereg (order 10^4-10^5 phrasing-IR pairs per vertical); the generation recipe, seed, and realized size are part of the frozen record.
backing_ref: docs/next/design/NLB.md section 3.2 (revision 2); poc/gpt56-review/rev-dNLB-20260711b/last-message.json (missing implementation-defining details; entity-linking underspecification; mask-equivalence formalization)
rationale: The review listed the implementation-defining details the design lacked (IR grammar, tokenizer/mask interface, candidate construction, confidence aggregation, entity mention detection with aliases/collisions/post-mapping, training-set sizes and recipe) - entity linking being the named dominant measured failure class - and required a formal definition of mask equivalence over tokenizer state, semantic constraints, and the entity trie; pinning these makes the design buildable at 110-360M and makes the mask-fidelity check meaningful rather than nominal.
notes: Concrete values (M, beam width, grid) are pinned at P3-E-NLB-1 prereg; this entry pins the shapes and the definitions.

claim: RAGC attribution semantics (revision 2, correcting the revision-1 overclaim): every RAGC contrast is a SYSTEM-LEVEL comparison in which multiple factors co-vary (S-vs-GR-A changes derived representation, index contents, evidence rendering, interface, and execution path; S-vs-GR-C changes engine, query surface, tool schema, and expressivity; S-vs-GR-D changes verifier information, error profile, calibration, and acceptance rate). A contrast licenses only (a) ELIMINATION sentences -- S failing to beat the generic bundle kills the kernel bundle's distinctive claim at scope, the K-P3v2(4) direction, which needs no factor isolation -- and (b) BOUNDED-DIFFERENCE sentences naming the full differing bundle. No pairwise contrast licenses a single-factor causal sentence; single-factor attribution requires a factorial design with interaction estimation, out of scope for RAGC/1 and priced only as a registered follow-up. The integration factor is isolated by no cell and is carried as an explicitly unattributed residual in every readout. K-P3v2(4) is evaluated on the S-vs-GR-A and S-vs-GR-C contrasts under the programme's section-2.5 statistical specification, consumed via P3-D-INDEX's analysis plan.
backing_ref: docs/next/design/RAGC.md revision 2 sections 0 and 7; poc/gpt56-review/rev-dRAGC-20260711b ranked concern 1
rationale: The external review demonstrated that the revision-1 contrast ladder claimed factorial identification its cells cannot deliver; elimination readings survive without factor isolation, so the control keeps its kill power while the causal overclaim is withdrawn.
notes: Supersedes the attribution reading of ASM-0926; ASM-0926's cell inventory, five reporting requirements, and kill wiring remain in force.

claim: RAGC byte accounting (revision 2): admissibility is TOTAL-SYSTEM first -- every arm's complete packed artifact set (generator checkpoint + tokenizer + store objects + index structures + embedder weights + verifier weights + executor binary beyond the frozen base image + serialisers/adapters) must fit B_k.bytes_max under the one KOT-SIZE/2 canonical packing script, warm and cold per FRONT/1 section 1. The equal store cap is DERIVED, never free-standing: store_bytes(B_k) := B_k.bytes_max minus the maximum over compared arms of that arm's non-store packed bytes; equal store caps alone do not establish KOT-FAIR/2 admissibility. Consequence stated openly: B_k is anchor-derived, so an anchor-sized generator plus any nonzero store/index cannot be admissible at its own rung -- a store-bearing arm at rung k necessarily runs a sub-anchor generator (the measured f2b shape: 135M generator + store against the 1.7B anchor's budget). The control-arm manifest carries, for every arm including GR-C and GR-D, the complete KOT-SIZE/2 six-figure report and the measured KOT-COST/2 vector (p95 latency, energy under the section-1.1 boundary rule, peak accelerator memory + host RSS, I/O, warm and cold) under the P3-D-HW rig protocol; build-ledger rows are necessary but not sufficient.
backing_ref: docs/next/design/RAGC.md revision 2 sections 2.2 and 8.1; poc/gpt56-review/rev-dRAGC-20260711b ranked concern 2
rationale: Anchor-derived budgets make the naive equal-store-cap reading arithmetically unsatisfiable for anchor-sized generators; deriving the cap as a residual of the total deployment budget and mandating full size/cost vectors per arm is what closes KOT-FAIR/2 admissibility rather than gesturing at it.
notes: Supersedes the byte-cap clause of ASM-0921 and extends the ASM-0927 manifest schema.
