claim: G1 pool: the §2.1 pinned-before-looking 20+5 repo list (names and order fixed at design-pin time, disjoint from the G0 six), size band [300,120000] analyzed *.py LOC, mechanical backup substitution in pinned order on clone/license/band failure, shallow-HEAD SHAs recorded; agent-selected disclosure — every cross-repo band is a resampling sensitivity band, never a generalization CI; all-*.py analyzed scope + package-source-only co-report.
rationale: Pinning the pool by name and order before any repo content is seen removes selection-after-looking; the agent-selected honesty clause keeps cross-repo bands from being read as population CIs.
notes: CODEVERT G1 design pin.

claim: G1 census = G0 census.py generator logic (content-hash-pinned, extractor-independent) with fresh pinned seed 20260716, generated and hashed before the extractor runs; extractor PY-STAT/1 byte-identical to poc/codevert-g0/extractor.py (any edit = extractor version change per ASM-1031, voiding the run); FL-4 primary aggregate (query-pooled + family-macro), all-8 families co-reported; repo-cluster bootstrap (seed 20260716, 10k) = sensitivity band only.
rationale: Census-before-extraction ordering and a byte-identical extractor make the G1 measurement a pure new-data replication of the pinned instrument.
notes: CODEVERT G1 design pin.

claim: Proxy gold: two LLM annotators (fable-a Claude-family; gpt56-b gpt-5.6-sol via pinned codex@0.144.1 invocation, effort medium — a disclosed deviation from the judge-1p 'low'), blind and independent, byte-identical instructions and context bundles; disagreements adjudicated by the Fable main agent with per-item logged rationale (annotation/adjudication.jsonl); ALL gold-dependent endpoints PROVISIONAL-ON-LLM-PROXY, void the moment a human Pass-B annotation of the same sample lands.
rationale: LLM stand-in gold is the maintainer-approved instrument for this leg; the blindness, logged adjudication, and void-on-human-gold clause keep it honestly provisional rather than silently authoritative.
notes: CODEVERT G1 design pin; g3-humangold pattern.

claim: Sample: 2 queries per (family x repo) cell over 5 measured families x 20 repos = 200 queries, seed-pinned (20260716) from the frozen census universes; cells smaller than 2 take all and log the shortfall; 120 KB context-bundle cap with pinned next-index replacement rule (logged, never silently dropped); no-label >10% per endpoint => instrument-invalid for that endpoint.
rationale: A seed-pinned cell design with mechanical replacement and a pre-stated invalidity threshold makes the proxy-gold sample auditable and fail-closed.
notes: CODEVERT G1 design pin.

claim: Element normalization rules of §4: contains/contained_in and repo-internal callees compared as relpath::qualpath symbols (module target = relpath); imports_of as repo:relpath for repo-internal (with parent-package closure + self-edges per ASM-1058) and ext:<base> for external per the PY-STAT/1 emission rule; where_defined as (relpath, lineno) sites with engine spans mapped to lines mechanically; callees_of precision/R_q computed on repo-internal elements only (disclosed).
rationale: Pinned normalization removes scorer discretion at comparison time; the repo-internal-only callee scoring is the one disclosed narrowing, taken because external-callee naming is not reliably normalizable.
notes: CODEVERT G1 design pin.

claim: Answerability protocol + endpoint definitions: each annotator marks answerable-static yes/no + gold set (possibly empty) + conditional/lazy flags; on adjudicated proxy gold, precision = element-level correctness of proved listings, R_q = fraction of gold-answerable queries returned proved with the FULL gold set, negative-answer validity = fraction of gold-empty queries answered proved-empty; floors quoted from ASM-1030 (kappa_q^indep >= 0.5, R_q >= 0.90, precision >= 0.95, neg-validity >= 0.90); the mechanical verdict is the coordinator's step — this run produces verdict-INPUT only.
rationale: Endpoint definitions and floors are fixed at pin time and the verdict is mechanically the coordinator's, preserving run-vs-verdict separation.
notes: CODEVERT G1 design pin.

claim: PY-STAT/2-SPIKE scope: D1 local-alias, D2 parametrized-decorator return analysis, D3 call-result return analysis — bounded local dataflow, candidates-only (NO proved upgrades), non-scored, measured on the pinned G0 corpus against the FROZEN G0 census; any full PY-STAT/2 build is an extractor version change per ASM-1031 (new inventory, new census freeze, new spike); spike output is a build/no-build verdict-INPUT only.
rationale: Candidates-only scoping lets the spike price the excluded-family territory without contaminating the scored instrument or upgrading anything to proved.
notes: CODEVERT G1 design pin.

claim: Spike metrics: '*'-mass conversion by mechanism, per-family kappa recovery vs the G0 ablation ceiling, and probe-trace candidate-narrowing exclusion count as the soundness guard.
rationale: Names the spike's three read-outs in advance so the build/no-build input is a pre-stated measurement, not a post-hoc selection.
notes: CODEVERT G1 design pin.

claim: Session governance for the G1 leg: no git/bd/kb operations by the experiment agent; no @-handle strings in artifacts; every measured number tagged [MEASURED]; every gold-dependent number additionally PROVISIONAL-ON-LLM-PROXY; nothing produced is a scored verdict — the coordinator runs the mechanical verdict against the pinned floors.
rationale: Records the role separation and tagging discipline the leg ran under, so provenance and the provisional status of every gold-dependent number are auditable.
notes: CODEVERT G1 design pin; auditability caveat: pin-before-clone is filesystem-time-consistent only (no git object at pin time), disclosed verbatim from G0.

claim: Unique-name assumption (UNA) holds within each item scope: distinct entity names in one item's fact set denote distinct individuals; emitted as pairwise owl:differentFrom within the item's family (bounded, <=5 entities).
rationale: Required premise for R-COVER-ELIM case elimination; contestable world-modelling, so named and sidecar-endorsed rather than hidden in the rule.
notes: Visible in every why() proof tree that uses it.

claim: Covering axiom: parent is covered by {mother, father} (every parent is a mother or a father) — minted as kind coveredBy in data/axioms-kinship-v1, endorsed by the molecules-v0 parent-family explications.
rationale: Load-bearing premise for the canonical parent->father derivation; a modelling choice about the kinship domain, not a measurement.
notes: OWL 2 RL cannot use it directly (assertion-only heads); compiled into the monotone Horn rule R-COVER-ELIM.

claim: The first architectural position tested is the prompt-time, temporary-world, external-reasoner memory slot (derivations enter as context injection and as a verify-retry oracle).
backing_ref: Yang/Ishay/Lee 2023 arXiv:2307.07696 (CLUTRR via LLM->ASP); Pan et al. 2023 arXiv:2305.12295 (Logic-LM)
rationale: Cheapest decisive position; strongest prior-art existence proofs on the exact task family; the only position where the non-inertness claim can attach at runtime.
notes: Design choice informed by literature; the literature claims themselves are LIT-BACKED in-doc.

claim: RULES-1 is gold-parse only: facts arrive as structured kot-world/1 records or pre-parsed triples; the NL->ABox front-end is out of scope and no NL-robustness claim attaches.
rationale: Isolates the derivation question from the measured parse wall; keeps the failure modes attributable.
notes: Scope limit frozen into the envelope.

claim: Engine build path: sparq-reason (OwlRl + N3, why() proof trees) is the primary reasoner; a ~200-LOC Python forward chainer implementing only the RULES-1 inventory is a differential twin; both must agree exactly on Cl(S) for every item or the run halts.
rationale: Conformance-tested engine + independent-implementation audit; de-risks Rust build friction on the 2-core shared box.
notes: MD-4.

claim: Primary eval world is data/nsk1-clutrr (real CLUTRR, third-party gold predating the kernel, CC-BY-NC-4.0 eval-side only); data/nsk1-eval is the redistributable twin; the onto-obo RO shard is the pre-registered second vertical, not in the MVE.
rationale: Real-data external gold removes gold circularity; twin covers redistribution; second vertical is a generality probe gated on the first readout.
notes: MD-1, MD-8. onto-sumo excluded: KIF strings need a translation step (separate bead).

claim: A new module data/axioms-kinship-v1 (~6 kot-axiom/1 records) is minted: parent relation, person class, mother subPropertyOf parent, father subPropertyOf parent, parent coveredBy {mother,father}; each record cites the endorsing molecules-v0 explication sha (axioms-definitional-v0 endorsement precedent).
rationale: Fills the named gaps (no parent, no person) required by the canonical target; endorsement keeps the definitions->axioms mapping authored and auditable, never automatic NL->OWL.
notes: New kinds subPropertyOf and coveredBy extend kot-axiom/1; unknown kinds elsewhere still refuse (fail-closed).

claim: Token-budget matching: arm A1 prompts are padded with neutral text to the token count of arm A2, so injection lift is not attributable to prompt length.
rationale: Standard confound control; padding text pinned by sha.

claim: Claim assignment across arms: A3 (verify-retry) carries the model-level claim; A2 (injection) is reported strictly as a systems claim (model+world-model derives what the model alone cannot), never as model reasoning.
rationale: Injection of the derived fact verbatim reduces the host task to reading comprehension; verify-retry forces the model to generate while the engine only licenses rejection.
notes: Adopted from the WM-1 risk analysis.

claim: Structural control: a Sattolo-deranged axiom set at identical topology and token cost (shuffled-rules) must collapse the lift, with recovery UB95 < 0.30, per the H-STRUCT discipline.
backing_ref: H-STRUCT discipline (prior kernel-of-truth controls); nsk1 B' precedent
rationale: Distinguishes content-driven lift from structure/bulk artefacts; the threshold is a design choice.

claim: The RULES-1 rule inventory is closed and terminating by construction: R-SUBP, R-DOMRNG, R-INV, R-CHAIN (chain depth <= 2, no recursion), R-COVER-ELIM; anything else refuses with ERR_RULE_UNIMPLEMENTED.
rationale: Bounds closure size (the known scale cliff) and keeps the engine fail-closed; the depth bound is an envelope term, not incidental.
notes: Closure blow-up datum (237K->~150M triples) is LIT-BACKED in-doc (GraphDB documentation).

claim: Non-inertness is operationalised as the inverted DECONF-A1 certificate: SUCCESS = C_dec = 1.0 on stated cells AND C_dec < 1.0 on entailed cells AND engine correct vs third-party gold at Wilson-LB >= 0.98; KILL-a fires if Cl(S)\S is empty or trivial.
rationale: Reuses the same instrument that measured inertness, so the flip is measured on the maintainer's own definition; CPU-only, ~$0.

claim: Host-lift primary decision rule: A3-A1 (and A2-A1, systems-level) on entailed cells, paired item BCa bootstrap B=10^4, one-sided 95% LB > 0 against the CLUTRR third-party gold; KILL-b fires on LB <= 0 (engine value remains oracle-only; route to L3b).
backing_ref: f2b/nsk1 statistical discipline (paired BCa, pre-registered kills)
rationale: Pre-registered, sign-safe, external-gold-scored; the specific rule is a design choice.

claim: Efficiency secondary: noninferiority of R1(135M)+engine (best of A2/A3) vs R3(1.7B)-alone on the entailed slice, margin 0, sign-only language, two rungs, Holm-corrected with the other secondaries.
rationale: Re-tests the small-matches-big thesis exactly where the offloaded mechanism should bind (multi-hop composition).

claim: GPU cost bands for RULES-1: certificate ~$0 (CPU); smallest decisive contrast $1-3 (0.5-1.5 A10G-h); full skeleton $3-10 (2-6 A10G-h); worst case with second vertical + paraphrase K=2 $15-25 (12-15 A10G-h).
backing_ref: nsk1 B' measured datum: 32,958 rows = 0.457 A10G-h ~= $0.50 (Modal)
rationale: Linear scaling from the one measured throughput datum on the same harness and instance class; bands widened to absorb k=4 retry variance.
notes: All tiers inside the standing Tier-0/F2 authorization. [batch-prep fix: resolution_path supplied; doc emission lacked it]
resolution_path: The RULES-1 run-log's actual Modal cost ledger ($ per leg) converts each band to MEASURED or refutes it; any leg whose measured cost exceeds its band forces a re-price before approval of the next leg.

claim: The activation-write position is deferred: no spend there until the memory-slot position reads out.
backing_ref: nsk1 B' verdict: INSTRUMENT-INVALID (shuffled control out-rescued real, 0.525 vs 0.400)
rationale: The one measured attempt at that position invalidated its own instrument; repeating before a working comparison point exists would be uninformative spend.
notes: MD-2 option (d).

claim: The train-time slot (fine-tuning on materialised default-world closure, KOTC arm C) is deferred to a named follow-on (RULES-2), gated on RULES-1 showing the engine's derivations matter at the memory slot.
backing_ref: Herron et al. 2025 (materialised entailments can degrade downstream); llm-integration survey d-train assessment
rationale: The train slot answers a different question (are the rules in the weights?) and carries a memorisation-vs-rule confound needing its own controls; sequencing it second avoids paying for it before the engine's value is established.
notes: MD-2 option (c).

claim: The decode-time logit-mask arm (A6) is optional in RULES-1, admitted only with the mandatory |L| decisiveness ledger (per-item licensed-answer-set size distribution; |L|=1 cells flagged) and a marginal-cost cap of ~$2.
rationale: Brackets the same position from the constrained-decoding side while the ledger guards against reporting the mask's answer as the model's.
notes: MD-5.

claim: The knull-sourced-rules ablation (rules re-derived from plain-dictionary definitions) is pre-registered now and run in phase 2, pending the knull-fork maintainer decision (issue #6); until it runs, any positive RULES-1 result is capped at 'machinery non-inert', never 'kernel-specific value'.
rationale: The rules could in principle be authored without the NSM kernel; the ablation is the only test of kernel-specificity, and the claim cap preserves honesty while it is pending.
notes: MD-7.

claim: The default-world arm (A4: closure pre-materialised offline, runtime lookup-only) is runtime-inert by design; no non-inertness claim attaches to it — its deliverable is the priced train-time-vs-prompt-time cost ledger.
rationale: Prevents claim inflation on the position whose runtime, by construction, cannot exhibit the property under test, while still answering the maintainer's default/temporary pricing question.
notes: MD-3.

claim: Engine termination comes from the safe, function-free (Datalog-safe) rule fragment plus explicit per-item resource budgets (max derived triples, wall-clock) failing closed with ERR_BUDGET_EXCEEDED; proof depth is an evaluation stratification axis only, never an engine semantics term. The closed rule inventory with refusal on unknown kinds is retained. Supersedes ASM-1130's chain-depth-<=2 semantic bound.
rationale: GPT-5.6 correctly identified that WMRE-1 conflated a semantic restriction with a resource control; ontology semantics should not change because a proof needs three rule applications. Blow-up control moves to budgets; evaluated cells still cover proof depth <= 4.
notes: Cross-model fold-in, adjudication HYBRID (mostly adopt GPT-5.6).

claim: Arm A7 (direct executor: engine answers from Cl(S) with proof, LM renders only) is added to RULES-1 and carries the systems claim via Holm-corrected secondary s4 (exact(A7) > exact(c6)); A3 (verify-retry) remains sole carrier of the model-level claim and of the GPU primary (KILL-b unchanged); A2 (injection) is demoted to a descriptive comparison arm. Refines ASM-1128.
rationale: Direct execution is the attribution-clean carrier of the systems claim (GPT-5.6); verify-retry is retained because the redirect's open question includes whether derivations help a host, which a renderer-only design cannot answer. One-primary discipline preserved.
notes: MD-9 recommendation (a).

claim: Every compiled rule and every proof node carries a semantic-regime tag, regime in {owl-rl, horn-def, policy}; complete-domain policy rules (R-COVER-ELIM family) are never reported as ontology entailment; SHACL-like constraints are emitted as a separate validation artifact that never becomes an entailment rule; existential definition heads refuse rather than mint individuals (classification vs entity generation).
rationale: Prevents local closure assumptions (covering, UNA, completeness) from acquiring the authority of standards semantics; makes the epistemically weakest stratum machine-visible and gives certificate and readout a free stratification axis.
notes: Adopted from GPT-5.6 DV-1. The endorsed TBox covering axiom + item-scoped UNA is retained over per-case completeness markers.

claim: The C5 certificate adds two per-target counterfactual gates: (i) removing the endorsing compiled definition makes the entailed target disappear from Cl(S); (ii) a meaning-preserving rule mutation changes exactly the predicted outputs. Optional E4-lite defined-class cells (person/photo/cat, CPU-only, no kill rule) may ride along gated on minting effort. Extends ASM-1131; SUCCESS/KILL-a rules unchanged.
rationale: Upgrades the certificate from 'the engine derives X' to 'the engine derives X because of that endorsed definition' — provenance made mechanical at CPU-only cost.
notes: GPT-5.6's small-N sealed GPU sets were rejected in favour of the 958-item third-party-gold slice (GPT-5.6 concurs when the slice is runnable).

claim: Control c6 (axioms-as-text) is added to RULES-1: stated facts plus the serialized TBox/rules as prose in context, no engine execution — the kernel-as-text null at the axiom layer, token-matched per ASM-1127 conventions. It is the comparator for secondary s4.
backing_ref: poc/gpt56-review/wmre-propose/last-message.json#7 stage 2 (arm A1); estate kernel-as-text control discipline
rationale: Distinguishes 'execution of definitions matters' from 'definition text in context matters'; WMRE-1's control set lacked this mandatory null and GPT-5.6's independent design supplied it.
notes: Adopted from GPT-5.6 DV-2/DV-8.

claim: The g2 Pi over-constraint (33/84 = 0.39 sound; failure concentrated in R3, 5/42) is diagnosed as a REPRESENTATION error, not per-concept authoring error: Pi re-purposes the refKind bookkeeping tag on the explication's own variables as a hard, universally-quantified, mutually-disjoint NECESSARY argument type, forcing NSM's broad SOMETHING prime to exclude persons. The R1 slice (bookmark) is a local authoring fix; the R3 slice (37 of 51 misses) is intrinsic to the 4-disjoint-sort readout; the R4 slice is the pre-registered typicality-vs-necessity gap. This is the classic selectional-restriction-as-hard-constraint error.
backing_ref: poc/g2/pi-project.py:69-70,192-203; poc/g2/build-materials.py:40-45,124-128; poc/g2/result.json sha256 96a23be5; docs/design-constraint-layer.md:89; Wilks 1975 preference semantics
rationale: Names the root cause so the remedy is scoped as 'replace the home-grown 4-sort typing', not 'author more carefully'; distinguishes the fixable authoring slice from the intrinsic representation slice.
notes: Selectional restrictions are preferences not constraints (Wilks 1975) is LIT-BACKED; the g2 rates are MEASURED; the diagnosis synthesising them is the stipulated call.

claim: Adopt a foundational ontology as the layer-1 type/meta ANCHOR and soft-typing + comparator source, NOT as the kernel's authoritative meaning formalism (directive-1 no-semantic-web-legacy preserved: meaning stays native NSM; the import is a downstream-anchored, curated, endorsed input). Primary pick gUFO (CC BY 4.0, which kernel-v0 already targets via gufo:Event/gufo:Kind); BFO (already ingested data/onto-obo, CC BY 4.0, ISO/IEC 21838-2) is the drop-in alternative and the cheapest immediate anchor since it is present and gUFO is BFO-aligned -> BFO-now / gUFO-later, one anchor family.
rationale: Foundational ontologies supply the sort lattice / role-phase distinctions the 4-disjoint-sort readout lacks; anchoring (not adopting-as-meaning) is what keeps directive-1 satisfied; BFO's prior ingestion + kernel-v0's existing gUFO targeting make this the lowest-cost path.
notes: Anchor/comparator posture, not destination; directive-1 bounds it.

claim: Source layer-2 per-concept argument typing from SUMO+WordNet (coverage; both already ingested, SUMO REDISTRIBUTABLE with IEEE attribution) and VerbNet/FrameNet selectional restrictions (the over-constraint fix, being corpus-grounded and defeasible; FrameNet already ingested CC BY 3.0), compiled into the RULES-1 policy/soft regime and NEVER as rdfs:domain/rdfs:range. Foundational ontologies alone cannot supply per-concept argument types (they are domain-empty by design).
backing_ref: data/onto-sumo/manifest.json (license verdict); data/lexical-wn31/; data/onto-framenet/manifest.json; VerbNet selectional restrictions; cross-ref ASM-1162 (RULES-1 regime tag)
rationale: The 0.39 failure is a layer-2 (argument-typing) problem; only SUMO/WordNet/VerbNet/FrameNet touch it, and only their SOFT forms avoid reproducing the hard-disjointness error.
notes: Soft regime routing is the direct designed-for fix; cross-ref RULES-1 ASM-1162.

claim: Auto-generation from foundational + lexical resources is PARTLY VIABLE with a clean split: it replaces the formal type/subsumption/domain-range layer and the inference rules (standard OWL-RL/RDFS, already conformance-tested in sparq), and it does NOT replace (a) the authored NSM explications (mechanical auto-verbalisation is below the scholarly bar and is the f2b-t value-carrying artifact) nor (b) the NSM-grounding thesis (resources are not prime-built). Given DECONF-A1's inert vector runtime and the rules-engine pivot, the formal layer's role is now axiom-source-for-the-engine, which is exactly where auto-import is strongest.
backing_ref: registry/verdicts/f2b-transfer.json; DECONF-A1 verdict (C_dec=1.0); reports/sparq-estate.md §4; OntoWordNet (Gangemi et al.); gUFO 2019
rationale: Separates what import saves (annotation + compute on the unsound formal layer) from what stays ours (transferring prose + grounding), so the recommendation neither over- nor under-claims.
notes: No feasibility conclusion on the theses; this scopes the SOURCE only.

claim: Slot-in to RULES-1 is AUGMENT-not-replace: the foundational-ontology TBox imports as an upper module in the C1 compiler (kernel domain axioms specialise gufo:/bfo: classes), imported domain/range routes to the policy/soft regime, existential heads still refuse entity minting, and every imported axiom is curated/endorsed on the axioms-definitional-v0 precedent (auto-import for recall, endorsement for authority). RULES-1's architecture, worlds, arms, endpoints, and kill rules are UNCHANGED; only one input (some TBox rows gain a gufo:/bfo: parent + regime=policy) changes.
rationale: The OBO/BFO second vertical already proves this pattern in-estate; making the foundational tier the explicit anchor for the kinship vertical too is an input change, not an architecture change, so it inherits RULES-1's validation unchanged.
notes: Cross-refs RULES-1 pending block; no engine change.

claim: Foundational-ontology import LOOSENS the 'grow to 500+' (g2 option B) type-layer blocker but does NOT by itself satisfy the g2 n>=500 Pi-soundness gate: import supplies a large typed backbone (legs 1-2 of kernel_checkable: record + licensing axiom) but never leg 3 (mapper parse into the closed 4-op grammar), and the Pi-soundness count reads AUTHORED explications, so >=500 judgeable Pi items still needs authored growth or a maintainer gate re-size. Import is complementary to option B, not a substitute.
rationale: Prevents the false inference that importing 100k+ typed concepts auto-clears the g2 instrument gate; checkability is not vocabulary coverage.
notes: Ties the import to both live threads honestly.

claim: License posture for any redistributed derived kernel: gUFO / BFO / OEWN clean (CC BY 4.0, attribution); RO / Wikidata clean (CC0); SUMO redistributable with IEEE attribution (estate verdict); FrameNet CC BY 3.0; schema.org / ConceptNet are CC BY-SA copyleft and MUST be isolated in their own share-alike shard or excluded; DOLCE license ambiguous on classic files (verify before use). Per-shard license isolation is estate policy and a redistribution precondition.
rationale: A derived kernel's redistribution is only as clean as its dirtiest incorporated shard; copyleft contamination is a non-starter for a permissive kernel unless shard-isolated.
notes: The already-ingested shards carry REDISTRIBUTABLE verdicts; the copyleft/ambiguous ones need the isolation/verify decision.

claim: Adoption of the import for the TYPE layer is gated on a falsifiable check: re-run the g2-style soundness proxy on the imported + soft-regime typing and require the sound-rate to rise materially above the 0.39 home-grown baseline; if it does not, the import is not worth carrying for typing and the home-grown scheme's demotion is not licensed by this route.
rationale: Makes the whole recommendation empirically decidable rather than argued; reuses the exact instrument that measured the over-constraint so the comparison is like-for-like.
notes: The go/no-go gate for replacing the 4-sort typing.

claim: Directional expectation only (never a premise): importing corpus-grounded SOFT selectional restrictions (schema.org domainIncludes, VerbNet preferences) in place of the hard 4-disjoint-sort universal readout will RAISE the g2-style sound-rate above 0.39, because the imported constraints are built as defeasible expectations rather than necessities and thus stop excluding the persons ordinary usage allows.
backing_ref: resolved by the ASM-1217 g2-style re-run on imported+soft-regime typing
rationale: Names the directional expectation behind the ASM-1217 gate so it is a recorded, falsifiable projection rather than an implicit hope; premises nothing while open.
resolution_path: the ASM-1217 g2-style soundness re-run on imported + soft-regime typing converts this to MEASURED (rise confirmed) or refutes it (no rise).
notes: Load-bearing for nothing; interpretive colour pending the 1217 re-run. [batch-prep fix: rationale supplied; doc emission lacked it]

claim: Expressivity-gap taxonomy: any claim that content is 'not expressible with the semantic-web stack' must name (a) one gap code from {GAP-CASE, GAP-NAF, GAP-UNA, GAP-ARITH, GAP-AGG, GAP-NARY, GAP-FUNC, GAP-HO, GAP-MODAL, GAP-MATH, GAP-CONSTRAINT} and (b) the axis: axis-1 not statable in OWL 2 DL/RDF/N3 at all; axis-2 statable but not derivable in the rule profiles (OWL 2 RL/N3 Horn); axis-3 statable only with wrong (open-world, monotone, non-UNA) semantics. Claims without a gap code and axis do not meet the maintainer's issue-#20 justification bar.
rationale: Operationalises the maintainer's requirement to explicitly justify every step outside the stack; makes the justification machine-checkable rather than prose.
notes: Extends the regime-tag discipline of ASM-1162 with a vocabulary.

claim: Layered expressivity stance: entailment machinery is chosen from the ladder L-A owl-rl -> L-B horn-def (N3/Datalog) -> L-C policy (pre-compiled case eliminations with named ASM premises) -> L-D stable-model (ASP, outside the stack) -> L-E proof-assistant (Lean seam) -> L-F fol-atp (reserved), always at the innermost layer that suffices; a rule sits at layer N only with a recorded gap-code justification excluding every layer below N; cross-regime derivations are visibly composite in proof trees; content no adopted layer covers refuses fail-closed (ERR_RULE_UNIMPLEMENTED), never approximates.
rationale: Keeps the cheap, decidable, tooled, proof-carrying stack as the default while guaranteeing the design is not expressivity-capped, per issue #20.
notes: Regime tags stable-model and proof-assistant are reserved names extending {owl-rl, horn-def, policy} (ASM-1162).

claim: R-COVER-ELIM is classified axis-2/GAP-CASE: the covering case elimination is statable in OWL 2 DL (coveredBy + functional + differentFrom entails the father role assignment under tableau semantics) and its non-derivability is a property of the OWL 2 RL profile (assertion-only rule heads, no disjunction elimination); the standing remedy is the in-stack policy-regime Horn precompilation with named covering/UNA premises (ASM-1120/1121), and adopting a full-DL tableau engine for RULES-1 is rejected (N2EXPTIME machinery, weaker fail-closed/proof-tree discipline, loss of the microsecond certificate loop, no capability gained on the finite case inventory).
rationale: Records the precise axis of the one measured expressivity gap already hit, so it is never misreported as 'OWL cannot express covering'.
notes: The RL incompleteness leg is MEASURED (conformance PR1); the classification and the tableau rejection are the stipulated parts.

claim: NSM explications are axis-1 outside the semantic-web stack (GAP-MODAL + GAP-NARY + GAP-HO): the prime inventory contains propositional-attitude, modal, conditional and temporal operators (THINK/KNOW/WANT/FEEL/SAY, IF, BECAUSE, MAYBE, CAN, WHEN/BEFORE/AFTER) taking propositional arguments, which SROIQ/RDF cannot encode without losing the embedded formula's logical structure; therefore C1 compiles only authored, endorsed extensional constraint records (the explication's shadow) and wholesale explication->OWL translation is out of scope permanently on expressivity grounds, not merely prudence.
backing_ref: docs/next/arch/expressivity-analysis.md#2.3; encoder 65-prime profile-1 lexicon; ASM-1126 (authored compilation stance)
rationale: Upgrades the existing no-automatic-NL->OWL stance from a prudence choice to a recorded expressivity necessity with the justification attached.
notes: The shadow/full-content distinction parallels the gUFO/UFO datum (ASM-1237).

claim: Mathematical content (the Lean corpora: data/math-lean-sample, data/mathlib-1000-sample, data/math-v0, data/math-mm) is axis-1/GAP-MATH+GAP-HO: induction schemas are second-order and arithmetic-with-induction admits no complete decidable axiomatisation (Goedel), so it is outside every OWL profile by mathematical necessity; such content routes only to the L-E proof-assistant regime (the ASM-1196 Lean seam, same authored/endorsed C1 boundary), never to Horn approximation or FOL ATPs; a cheap first Lean artifact, if the seam opens, is a machine-checked soundness certificate for R-COVER-ELIM given its covering/functional/UNA premises.
rationale: Discharges the maintainer's #19/#20 mathematics flag with the specific impossibility justification and names the cheapest useful first Lean deliverable.
notes: Out of RULES-1 scope; sequencing unchanged from ASM-1196.

claim: The designated first formalism OUTSIDE the semantic-web stack is ASP (clingo, stable-model semantics), covering GAP-NAF (closed-world/defaults), GAP-AGG (counting as derivation), GAP-UNA at scale, and non-precompilable GAP-CASE; it is NOT adopted now — adoption is gated on a named experiment triggered by a concrete blocked inference, run at the RULES-1 certificate discipline, with stable-model outputs regime-tagged and never mixed silently into owl-rl closures; full-FOL ATPs (Common Logic/SUO-KIF via E/Vampire) are reserved (L-F) pending the onto-sumo translation bead and are disfavoured for fail-closed use because timeout, refusal and disproof are indistinguishable at the interface.
backing_ref: Yang/Ishay/Lee 2023 arXiv:2307.07696 (LLM->ASP on CLUTRR, existence proof on the exact task family, per ASM-1122 backing); docs/next/arch/expressivity-analysis.md#3,#4.2
rationale: Picks the step-outside formalism with decidable ground reasoning, single-binary tooling fit for the 2-core box, and direct prior art on our task family, while keeping it gated rather than adopted.
notes: Provenance gap disclosed: answer sets are not proof trees; any adoption experiment must include a why()-equivalent (e.g. xclingo-style) leg.

claim: No concept currently in scope — the RULES-1 kinship inventory (subPropertyOf, domain/range, inverse, functional, disjoint, length-2 chains, the covering policy rule), the g2 claim families (R1 subClassOf, R3 domain/range, R4 existential read-outs), and the onto-obo RO second vertical (inverse_of, transitive_over, holds_over_chain) — requires any layer beyond L-A/L-B/L-C (i.e. beyond OWL RL + N3 Horn + policy precompilation); this is an audit-of-kinds projection and does NOT extrapolate to future kernel growth, new verticals (temporal narrative, closed-world counting, onto-sumo), or any content class not enumerated here.
backing_ref: docs/next/arch/expressivity-analysis.md#2,#4.3; poc/rules-1/RESULT.md (kinds actually compiled); data/onto-obo/ RO shard
rationale: States precisely why RULES-1's engine choice is unchanged by the expressivity directive, with the extrapolation boundary explicit.
resolution_path: Re-audit against the gap taxonomy whenever a new axiom kind, vertical, or world class is proposed; the first blocked inference fires the L-D gate experiment (ASM-1235).
notes: RULES-1 arms, endpoints, kill rules, envelope untouched. [batch-prep fix: load_bearing forced false per register convention for EXTRAPOLATION rows; doc emitted true]

claim: gUFO/UFO stance: full UFO's inference-bearing metaproperties (rigidity, anti-rigidity, relational dependence) are quantified-modal (axis-1/GAP-MODAL+GAP-HO — quantification over worlds and over predicates), which is why only the gUFO subset exists in RDF (the maintainer's observation, adopted as a datum); accordingly, if UFO-grade metaproperties are ever wanted here they enter as annotation/validator discipline (non-inference-bearing) unless and until a modal layer is deliberately adopted under the ASM-1230 justification protocol — an RDF rendering of a modal ontology keeps the taxonomy and loses the theorems, and must never be presented as carrying the full theory.
backing_ref: Guizzardi 2005 (UFO in quantified modal logic); gUFO specification (Almeida et al.); maintainer note on issue #20
rationale: Generalises the maintainer's own gUFO observation into a reusable rule about modal foundational ontologies and the stack.
notes: Directly parallels the explication-shadow stance (ASM-1233).

claim: Expressivity is NOT the binding constraint on the g2/Pi line: the R1/R3/R4 claim families are OWL-EL-grade statable, and the proxy-strength failure there is ordinary-meaning soundness of the derived statements (precision bracket 0.29-0.45, PROVISIONAL-ON-LLM-PROXY, human gold adjudicates), which no richer logic repairs; no expressivity upgrade may be cited as a remedy for g2/HS2. The one genuine expressivity boundary on that line — deriving named individuals from existential heads — is kept closed by design (refusal over skolemisation, ASM-1162 rider ii).
rationale: Prevents the expressivity workstream from being spent, or spun, as a fix for a soundness problem.
notes: Ids 1239 unused; block 1230..1239 reserved to this document.

claim: g2-import (ONT-TYPE-G2/1) is the issue-#20 go/no-go BUILD-AND-EXPERIMENT record only: it tests whether an imported SOFT foundational-ontology type layer beats the frozen g2 hard-typing soundness baseline on the same 84 slots; it states no feasibility conclusion, adopts nothing automatically, and final promotion remains a maintainer decision (plan sections 1, 9, 10).
rationale: Separates the experiment authorization from any empirical or adoption conclusion.

claim: The A0 baseline is the FROZEN g2 PROVISIONAL-ON-LLM-PROXY primary readout: 33/84 = 0.392857 sound on the judge-pA construction (poc/g2/labels-proxy.jsonl sha 93a124478b8dba411bfd1a9fd07cbc96e874def8e6ac819202c54c1b121754b3; poc/g2/result.json sha 96a23be5b85f1d20d3182c04af8928c34b66356ca10588ca141ebf34891ab94a); A0 is consumed as this pinned constant with ZERO new generation or adjudication (plan section 7.2), and its own estimation-only caveat (the frozen g2 n>=500 gate unattainable on this corpus) is inherited and restated in every readout.
backing_ref: poc/g2/result.json; poc/g2/labels-proxy.jsonl; docs/next/design/ontology-import-plan.md#72-arms; pins: labels-proxy.jsonl sha256 93a124478b8dba411bfd1a9fd07cbc96e874def8e6ac819202c54c1b121754b3, result.json sha256 96a23be5b85f1d20d3182c04af8928c34b66356ca10588ca141ebf34891ab94a

claim: The unit and denominator are frozen: exactly the 84 g2 item IDs in poc/g2/materials/items.jsonl sha 7a4728840550227703338880a79f61491a3c93e5022f07215631f6caa7077008; every arm emits exactly one scored slot per ID; the harness refuses on any ID drift (ERR_ONTG2_IDS/ERR_ONT_SLOTS); abstention or sparsity can never shrink the denominator.
rationale: Freezes the unit and denominator so abstention or output sparsity can never shrink the primary score's denominator.

claim: The primary gate resolves 'beat 0.39' as the exact integer rule sound_A3 >= 34/84 on the primary judge-pA construction — a maintainer-ratified point-estimate ENGINEERING gate; Wilson 95% intervals and exact paired McNemar (A3-A0, A2-A0) are mandatory co-reports and never verdict-bearing; no statistical-superiority claim is licensed even on PASS.
rationale: Pins the go/no-go resolution rule as a maintainer-ratified engineering point-estimate gate and blocks any statistical-superiority over-read.

claim: The frozen arms are A0 (frozen baseline constant), A1 (BFO-only soft breadth control), A2 (BFO+SUMO soft typing), A3 (BFO+SUMO+FrameNet soft typing, the intended adoption arm); renderings are MONOTONE (A1 <= A2 <= A3: clauses appended only where pinned source evidence exists) and A1 can never authorize adoption regardless of score.
rationale: Fixes the arm ladder and monotone-rendering rule so the comparison isolates the added soft-typing sources.

claim: Gold reuses the maintainer-directive-#11 blind cross-family proxy protocol with FRESH labels and FRESH per-judge-per-arm-per-phase order seeds: stateless GPT-5.6-Sol primary (npx-pinned codex CLI 0.144.1 invocation family, verbatim from the audited poc/g2/run-g2lp.py) and Claude Haiku 4.5 sensitivity judge (vendor-family overlap with the materials' authoring agents DISCLOSED, never sole gold); the SAME frozen g2 rubric bytes; {yes,no,cannot-say}; first valid answer final; kappa is pair stability only; every result is PROVISIONAL-ON-LLM-PROXY and the later two-human adjudicated gold retains sole authority for permanent scientific adoption.
rationale: Reuses the audited blind cross-family proxy protocol with fresh labels/seeds so proxy labels stay comparable to the frozen baseline instrument.

claim: Blinding is binding: judges never see arm identity, source ontologies, derivation rules, vacuity flags, bridge/soft records, the A0 baseline labels, or any statement that items come from a derivation or import system; the g3lp-v3 blinding scan runs on every call surface and any hit ABORTS the experiment (never silently retried).
rationale: Makes blinding a hard abort condition on every call surface rather than a best-effort convention.

claim: Instrument gates (failure => INSTRUMENT-INVALID, never FAIL/PASS/NULL): kappa >= 0.40 per new arm; each judge decisive on >= 90% of the 84 real items per arm (0.90 is the highest RT-4-decidable decisiveness bar at n=84: the Wilson 95% lower bound at the expected ~0.98 decisive rate is 0.936, so a 0.95 bar would be undecidable at the planned n); deranged-sort probe false-satisfaction <= 0.30 per judge per arm over 20 probes per arm; all pins match at every phase boundary; zero blinding hits; judge CLI version banners stable across each block.
rationale: Pre-declares instrument-validity gates so an instrument failure can never be read as an experimental outcome.

claim: The probe instrument is 60 deranged-sort items (20 per arm) built by substituting each slot's anchor with a wrong-sort gloss while KEEPING the soft-hedge phrasing ('normally ...') — measuring precisely whether soft modality induces false satisfaction; expected answer 'no' by construction; probe items never enter the soundness count.
rationale: Defines the deranged-sort probe so soft-hedge false satisfaction is measured, not assumed.

claim: Vacuity is operationalised mechanically: a slot is non-vacuous in an arm iff its BFO anchor is strictly below `entity` OR a source-specific SUMO/FrameNet preference is present in that arm; vacuous slots score 0 regardless of the judge label (scoring rule yes=1 else 0 rides on top); the informativeness guard requires >= 67/84 A3 non-vacuous AND >= 34/42 R3 non-vacuous, else the verdict is FAIL read as NO-GO-VACUOUS even above 0.39.
rationale: Mechanises vacuity so uninformative slots cannot count toward soundness.

claim: Build-time generation report: all 84 slots rendered in all arms; non-vacuous 67/84 (A1), 83/84 (A2), 83/84 (A3) with 42/42 R3 at A3 (guard satisfiable before spend); the five NSM-ontology pattern families cover the table (event-action 27, agent-intention-goal 25, generic-type-vs-kind 16, role-vs-phase 13, quality-vs-disposition 3); zero hard operational axioms emitted; 5 conflicts recorded, not resolved (4 alignment-divergence, 1 underdetermined `kind`).

SOFT-only routing is machine-enforced at build: every kot-soft-type/1 record carries binding:false, regime:policy, authority:preference, effect:rank-only and the five forbidden_effects (assert-type, reject-world, derive-disjointness, mint-entity, close-domain); every kot-ont-bridge/1 record is identity_bearing:false authority:candidate; the build fails closed on any violation or any hard-law marker in a rendering; nothing enters D+/DT+ closure and no concept hash changes.
rationale: Machine-enforces SOFT-only routing so the import cannot silently acquire hard-typing authority.

claim: Source closure: generation reads ONLY the pinned BFO (bfo.jsonl sha c8451264...), SUMO (terms 47d4d8be..., axioms d5ee964f...), FrameNet metadata (frames 08f4387f..., relations af881b75...) and the three frozen alignment-kernel-v0.json files (shas recorded in data/onto-softtype/manifest.json); no WordNet, no gUFO, no network fetch, no embedding retrieval, no model-generated external fact; FrameNet is frame METADATA — no empirical filler-distribution claim is made.
rationale: Closes generation to the pinned source estate so the build is reproducible with no hidden enrichment.

claim: Where a frozen alignment-kernel-v0.json link exists for a concept, the bridge cites it; additions beyond the alignments are monotone evidence with full record-level provenance; divergences are RECORDED in conflicts.jsonl and never resolved by silently narrowing (plan combination rule); conflict or missing evidence yields underdetermined, not a forced type.
rationale: Preserves alignment-kernel provenance and records conflicts instead of silently narrowing.

claim: The 84-slot bridge table (build-softtype.py BRIDGE) is AUTHORED EXPERIMENT-ONLY content derived from the g2 failure MODE, the explication structures, and the pinned source evidence — NOT tuned to per-item baseline labels; the per-item A0 labels enter only the paired McNemar at analysis time; the pre-freeze skeptic pass must attack this claim by inspecting BRIDGE against labels-proxy.jsonl before freeze.
rationale: Declares the bridge table experiment-only authored content and shields it from per-item baseline-label tuning.

claim: Every rendered candidate keeps the tested argument/relationship explicit and makes soft modality lexically explicit ('normally', 'typically', 'usually'); the original g2 rubric bytes are UNCHANGED (prompt-template sha d8724154...) — the judge evaluates the rendered statement as written under ordinary meaning; a statement omitting the tested position scores 0.
rationale: Keeps the judge rubric bytes unchanged so arm scores remain comparable to the frozen g2 instrument.

claim: Fresh-runs pre-commitment: no logged proxy row of any frozen record is consumed as an A1/A2/A3 output (all three arms are new statements never adjudicated anywhere); A0 is a pinned constant per ASM-1361, not a reused run-row; the practice-5 reuse-check is still run and recorded pre-spend with this entry as the recorded decision.
rationale: Pre-commits to fresh runs so no logged proxy row is reused as an arm output.

claim: Resource band: <= 628 stateless proxy calls (3 arms x (84+20) x 2 judges + 4 calibration; hard ceiling enforced in-harness), CPU-only build under one machine-hour, target cash $1-5 with the $10 budget.usd_cap as hard stop, wall-clock ~2-6 h at measured g2lp per-call latency; reaching any cap before complete labels stops WITHOUT partial scoring; the run ledger replaces these estimates.
resolution_path: Replace with the run ledger after adjudication.

claim: Verdict-name mapping is pinned in the record: PASS == GO-BFO-SUMO-FRAMENET (go_combined) or GO-BFO-SUMO + NO-GO-FRAMENET-pending-redesign (go_bfo_sumo_only); FAIL == NO-GO-VACUOUS (informative gate failed) or NO-GO-ONTOLOGY-IMPORT (neither arm reaches 34/84); INSTRUMENT-INVALID == repair and mint a new frozen run; a GO licenses ONLY bounded promotion of the winning non-binding advisory shard — Pi-replacement-vs-coexistence, endorsement quorum, proxy-vs-human sufficiency, confidence-bound gating, any later hard promotion, and all gUFO decisions remain open maintainer decisions (plan section 10).
rationale: Pins the verdict-name mapping before any label exists.

claim: Role separation for g2-import: the experiment-engineering role built the layer, harness, and DRAFT record to freeze-ready (green mock go/nogo/instrument, 2026-07-12); the coordinator registers this ASM block and prereg-freezes after the skeptic pass; the runner role executes the pinned harness only (never edits materials or the bridge table); the analyst grades via verdict-gen; the cross-vendor Codex audit confirms; no identity that authored the bridge table serves as a judge, and no outcome is a feasibility conclusion.
rationale: Records the role separation so design, registration, and execution stay in separate hands.

claim: Feasibility-synthesis-v4 thesis state: CORRECTNESS and EFFICIENCY both remain INCONCLUSIVE-PENDING. f2b-transfer PASS and DECONF-B PASS move the authored aligned-key verifier mechanism to MEASURED-positive at their registered scopes, while DECONF-B GS-A–kernel identity 1.0 and DECONF-A1 C_dec=1.0 license no kernel-specific runtime contribution at the F2 checker seam. No programme feasibility conclusion follows.
rationale: Records the updated cross-experiment state without widening either experiment or converting a mechanism result into a thesis verdict.
notes: Every use carries the covered-slice, self-authored-item, oracle-addressed-store, external-adjudication, and scale riders of the source verdicts.

claim: RULES-1 certificate claim cap: stated-cell C_dec=1.0, entailed-cell C_dec=0.0 and 858/858 CLUTRR correctness with Wilson-LB95 0.9955 constitute a correctness-direction positive for RULES-ENGINE MACHINERY NON-INERTNESS only. They do not establish kernel-specific value, host-model lift, natural-input reach, scale transfer, or efficiency. Kernel-specific attribution remains pending the phase-2 knull rules-source ablation; programme use remains subject to RULES-1 freeze, registered-run, verdict and audit discipline.
rationale: Prevents the first positive entailment certificate from being over-read as a kernel or feasibility result.
notes: The certificate is a real measurement but is tagged MEASURED-CERTIFICATE, PRE-VERDICT in the synthesis.

claim: g2 split reading: the mechanical verdict is INSTRUMENT-INVALID because the full pinned Pi dump yields n_gold=84 below the frozen n>=500 gate, which is unattainable on that corpus. The stable LLM-proxy readout lies decisively below the 0.86-0.94 indeterminate band and is an adverse proxy warning about ordinary-meaning soundness, not a FAIL verdict and not authority to demote Pi. Human adjudication plus an attainable amended gate or genuine corpus growth is required.
rationale: Keeps the strong proxy direction visible without overriding the instrument verdict or human-gold authority.
notes: Proxy labels remain quarantined from future human adjudication.

claim: Architecture pivot: preserve authored native NSM explications as the meaning and warrant layer; replace the home-grown hard four-sort Pi typing route with a curated foundational-ontology type anchor plus corpus-grounded soft argument typing; route imported domain/range and selectional restrictions through RULES-1's policy/soft regime, never blindly as hard rdfs:domain/rdfs:range. Import augments the axiom source and does not replace endorsement, the rules engine, the NSM grounding thesis, natural-input parsing, or the deferred Lean expressivity seam.
rationale: States the NSM–UFO/world-model pivot precisely enough to prevent either Semantic-Web-as-meaning drift or repetition of g2's hard-selectional-restriction error.
notes: Adoption remains gated on a like-for-like g2-style soundness rerun and licence/provenance review.

claim: CASC-0-prime pending discipline: while the frozen factorial M2 isolator is running, no structured-medium advantage, reasoner-shrink, small-matches-large, cascade-efficiency, kernel-dialect-attribution, natural-input, or renderer claim is licensed. f2b verifier-offload and RULES-1 machinery non-inertness are adjacent mechanisms, not substitutes for the CASC-0-prime size-by-medium interaction.
rationale: Preserves deferral discipline while the experiment that directly identifies M2 is in flight.
notes: Resolve by the CASC-0-prime verdict and audit; supersede rather than edit the synthesis if its result changes the thesis ledger.

claim: Direction-only cross-experiment pointer: the accumulated evidence favours investigating authored content plus aligned stores plus explicit worlds plus deterministic proof-carrying inference plus honest incompleteness, with imported soft typing replacing home-grown hard selectional restrictions. This is not an established architecture, is load-bearing for no thesis verdict, and is resolved only by knull authoring/rules-source controls, the RULES-1 host campaign, the imported-typing soundness rerun, CASC-0-prime, and a natural-input gate.
rationale: Makes the emerging architectural direction explicit while preventing a cross-experiment conjunction from becoming evidence.
resolution_path: knull authored-content and rules-source controls; RULES-1 registered host campaign; imported-soft-typing g2-style rerun; CASC-0-prime verdict; natural-input retention and safety gate
notes: Never a premise.

claim: ONT-TYPE-G2/1 is a build-and-experiment plan for a foundational-ontology-backed soft type layer; it states no feasibility conclusion and authorizes no production adoption before its go/no-go gate and maintainer review.
rationale: Separates design authorization from empirical conclusion.

claim: The first ontology-import build is closed to the loaded sources BFO, SUMO Merge+MILO, and FrameNet 1.7 metadata; WordNet and gUFO are not loaded and are excluded from generation, adjudication, and hidden enrichment.
rationale: Makes the experiment reproducible and respects the actual loaded estate.

claim: The anchor sequence is BFO-now and gUFO-later: BFO supplies the immediately loaded continuant/occurrent, object/process, quality/role/disposition scaffold; gUFO requires a separately pinned ingestion and new frozen comparison before replacing or refining BFO projections.
rationale: Uses the available redistributable anchor without pretending gUFO is present.

claim: Authored profile-1 NSM explications remain the sole meaning layer and retain concept identity; ontology imports supply interpretations, candidate classifications, and preferences only and cannot replace definitions or alter concept hashes.
rationale: Preserves the hybrid authority boundary.

claim: kot-ont-bridge/1 records are versioned, provenance-bearing, non-identity-bearing sidecars; allowed relations are exactAlignment, supportedClassification, necessaryConditionFor, compatibleWith, contradicts, and underdetermined, with underdetermined as the default.
rationale: Prevents tentative ontology judgments from silently becoming meaning or identity.

claim: The first bridge library is limited to five pattern families: event/action; agent/intention/goal; generic type versus Kind; role versus phase; and quality versus disposition.
rationale: Bounds the prototype to the reviewed families.

claim: Bridge patterns propose and lint but never autonomously endorse; missing, conflicting, merely lexical, or modally insufficient evidence produces underdetermined with recorded alternatives rather than a forced narrower type.
rationale: Fail-closed abstention prevents richer vocabulary from increasing over-commitment.

claim: Every imported cross-layer type, domain, range, valency, role, disjointness, or existential candidate is non-binding during the trial and must never be emitted as an operational hard law over kernel or world entities.
rationale: Directly prevents recurrence of the g2 hard-selectional-restriction failure.

claim: Ontology import uses the innermost expressivity ladder only: L-A owl-rl for offline source-internal normalization, L-B horn-def for deterministic candidate joins, and L-C policy for advisory preferences; L-D stable-model and richer layers are excluded because no identified import inference requires them.
rationale: Keeps the experiment within justified, already available machinery.

claim: A soft imported record carries regime=policy, authority=preference, binding=false, and effect=rank-only; it may rank, lint, and explain but cannot add closure facts, reject a world or answer, create contradictions, close a domain, or mint an entity.
rationale: Makes non-binding semantics machine-enforceable rather than comment-level.

claim: SUMO domain, range, subclass, and relation declarations remain hard only inside their pinned SUMO source theory; when transferred through a partial kernel-to-SUMO alignment they become cited soft preferences and never rdfs:domain or rdfs:range laws.
rationale: Distinguishes source semantics from uncertain cross-ontology transfer.

claim: The loaded FrameNet input is frame metadata rather than the annotated sentence corpus: Core frame elements support soft valency evidence and FE definitions may propose broad filler categories, but FE role names never automatically become ontological types.
rationale: Prevents overclaiming corpus-grounded filler distributions absent from the loaded shard.

claim: Redistribution eligibility is a build gate: BFO is CC BY 4.0, SUMO is redistributable under the recorded IEEE attribution terms, and FrameNet 1.7 metadata is CC BY 3.0; record-level provenance and shard-level attribution are mandatory.
rationale: Implements the redistributable-sources-only decision.

claim: ONT-TYPE-G2/1 freezes the exact 84 IDs and rule positions in poc/g2/materials/items.jsonl SHA-256 7a4728840550227703338880a79f61491a3c93e5022f07215631f6caa7077008; every arm must emit exactly one scored slot for every ID.
rationale: Makes the comparison like-for-like and prevents denominator drift.

claim: The frozen arms are A0 current Pi baseline, A1 BFO-only soft breadth control, A2 BFO+SUMO soft typing, and A3 BFO+SUMO+FrameNet soft typing; A3 is the intended combined adoption arm and A1 can never independently authorize adoption.
rationale: Separates anchor breadth from the per-concept content sources intended to fix over-constraint.

claim: The primary go threshold is strict improvement over the exact current baseline 33/84=0.392857: the candidate arm must receive at least 34 yes labels out of the fixed 84 under the primary directive-#11 proxy construction; this is a point-estimate engineering gate, with intervals and paired tests reported but non-verdict-bearing.
rationale: Resolves the shorthand 'beat 0.39' into an unambiguous integer rule.

claim: The soundness gate cannot be passed through abstention or vacuity: missing, underdetermined, cannot-say, invalid, top-Entity-only, or position-omitting outputs score zero; the candidate must also supply at least 67/84 non-vacuous outputs including at least 34/42 R3 positions or receive NO-GO-VACUOUS.
rationale: Prevents broad foundational categories or sparse emission from creating a trivial soundness win.

claim: Gold for the first gate uses the maintainer-directive-#11 blind cross-family proxy protocol with fresh labels: stateless GPT-5.6-Sol primary and Claude Haiku 4.5 sensitivity judge, kappa interpreted only as proxy stability, proxy results tagged PROVISIONAL-ON-LLM-PROXY, and later adjudicated two-human gold retaining authority for permanent scientific adoption.
rationale: Reuses the existing bounded instrument without treating proxy labels as human gold.

claim: Expected resource band for ONT-TYPE-G2/1 is 2-5 engineering days, CPU-only generation below one machine-hour, at most 628 proxy calls, target cash 1-5 USD with a hard 10 USD stop, and 4-8 human annotator-hours if the human reconciliation stage is authorized.
rationale: Provides a bounded planning estimate; actual cost is measured and reported by the run.
resolution_path: Replace estimates with the run ledger after candidate generation and adjudication.

claim: A passing experiment licenses only bounded promotion of the winning non-binding preference shard; proxy-versus-human authority, confidence-bound versus point-estimate gating, non-vacuity thresholds, endorsement quorum, policy-schema shape, any later hard promotion, and the gUFO version/migration remain explicit maintainer decisions.
rationale: Keeps irreversible authority and scope changes with the maintainer.

claim: Lift anatomy: the f2b-transfer stage-2 primary +0.25066666666666704 decomposes exactly as 190 help - 2 harm = 188 net corrected item-seeds/750; net lift is 100% def-match (+98) + term-match (+90); both claim types net zero; zero MCQ harm.
notes: DERIVED from pinned run-records; reproduces the frozen effect_size exactly.

claim: Baseline determinism: both alone arms are argmax-deterministic; per-item vectors byte-identical across seeds {0,1,2}; all seed variance is retry-sampling variance.
notes: DERIVED from pinned run-records.

claim: Claim no-bias: R1 AND R3 answer 'no' on 101/101 claim items at attempt 0; at these rungs claim-type items measure a response prior, not content discrimination; claim-false's 0.968 and claim-true's 0.205 are the same constant behavior.
notes: DERIVED from pinned run-records.

claim: Engaged-but-immovable: the verifier rejected all 39 claim-true attempt-0 answers on every seed (39 of the 157 aggregate rejects) yet finals flipped on 4/117 item-seeds; the k=4 retry channel cannot move a ~99% binary prior.
notes: DERIVED from pinned run-records.

claim: Refuted-key asymmetry: all 10 kernel-key-vs-ext-gold disagreement eval items are claim-type (0 of 149 MCQ keys refuted); accept-path enforcement locked 2 items wrong 6/6 item-seeds; reject-path enforcement failed 20/24; net measured harm -2 item-seeds (-0.0027); counterfactual full-enforcement harm ~ -0.029.
notes: DERIVED from pinned run-records; counterfactual figure is a recomputation, not a run.

claim: Residual attribution: of 265 kernel-arm wrong item-seeds, ~97% are host proposal failure under budget k=4 and ~3% kernel-content-attributable.
notes: DERIVED from pinned run-records.

claim: Noninferiority localization: the noninferiority_vs_r3 FALSE is entirely term-match (-0.307); on def-match the R1+verifier arm exceeds R3-alone by +0.387 and repairs 59% of R3's own def-match errors.
notes: DERIVED from pinned run-records.

claim: Forced-miss signature: shuffled-verify recovers 2.7% of the lift; term-match shuffled is identical to alone exactly at every seed; retrieval-miss degrades to model-alone, not below it, via budget exhaustion.
notes: DERIVED from pinned run-records.

claim: Shared-quantile sampler: the retry multinomial generator is seeded (SEED_BASE, seed, attempt) without item - within-seed cross-item draw correlation exists; per-item-independence assumptions of the bootstrap are partially violated; no verdict-risk determination is made here.
notes: Disclosed instrument limitation, flagged to the audit record.

claim: Mechanism law (INTERPRETIVE class): at this scope, verify-retry lift is selection-not-injection - bounded by host tail mass on the key within k; harm from wrong keys bounded by the same steerability; value of the store concentrated in the stop rule's content.
rationale: Interpretive mechanism reading adopted at this scope only, resting on the document's sections 1-6 derived facts; licenses no verdict change and no wider claim.

claim: KOT-HON/1 per-item utility is u_lambda = +1 correct / 0 explicit fail-closed abstention (a MISS: stays in the denominator) / -lambda wrong; a silent empty answer on an answerable item is WRONG, not an abstention; S_lambda = mean u_lambda over ALL in-scope items; abstentions are never removed from the denominator.
rationale: Defines the utility so abstention is priced explicitly and silent empties cannot masquerade as abstentions.

claim: The penalty band is lambda in [2, 5], default lambda = 3; lambda fixes the rational confidence threshold t* = lambda/(1+lambda) (2/3 ... 5/6), making sub-t* guessing strictly EV-negative while bounding single-item influence (1+lambda)/N at programme gold sizes; lambda is NOT set to precision-floor odds pi/(1-pi).
rationale: Fixes the penalty band from the rational-confidence-threshold argument rather than post-hoc fit to any observed number.

claim: lambda is pinned per surface at prereg-freeze, before any number exists - from a declared wrong:correct harm ratio clamped to [2, 5] where the surface declares one, else default 3; changing lambda after a readout is an endpoint version change (ASM-1116-class discipline: registered before any re-scored readout, as-written numbers carried beside in perpetuity); S_2 and S_5 are mandatory sensitivity co-reports.
rationale: Pins lambda at prereg-freeze so scoring can never be tuned to an observed readout.

claim: The S_lambda scalar never travels without its vector: answer rate, precision-on-answered, abstention rate (reasoned vs reasonless split), wrong-rate, raw recall, and the S_2/S_5 pair; CIs by item-level (hierarchical) bootstrap per the ASM-0813 house rules, margin claims by LCB.
rationale: Prevents the scalar from travelling without its diagnostic vector.

claim: Degenerate-abstention guard: abstain-always scores S_lambda = 0 and FAILS; every KOT-HON/1 endpoint carries a pre-registered answer-rate/coverage co-floor or a full risk-coverage curve; S_lambda prices answering honesty only - coverage remains a separate axis with its own endpoints.
rationale: Blocks abstention gaming as a route to a passing score.

claim: Non-retroactivity: KOT-HON/1 amends no frozen floor, endpoint, or verdict; the CODEVERT G1 mechanical verdict on the ASM-1030 floors as written stands; applying KOT-HON/1 to any frozen result is an exploratory co-reading carried beside the as-written numbers; re-pinning any frozen surface onto KOT-HON/1 (the G1 option-(iii) route) is a maintainer + review-gate decision.
rationale: Keeps KOT-HON/1 non-retroactive over frozen floors, endpoints, and verdicts.

claim: The CODEVERT G1 asymmetric co-reading (DERIVED, exploratory, PROVISIONAL-ON-LLM-PROXY, void on human re-annotation): R_q leg (160 gold-answerable) C=123/A=37/W=0 gives S_lambda = 0.7688 lambda-invariant vs 0.3063/0.0750/-0.3875 for a forced-answer system at identical raw R_q; precision leg (474 elements, 32 AnnAssign wrongs) S_3 = 0.7300 vs raw 0.9325; neg-validity leg 6/6 gives 1.0; it licenses no verdict change.
notes: Exploratory co-reading of frozen numbers; PROVISIONAL-ON-LLM-PROXY; void on human re-annotation; never a premise for a verdict change.

claim: Dual-variant rule: S-HON (honesty-first, fail-closed reasoned abstention tuned to the pinned lambda) is the programme's default identity and the subject of all product/honesty claims; S-BENCH (non-penalised forced-answer, same architecture/store where possible) is the ONLY variant entered in AI-index/W1/G4 head-to-heads and external leaderboards and makes no honesty/provenance claim; variant identity is disclosed on every claim surface; numbers are never mixed; each prereg names which variant each endpoint binds.
rationale: Separates the honesty-first identity from the benchmark variant so numbers are never mixed across claim surfaces.

claim: Programme-3 integration: the KOT-AI-INDEX/2 headline stays raw-accuracy-normalised and W1 is fought by S-BENCH (no change to ASM-0810/0811); S_lambda (lambda=3, with S_2/S_5) is co-reported per domain in the index vector for abstention-capable arms as a diagnostic, never the headline; the section-1.5 answer-rate co-floor is the mechanised counter for the threat model's named abstention-gaming channel; the price-of-honesty delta INDEX(S-BENCH) - INDEX(S-HON) is a mandatory published figure wherever both variants run; landing these in the frozen framework goes through P3-D-INDEX's own review path.
rationale: Integrates S_lambda into programme-3 as a diagnostic co-report without changing the frozen headline.

claim: Scope: KOT-HON/1 applies to item-level answer/abstain/wrong surfaces (query answering, verifier-loop outputs, parse-or-abstain front-ends, store-backed QA); it does not apply to legs without an abstention action (coverage censuses, kappa agreement legs, determinism gates), which retain their own endpoints.
rationale: Bounds KOT-HON/1's scope to surfaces that actually have an abstention action.

claim: The NLB-0-A readout's doc-note stipulations ASM-1090...ASM-1095 (generalised fail-closed frame/op-selection law; inventory-A ablation status; l3a co-report licence; l3a op-arity rule; section-7.1 arithmetic instantiation; post-outcome disclosure attachment) are registered as written in docs/next/analysis/nlb-0a.md section 7, retaining their original numbering.
rationale: Adopts the readout's doc-note stipulations into the ledger without renumbering.

claim: NLB.md section 2's a5 dangerous-class mechanism description ('direction-table flip') is superseded by the measured mechanism (same-orientation two-op substitution, contained-in vs where-defined, discriminating information absent from the surface); the next NLB.md revision must carry the corrected text and restate section 3.1's repair law in the ASM-1090 generalised form; until that revision lands, any citation of NLB.md section 2's mechanism line must carry this correction.
rationale: Ensures the superseded mechanism description cannot be cited uncorrected.

claim: Per NLB.md section 7.2 read strictly, the a5 vertical's NLB-0-B GO condition is unsatisfiable while the section-7.1 A-leg proceed condition stands NOT MET (measured 2026-07-11); the a5 vertical is design-blocked pending a design-revision decision on the container-ask two-op ambiguity (annotation-channel vs distinguishable-phrasing protocol, or both - the 10% ambiguity cap of ASM-0942(3) is the binding constraint to check); the l3a vertical is NOT blocked by this; no registered redesign cycle is consumed (ASM-0904(4)/ASM-0944(4)).
rationale: Records the mechanical design block without consuming a registered redesign cycle.

claim: Any a5 NLB-0-B retention read must be decomposed against the container-ask frame-group before interpretation: the crossed-surface mass is information-absent for ANY parser tier, so retention shortfall attributable to it measures the known ambiguity, not Tier-1 recipe headroom; the decomposition is computed from the pilot's per-family counters, never assumed.
rationale: Forces decomposition against the known two-op ambiguity before any retention read.
